1021.name: Mediterranean Mantis Shrimp
1021.desc: This mantis shrimp lives in the Mediterranean and the Atlantic. It digs a burrow in the muddy seabed and uses its mantis-like front legs to snatch small fish and shrimp. In Europe, people enjoy eating it as seafood.
facts: It digs a U-shaped burrow in the muddy seabed.
It snatches prey in a flash with its mantis-like front legs.
It is a popular seafood in the Mediterranean region.

1022.name: Horned Ghost Crab
1022.desc: This crab has horn-like bumps above its eyes. Its sandy colour makes it almost invisible in daylight, which is why it's called a ghost crab. It dashes sideways at high speed and cleans the beach at night.
facts: Sharp, horn-like bumps rise above its eyes.
It is one of the fastest crabs, dashing tens of centimetres per second.
It is a hardworking cleaner that tidies the beach at night.

1023.name: Atlantic Ghost Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · North America · South America

1023.desc: This pale, sand-coloured crab lives along Atlantic beaches. It digs a deep burrow in the sand and comes out at night to search for food. If danger appears, it vanishes into its burrow in the blink of an eye.
facts: Its colour matches the sand so closely that it is almost invisible by day.
It digs a burrow in the sand more than a metre deep.
It signals to others by bubbling sounds and rubbing its legs together.

1024.name: Atlantic Sand Fiddler Crab
1024.desc: This funny crab has males with one huge claw and one tiny claw. It waves its big claw to call a mate and chase off rival males. It eats mud from the flats, filtering out only the tiny bits of food.
facts: In males, one claw grows as large as its entire body.
It waves its big claw like playing a violin to call a mate.
It scoops mud into its mouth, filters out the food, and spits out little round balls of mud.

1025.name: Fiddler Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

1025.nickname: The waving crab of the mudflats
1025.desc: This fiddler crab lives on mudflats across the Indian and Pacific Oceans. Males wave their big claw up and down to call a mate and show off their territory. It survives by filtering tiny food out of the mudflat's soil.
facts: When a male waves its big claw, it looks just like waving hello.
It digs a burrow in the mud and hides inside when the tide comes in.
By turning over the mud, it helps keep the mudflat healthy.

1026.name: Caribbean Hermit Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Atlantic Ocean · North America

1026.desc: This land hermit crab lives in the Caribbean. It carries an empty shell on its back to protect its soft belly. As it grows, it moves into a bigger shell. It is also popular as a pet.
facts: It uses an empty shell as its home, carrying it on its back.
As its body grows, it finds and moves into a bigger shell.
It lives on land rather than in water, but its gills must stay damp for it to breathe.

1027.name: Strawberry Hermit Crab
1027.desc: This pretty land hermit crab is strawberry-red all over with white spots. It lives on island shores across the Indian and Pacific Oceans. It makes its home in an empty shell and comes out at night to look for food.
facts: Its strawberry-red body is dotted with small white spots.
It needs to drink both salt water and fresh water to stay healthy.
As it grows larger, it moves into a much bigger shell.

1028.name: Ecuadorian Hermit Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · North America · South America

1028.desc: This small land hermit crab lives along the Pacific coasts of Central and South America. Being one of the smallest hermit crabs, it is a popular pet. It stays busy and active, cleaning up scraps along the shore.
facts: It is one of the smallest hermit crabs kept as pets.
It is very active and often moves around even during the day.
It eats dead matter along the shore, helping keep the beach clean.

1029.name: White-spotted Hermit Crab
1029.desc: This large marine hermit crab has a red body dotted with white spots. It lives on coral reefs across the Indian and Pacific Oceans. It makes its home in a large shell and comes out at night to search for food.
facts: Its red body with white spots makes it easy to spot.
It is one of the largest hermit crabs in the Indian and Pacific Oceans.
Active at night, it eats dead matter and helps clean the sea.

1030.name: Anemone Hermit Crab
1030.nickname: The hermit that carries an anemone
1030.desc: This clever hermit crab carries a sea anemone on top of its shell. The anemone's stinging tentacles keep enemies away, while the anemone gets to share the crab's leftover food. They are true partners who help each other.
facts: It carries an anemone on its shell like a living shield.
When it moves to a new shell, it carefully carries the anemone along too.
It and the anemone are good partners who help each other survive.

1031.name: Blue-legged Hermit Crab
1031.desc: This tiny, colourful marine hermit crab has blue legs with red joints. It lives on Caribbean coral reefs. It is a popular aquarium cleaner because it happily eats algae.
facts: Its blue legs with red joints make it very colourful.
As an aquarium cleaner, it busily eats up algae.
Being very small, it uses tiny shells as its home.

1032.name: Giant Hermit Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · North America · South America

1032.desc: This very large marine hermit crab lives in the western Atlantic. It makes its home in a big shell, especially favouring queen conch shells. It can even crack open clams with its powerful claws.
facts: It is the largest hermit crab in the Atlantic, growing up to 30 centimetres.
It likes to use large queen conch shells as its home.
It cracks open clam shells with its strong claws to eat what's inside.

1033.name: Red King Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Pacific Ocean · Arctic Ocean · Asia · North America

1033.desc: With its legs spread wide, this is one of the largest crab-like crustaceans of all. It lives in the cold waters of the North Pacific. Its long legs are packed with meat, making it one of people's favourite crabs. Although it's called a crab, it is actually more closely related to hermit crabs.
facts: With its legs spread out, it can measure more than 1.5 metres across.
Though called a crab, it is actually a closer relative of hermit crabs.
Young king crabs huddle together in a ball to avoid danger.

1034.name: Blue King Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Pacific Ocean · Arctic Ocean · Asia · North America

1034.desc: This king crab has a bluish tint to its body. A close relative of the red king crab, it lives in even colder waters further north. Its large, meaty legs make it a prized seafood.
facts: True to its name, its shell has a slight blue tint.
It lives in colder waters further north than the red king crab.
It is found in large numbers only around a few islands in the Bering Sea.

1035.name: Golden King Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia · North America

1035.nickname: The golden king of the deep
1035.desc: This king crab shines gold all over its body. It lives in deeper waters than other king crabs. Though a bit smaller, it is a popular seafood prized for its sweet taste.
facts: Its name comes from its body shining a golden colour.
It lives much deeper in the sea than other king crabs.
Its shell is covered in sharp, pointed spines.

1036.name: Snow Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Arctic Ocean · Asia

1036.desc: This crab from cold seas has long, slender legs. It is called snow crab because its meat is as white as snow. It lives on the seabed of the cold North Pacific and North Atlantic and is a popular seafood.
facts: Living only in cold seawater, it has meat as white as snow.
With its long, thin legs, it belongs to the group of long-legged crabs.
After hatching, the young drift through the water as they grow.

1037.name: Blue Swimmer Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia · Oceania

1037.desc: This crab has paddle-shaped back legs that make it a strong swimmer. Males are blue and females are brown. It lives in the warm waters of the Indian and Pacific Oceans and is a favourite food for people.
facts: Its paddle-shaped back legs let it swim well through the sea.
Males are blue and females are brown, so you can tell them apart by colour.
It hides in the sand by day and comes out to hunt at night.

1038.name: Mud Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Africa · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1038.desc: This large crab lives in warm coastal mudflats and mangrove forests. Its claws are so strong they can crack open shells. It is also well known as a delicious crab enjoyed by people.
facts: Its claws are so powerful they can crush even hard shells.
It moves between mangrove forests and mudflats.
It is a popular edible crab across Asia.

1039.name: Calico Crab
1039.desc: This pretty crab has a shell covered in colourful red spots. It lives buried in the sand. It can be found on the warm sandy seabeds of the Atlantic.
facts: The red spots on its shell are strikingly colourful.
It is skilled at digging into sand to hide its body.
It sometimes carries a sea anemone on its back.

1040.name: Yellowline Arrow Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · North America · South America · Atlantic Ocean

1040.desc: This crab has a small body but very long, thin legs that make it look like a spider. It's called an arrow crab because the front of its head comes to a sharp, arrowhead point. It tiptoes gracefully among the coral reefs.
facts: Its legs are extremely long and thin, just like a spider's.
The front of its head is shaped like a sharp arrowhead.
It becomes more active at night.

1041.name: Umbrella Crab
1041.desc: This curious crab has a wide shell that spreads out like an umbrella, covering its legs. Seen from above, its legs are almost invisible. It lives on the rocky seabeds of the cold North Pacific.
facts: Its wide shell covers its legs like an umbrella.
Seen from above, its legs are barely visible.
Its colour matches the rocks, helping it hide well.

1042.name: Graceful Decorator Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · North America · Asia · Pacific Ocean

1042.desc: This crab disguises itself by attaching seaweed and tiny creatures to its body. When it stays still, it's hard to tell it's even a crab. It lives in the cold waters of the North Pacific.
facts: It disguises itself by attaching seaweed to its body.
When it holds still, it's hard to recognise it as a crab at all.
Its slender, long legs give it a graceful look.

1043.name: Porcelain Crab
1043.desc: This small crab has a smooth, flat body like porcelain. It's so easily startled that it will snap off a leg and flee if frightened. It lives in groups among the rocky crevices near New Zealand.
facts: Its body is as smooth as porcelain.
It uses fan-like mouthparts to filter food from the water.
When startled, it will shed a leg on its own and flee.

1044.name: Common Hermit Crab
1044.desc: This hermit crab carries an empty whelk shell on its back like a house to protect its soft belly. As it grows bigger, it moves into a larger shell. It is very common along European shores.
facts: It carries an empty whelk shell as its home.
As it grows, it moves into a bigger shell.
It sometimes carries a sea anemone on its shell and lives alongside it.

1045.name: Porcelain Anemone Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1045.nickname: The tiny crab in the anemone
1045.desc: This small, pretty crab lives hidden among the tentacles of a sea anemone. Its body has a special way of avoiding the anemone's stinging cells. It uses fan-like mouthparts to filter food from the water.
facts: It lives hidden among the tentacles of a sea anemone.
It is not harmed by the anemone's sting.
It filters food from the water using fan-like mouthparts.

1046.name: Mud Shrimp
1046.nickname: The burrowing shrimp of the mudflats
1046.desc: This shrimp digs a deep burrow in the mud of the flats and lives inside it. Because it spends all day underground digging, it is rarely seen. Its body is soft and see-through.
facts: It digs a deep burrow in the mud and lives inside.
Its body is soft and semi-transparent.
Because it lives underground, it is hard to spot.

1047.name: Porcupine Crab
1047.desc: This large crab has sharp spines covering its body and legs. It lives on the seabed of very deep, cold waters. Thanks to its spines, predators think twice before attacking.
facts: Its whole body is covered in sharp spines.
It lives deep on the ocean floor.
Its spines act as a shield to protect its body.

1048.name: Red Reef Lobster

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Africa · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1048.desc: This small lobster is bright red all over with white spots. By day it hides in a coral reef crevice, coming out at night to search for food. It's also a popular choice for aquariums.
facts: Its whole body is bright red with white spots.
It hides in its burrow by day and is active at night.
It acts as a cleaner on the coral reef.

1049.name: Japanese Fan Lobster
1049.desc: This lobster has a flat, broad body shaped like a fan. Instead of claws, it uses its wide body to bury itself in the sand. It's a tasty lobster caught in the seas near Japan.
facts: Its flat body is shaped like a fan.
It is skilled at burying itself in the sand.
It has no claws, but instead has a broad, flat body.

1050.name: Flathead Lobster

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Africa · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1050.desc: This lobster has a flat, fan-shaped head and body. It glides across the sandy seabed as it moves. It lives in the warm waters of the Indian and Pacific Oceans and is caught for food.
facts: Its head and body are flat and fan-shaped.
It glides smoothly across the sandy seabed.
It is a lobster enjoyed as food across Asia.

1051.name: Giant Pacific Octopus
1051.desc: The octopus is a remarkable animal that lives in the sea. It has eight arms and can squirt ink to escape from predators. It can even change colour to hide. It's truly a clever creature!
facts: The octopus has three hearts.
It can freely change both the colour and texture of its skin.
It can taste things using its suckers.

1052.name: Squid

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1052.desc: The squid is a fascinating creature of the sea. It can change the colour of its body and even glow with light. After mating, a squid's life comes to an end.
facts: A squid's life ends after it mates.
It can change the colour of its body.
It has the ability to glow with light.

1053.name: Horned Turban Snail
1053.desc: The turban snail is a sea creature that looks a bit like a clam. Its shell is thick and hard. It hides during the day and comes out at night to eat seaweed. If you ever eat one, you need to be careful with its shell.
facts: Its shell is made of thick, hard calcium.
It hides in the shade of rocks by day and becomes active at night.
Its salivary glands contain a toxin, so care must be taken.

1054.name: Abalone

info.서식지: Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1054.desc: The abalone is an interesting sea creature. Its shell is shaped like an ear, which is why it is sometimes called an ear-shell. It clings to rocks and lives on seaweed. On Jeju Island, abalone is used to make many delicious dishes.
facts: It is also called an ear-shell.
It clings to rocks and grazes on seaweed.
Jeju Island's abalone is listed in the Ark of Taste.

1055.name: Land Snail
1055.desc: The snail is a slow-moving animal with a small shell. It lives in meadows, eating plants and leaving a trail of slime as it crawls. It has two pairs of tentacles.
facts: It releases slime to reduce friction as it moves.
It has two pairs of tentacles, with eyes at the tips of the upper pair.
It has a highly developed nervous system, which lets it learn and remember.

1056.name: Scallop

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1056.desc: The scallop is a sea clam with a fan-shaped shell. It can dart away quickly by opening and closing its shell. It watches the world around it with many tiny eyes.
facts: By opening and closing its shell to jet water backward, it can move quickly.
It has many tiny eyes along the inner edge of its shell.
Its shell has long been used as a symbol for pilgrims.

1057.name: Common Cuttlefish
1057.desc: The cuttlefish is a remarkable sea animal. It can change the colour of its body, making it a master at hide-and-seek. With eight arms, it easily catches crabs and fish. The cuttlefish is like an amazing magician!
facts: It has an unmatched ability to camouflage, changing its colour and pattern to match its surroundings.
It hunts a wide range of sea creatures, even venomous blue-ringed octopuses and baby pufferfish.
Its bone is rich in calcium and is often given to pet birds.

1058.name: Sea Slug

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1058.desc: The sea slug is a small, snail-like creature that lives in the sea. Its bright, colourful body warns other animals not to eat it. It wanders across the seafloor, feeding on shellfish or seaweed.
facts: Its bright colours warn predators that it may be toxic.
It is a hermaphrodite, but it does not fertilise itself.
Some sea slugs store toxins from their prey inside their own bodies for defence.

1059.name: Chambered Nautilus
1059.desc: The chambered nautilus is an ancient sea creature. Its shell is divided into many chambers, which is why it's sometimes called the 'chambered shell'. It lives in the deep sea and is famous for its striking appearance.
facts: It is often called a living fossil.
It lives in the deep sea, mostly in the Pacific region.
It is a distant relative of squid and octopuses.

1060.name: Blue-ringed Octopus

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean · Oceania

1060.desc: The blue-ringed octopus is small with beautiful patterns. But you should never touch one — its venom can be dangerous. It usually lives alone in the sea, eating small fish and crabs.
facts: Its venom is extremely strong and can be deadly to humans.
When it feels threatened, its skin colour changes suddenly as a warning.
Its body is so flexible that it can squeeze into tiny cracks to hide.

1061.name: Pen Shell
1061.desc: The pen shell is a large clam that lives in the sea. Its shell grows more than 20 centimetres long, and it has a tasty part that people enjoy eating. It lives in the sea, feeding and growing on tiny plankton.
facts: Its shell grows to more than 20 centimetres long.
It has a tasty part called the adductor muscle, prized as food.
It is widely farmed for food.

1062.name: Sea Spider

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean · Arctic Ocean · Southern Ocean

1062.desc: The sea spider is a small sea creature with long legs. It mostly survives by feeding on the body fluids of other sea animals. There are about 1,000 known species of sea spiders around the world.
facts: It walks slowly through the water on its long legs.
It mainly feeds by sucking the body fluids of other sea animals.
About 1,000 species are known worldwide.

1063.name: Bigfin Reef Squid

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1063.desc: This squid has large fins along both sides of its body, making it look a bit like a cuttlefish. It can change its colour and pattern in an instant. It hunts small fish and shrimp in shallow waters.
facts: It is known for the wide fins running along both sides of its body.
It changes its colour and pattern extremely quickly.
Its entire life cycle lasts only about a year.

1064.name: Spear Squid
1064.desc: This squid has a long, pointed body shaped like an arrow. It swims very fast to catch small fish. It tends to gather around lights at night.
facts: Its body is pointed like an arrow.
It gathers around lights at night.
It swims together in groups.

1065.name: Swordtip Squid
1065.desc: This squid has a tail that comes to a sharp, spear-like point. Its body is semi-transparent, so you can faintly see through it. It becomes active at night, hunting small fish.
facts: The tip of its tail is as sharp as a spear.
Its body is semi-transparent.
It is more active at night.

1066.name: White Squid
1066.desc: This small squid has a pale, almost see-through body. Like other squid, it catches food with its arms. When it senses danger, it squirts ink and flees.
facts: Its body looks pale and see-through.
It squirts ink to escape.
It catches food with its arms.

1067.name: Firefly Squid
1067.desc: This tiny squid glows blue from its body. It lives in the deep sea but rises to shallow water to lay its eggs. When countless firefly squid glow together, the whole sea sparkles.
facts: Its body glows with a blue light.
It lives in the deep sea.
In spring, it gathers in glowing swarms.

1068.name: Mussel

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean

1068.desc: This clam has a black shell and clings tightly to rocks with sticky threads. It filters tiny food out of the water to eat. You can see them clustered together on rocks along the shore.
facts: It clings to rocks using sticky threads.
It filters water to catch its food.
It lives clustered together in groups on rocks.

1069.name: Mediterranean Mussel

info.서식지: Ocean · Europe · Asia · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1069.nickname: The traveling mussel of the Mediterranean
1069.desc: This is the dark blue-shelled mussel we often eat. It originally lived only in the Mediterranean but has now spread to seas across the world. It clings to rocks, filtering tiny food from the water.
facts: It is the mussel most commonly eaten by people.
It originally lived in the Mediterranean.
It has since spread to seas around the world.

1070.name: Pen Shell
1070.desc: Its large, triangular shell resembles a fan or a winnowing tray used for grain. It lives half-buried in the mudflat, with its pointed end stuck into the bottom. It draws in water to filter out tiny food.
facts: Its shell is large, triangular, and fan-shaped.
It lives with its pointed end stuck into the seabed.
It filters water to catch its food.

1071.name: Grand Jackknife Clam
1071.desc: This clam has a long shell shaped like a bamboo tube. It digs a vertical burrow in the mudflat and hides inside. When it senses danger, it quickly disappears deep into the sand.
facts: Its shell is long, like a bamboo stem.
It digs a burrow in the mudflat and lives inside.
When in danger, it hides deep in the sand.

1072.name: Chinese Cyclina
1072.desc: This small clam has a round, black shell. It lives buried just under the surface of the mudflat. Its broth has a refreshing taste, so people love to eat it in soup.
facts: Its shell is black and round.
It lives buried in the mudflat's mud.
When cooked in soup, it gives a refreshing taste.

1073.name: Surf Clam
1073.desc: This common mudflat clam has a round, plump shell. It lives buried just below the surface in mudflats where sand and mud mix. It draws in water to filter out tiny food.
facts: Its shell is round and plump.
It is very common on mudflats.
It filters water to catch its food.

1074.name: Common Orient Clam
1074.desc: This clam has a smooth shell with beautiful patterns. It is said to have a hundred different patterns, which is why its Korean name means 'hundred lily'. It lives buried in the sand of the mudflats and is prized for its delicious taste.
facts: No two shells have exactly the same pattern.
Its name comes from the idea that it has a hundred different patterns.
It is a prized clam known for its delicious taste.

1075.name: Jedo Venus Clam
1075.desc: This clam has a shell with bumpy, ridged stripes. Its Korean name means 'meaty clam' because it is packed with plump flesh. It lives buried in the sand and gravel of mudflats, filtering water for food.
facts: Its shell has bumpy, ridged stripes.
It's called 'meaty clam' because its flesh is so plump.
It lives buried in the sand of the mudflats.

1076.name: Softshell Clam
1076.desc: This clam hides deep in the mud of the flats. It stretches out a long siphon to draw in water and filter out food. When startled, it squirts out a jet of water, earning it the nickname 'squirt clam'.
facts: It stretches its long siphon out of the mud to filter water for food.
When startled, it squirts out a jet of water.
Its shell is thin and breaks easily.

1077.name: Geoduck
1077.desc: The geoduck is a giant clam with a very long siphon that stretches out like an elephant's trunk. Its body is so large it can't fit entirely inside its shell. It hides deep in the sand and lives for a remarkably long time.
facts: It is an extremely long-lived clam that can live for over 100 years.
Its long siphon looks just like an elephant's trunk.
It is one of the largest burrowing clams in the world.

1078.name: Button Top Shell
1078.desc: This small snail has a pretty, round, flat shell. It slips smoothly into the sand to hide. Its colourful patterned shell looks just like a button.
facts: Its shell is round and flat, shaped like a button.
It quickly burrows into the sand.
Each shell's colour pattern is different.

1079.name: Triton's Trumpet

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1079.desc: Triton's trumpet is a sea snail famous for its huge shell. Blowing into the shell makes a trumpet-like sound, so people have used it that way since ancient times. It is a guardian of the sea, hunting the fearsome crown-of-thorns starfish.
facts: Blowing into its shell makes a trumpet-like sound.
It preys on the starfish that damage coral reefs.
It is a protected species in Korea.

1080.name: Keyhole Limpet
1080.desc: The keyhole limpet has a cone-shaped shell with a small hole at the top. It uses the hole to release water and waste. It clings tightly to rocks, grazing on algae.
facts: Its shell has a small hole at the top.
It releases water through that hole.
It clings very tightly to rocks.

1081.name: Turban Snail
1081.desc: This snail has a hard lid sealing its shell's opening that looks just like an eyeball. When in danger, it closes the lid and hides. It clings to rocks, grazing on algae.
facts: Its round lid looks just like an eyeball.
When in danger, it snaps its lid shut.
It is commonly found on rocks along the shore.

1082.name: Toothed Top Shell
1082.desc: This small snail has a single tooth-like bump at the opening of its shell. Its bumpy shell is sturdy and tough. It crawls over shoreline rocks, grazing on algae.
facts: There is a tooth-like bump at the opening of its shell.
Its shell is bumpy and sturdy.
It is very common on rocks along the shore.

1083.name: Rock Shell
1083.desc: This rock shell is a hunter that preys on other shellfish. It drills a hole through a hard shell to eat the soft flesh inside. Its own shell is covered in bumpy ridges.
facts: It drills a hole through another shellfish's shell to eat it.
Its shell is covered in bumpy ridges.
It is considered a pest at oyster farms.

1084.name: Knobbed Rock Shell
1084.desc: This snail has a shell covered in bumps like a rash. Like the rock shell, it preys on other shellfish. It is commonly found among rocks along the shore.
facts: Its shell is covered in lumpy bumps.
It drills through other shellfish to eat them.
Its name comes from the bumpy, knobbly pattern on its shell.

1085.name: Top Shell
1085.desc: This snail's shell is pointed and round like a spinning top. The inside of its shell shines like pearl. It lives on shoreline rocks, grazing on algae.
facts: Its shell is pointed like a spinning top.
The inside of its shell shines with a pearly glow.
It is commonly found on rocks along the shore.

1086.name: Chestnut Top Shell
1086.desc: This snail's shell is black and round like a chestnut. The inside of its shell shines silver. It clings to shoreline rocks, grazing on algae.
facts: Its shell is black and round, like a chestnut.
The inside of its shell shines silver.
It is a common sea snail often boiled and eaten.

1087.name: Mud Creeper
1087.desc: This mudflat snail has a long, pointed shell. It crawls slowly across the mud. It acts as a cleaner, feeding on scraps and algae on the mudflat's surface.
facts: Its shell is long and pointed.
It acts as a cleaner of the mudflat.
It crawls across the mudflat in groups.

1088.name: Bladder Moon Snail
1088.desc: This snail has a shell round like a marble. It wraps its wide foot around a clam buried in the sand, drills a round hole through its shell, and eats what's inside. It builds a doughnut-shaped egg case out of sand.
facts: It drills a round hole through a clam's shell to eat it.
It builds a doughnut-shaped egg case out of sand.
It wraps its wide foot around shells larger than its own body.

1089.name: Chinese Mystery Snail
1089.desc: This large snail lives in rice paddies and ponds. Instead of laying eggs, it grows its babies inside its body and gives birth to live young. People have caught and eaten it since ancient times.
facts: Instead of laying eggs, it grows its young inside its body and gives birth to live baby snails.
By eating algae and scraps in the water, it helps keep the water clean.
It protects itself by sealing the opening of its hard shell with a lid.

1090.name: Korean Land Snail
1090.desc: This large snail lives in Korea's forests. It carries a round shell on its back and crawls along slowly. It often comes out on damp, rainy days.
facts: It is one of the larger snails found in Korea.
It prefers damp places to keep its body from drying out.
Its tongue is covered in tiny teeth that it uses to graze on leaves.

1091.name: Asian Land Snail
1091.desc: This land snail lives across Asia. It crawls slowly through damp forests and meadows. When in danger, it quickly pulls its body into its shell.
facts: Its eyes sit at the tips of the tentacles on its head.
It glides along, leaving a trail of slippery slime.
When in danger, it tucks its body safely into its shell.

1092.name: Onchidium Sea Slug
1092.desc: This unusual snail lives on coastal mudflats. Unlike other snails, it has no hard shell — just a soft back. It crawls across the mudflat once the tide goes out.
facts: Although it is a snail, it has no hard shell.
It crawls across the mudflat at low tide, searching for food.
Its soft back can have several tiny eyes.

1093.name: Green Chiton
1093.desc: This creature clings tightly to rocks along the shore. Its back is lined with eight hard plates that make it look like it's wearing armour. When in danger, it can curl its body into a ball.
facts: Its back is lined with eight hard plates.
It clings so tightly to rocks that even waves cannot pull it off.
It scrapes algae off rocks with its tongue to eat.

1094.name: Japanese Chiton
1094.desc: This large chiton clings to rocks along the shore. Its back is lined with eight hard plates, like a suit of armour. It crawls out at night to graze on algae on the rocks.
facts: It is a large chiton commonly found along Korea's shores.
A bumpy border rings the edge of its body.
It hides by day and comes out to feed at night.

1095.name: Shipworm

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean

1095.desc: Although its name says worm, the shipworm is actually a long, slender clam. It burrows into wood underwater and lives inside. In the past, it was a real nuisance for drilling holes into wooden ships.
facts: Despite its name, it is actually a clam, not a worm.
It uses its small shell to gnaw a tunnel through wood.
It once drilled holes into wooden ships, which sailors greatly feared.

1096.name: Half-crenate Ark Shell
1096.desc: This clam lives in the mud of the flats. Its shell is etched with ridges that fan out like spokes. People enjoy boiling it to eat as a side dish.
facts: Its shell is etched with fan-like ridges.
It draws in water and filters out tiny food.
It is commonly found on mudflats in southern Korea.

1097.name: Blood Cockle
1097.desc: The blood cockle is an unusual clam with red blood inside its body. Most clams don't have red blood, but this one is as red as ours. It lives in the mud of the flats and is a favourite food for people.
facts: Red blood flows through its body, just like ours.
It is one of the largest members of the cockle family.
Its shell is etched with many thick ridges.

1098.name: Whelk
1098.desc: This snail lives in the sea. Its shell spirals round and round like a spinning wheel. It is a hunter that preys on other shellfish and small animals.
facts: Its shell spirals round and round like a spinning wheel.
It is a hunter that uses its long snout to prey on other shellfish.
It favours cold water and can be found deep in the sea.

1099.name: Horned Turban
1099.desc: This turban shell has sharp horns growing from its shell. It clings to underwater rocks, grazing on algae. It seals the opening of its shell tightly with a hard lid.
facts: Its shell has several sharp horns growing from it.
It protects itself by sealing the opening with a hard lid.
It grazes on sea algae and kelp.

1100.name: Golden Apple Snail
1100.desc: This very large snail originally came from South America. It lays clusters of bright pink eggs on water plant stems. Farmers sometimes raise it to eat weeds in rice paddies, but it can become a problem when it eats the rice plants too.
facts: It lays clusters of pink eggs on plant stems above the water.
Originally from South America, it was later introduced to Korea.
It can help by eating weeds, but it also eats rice plants, which causes problems.

1101.name: Giant Squid

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1101.desc: The giant squid can grow to over ten metres long. It sees through the pitch-black depths with eyes as big as dinner plates. Scars from battles with sperm whales have even been found on its body.
facts: Its eyes are as big as dinner plates, among the largest of any animal.
It is a favourite prey of sperm whales.
Because it lives so deep in the sea, it is rarely seen alive.

1102.name: Colossal Squid
1102.nickname: The heaviest squid in the world
1102.desc: The colossal squid, weighing close to half a tonne, is the heaviest squid in the world. Its tentacles are lined with rotating hooks that grip prey tightly. It lives in the freezing waters around Antarctica.
facts: It is the heaviest squid in the world.
Its tentacles are lined with rotating hooks.
Its eyes are the largest of any animal, about the size of a football.

1103.name: Humboldt Squid

info.서식지: Ocean · North America · South America · Pacific Ocean

1103.desc: This fierce squid flashes red when it becomes agitated. It hunts in packs of hundreds. It lives in the waters off the west coast of the Americas, where the Humboldt Current flows.
facts: When agitated, its body flashes red.
It hunts in packs of hundreds.
Fishermen sometimes call it the 'red devil'.

1104.name: European Squid
1104.desc: This squid is commonly found in the Mediterranean and the Atlantic. It has a long body and fins shaped like arrowheads. It can even change colour to show its mood.
facts: It can change the colour of its body in an instant.
Its fins are as pointed as arrowheads.
It is a squid commonly eaten across Europe.

1105.name: Northern Shortfin Squid
1105.desc: This squid swims swiftly through the cold waters of the North Atlantic. Its fins are short and its body is streamlined. It travels long distances in large schools.
facts: Its fins are short and its body is sleek and streamlined.
It travels long distances in large schools.
It is caught in large numbers and often appears on dinner tables.

1106.name: Japanese Flying Squid
1106.nickname: The squid that flies over water
1106.desc: This squid shoots a powerful jet of water to briefly launch itself above the waves. It is caught in huge numbers in the seas around Korea and Japan. It gathers around lights at night.
facts: It jets water to briefly leap above the sea's surface.
It is caught in huge numbers in Korea and Japan.
It gathers around lights at night.

1107.name: Firefly Squid
1107.desc: This tiny squid glows blue from small light organs on its body. In spring, huge numbers of firefly squid gather and light up the sea in blue. Toyama Bay in Japan is famous for this sight.
facts: It glows blue from tiny light organs on its body.
In spring, it gathers in huge numbers and lights up the sea.
It is one of the rare animals that can distinguish colours.

1108.name: Vampire Squid

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1108.nickname: The little vampire of the dark
1108.desc: This creature gets its name from the black, cape-like webbing between its arms. It lives deep in the sea where oxygen is scarce. Despite its scary looks, it's actually a gentle animal that only eats dead scraps.
facts: It has black, cape-like webbing between its arms.
It can survive deep in the sea where oxygen is scarce.
Despite its scary looks, it only eats dead scraps.

1109.name: Caribbean Reef Squid

info.서식지: Ocean · North America · South America · Atlantic Ocean

1109.desc: This clever squid changes its colour and pattern in an instant to signal to its companions. It travels in small groups near Caribbean coral reefs. It drifts gracefully through the water.
facts: It changes colour to send signals to its companions.
It travels together in small groups.
It swims as if drifting gracefully through the water.

1110.name: Bigfin Reef Squid

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Africa · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1110.desc: This squid has wide fins running the length of its body, making it look like a cuttlefish. It lives in the warm coral reef waters of the Indian and Pacific Oceans. It changes colour to express its mood.
facts: Wide fins run the entire length of its body.
It changes colour to express its mood.
It grows quickly, lives a short life, and lays many eggs.

1111.name: Common Cuttlefish
1111.desc: This clever animal can change its skin colour and pattern in an instant. Inside its body is a hard structure called a cuttlebone that helps it float. It has smart, W-shaped pupils.
facts: It can change its colour and pattern in an instant.
It has a hard internal structure called a cuttlebone.
Its pupils are shaped like the letter W.

1112.name: Pharaoh Cuttlefish

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Africa · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1112.desc: This large cuttlefish has wave-like patterns that shimmer and ripple across its skin. It lives in the warm waters of the Indian Ocean and western Pacific. It also changes colour to send signals to a mate.
facts: Wave-like patterns shimmer and ripple across its skin.
It changes colour to send signals to a mate.
It is a cuttlefish enjoyed as food across Asia.

1113.name: Flamboyant Cuttlefish
1113.desc: This small cuttlefish flashes brilliant colours of red, yellow and purple. It prefers walking on the seabed to swimming, and its body contains toxins, so it should never be touched.
facts: It is one of the few cuttlefish that is dangerous to eat because of its toxins.
When threatened, it flashes bright warning colours across its body.
It walks along the seabed on its arms more than it swims.

1114.name: Hawaiian Bobtail Squid
1114.desc: This tiny squid is no bigger than a thumbnail. Glowing bacteria living inside its body help it produce light from its underside, erasing its shadow in the moonlight to stay hidden.
facts: It lives in a helpful partnership with glowing bacteria inside its body.
During the day, it buries itself in the sand to hide.
At night, it glows to erase its shadow and stay hidden.

1115.name: Giant Pacific Octopus
1115.desc: This is the largest octopus in the world - with its arms spread out, it can measure several times an adult's height. It is so intelligent that it can unscrew jar lids and solve mazes.
facts: When fully grown, its arms can span up to 5 metres, making it the largest octopus.
It has three hearts and blue blood.
It can change its colour and pattern in an instant to hide.

1116.name: Common Octopus

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1116.desc: This is the octopus most familiar to us. It is remarkably clever, able to use tools and solve problems. When in danger, it squirts ink and escapes.
facts: Each sucker can taste what it touches.
It squirts ink to blind enemies and make its escape.
It is considered one of the most intelligent invertebrates.

1117.name: Mimic Octopus
1117.desc: This amazing octopus can imitate the appearance of other sea animals. It changes its shape to look like a flounder, sea snake or lionfish to fool its enemies.
facts: It can imitate more than ten different animals.
It disguises itself as dangerous animals to scare off enemies.
It was only first discovered by scientists in 1998.

1118.name: Coconut Octopus
1118.desc: This octopus collects coconut shells or seashells and wraps them around its body like armour. It also opens and closes two half-shells to hide inside.
facts: It is one of the few invertebrates known to use tools.
It carries coconut shells and walks on two arms.
It opens and closes shells like a door to hide inside.

1119.name: Dumbo Octopus
1119.desc: It has fins on either side of its head that look like elephant ears, which it flaps to swim gracefully. It lives in the deep sea, far below where any light reaches.
facts: It is one of the deepest-living octopuses known.
It swims by flapping its ear-like fins.
It swallows its prey whole.

1120.name: Blanket Octopus

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1120.desc: This octopus has a huge web of skin between its arms that unfurls like a colourful blanket. Females grow very large, while males are as small as a fingernail.
facts: Females can reach 2 metres while males are only a few centimetres long, an enormous size gap.
When threatened, it spreads its blanket-like webbing to look bigger.
It sometimes carries jellyfish stinging tentacles as a weapon.

1121.name: Greater Argonaut

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1121.desc: This remarkable octopus has females that build a thin, paper-white shell to carry their eggs inside. The shell is not made of bone but crafted using two of her arms.
facts: Only the female builds a paper-thin shell.
It uses trapped air inside the shell to stay afloat.
The male is very small and has no shell.

1122.name: Algae Octopus
1122.desc: This small octopus has bumpy skin that makes it look like seaweed. It sometimes walks briefly out of the water in tide pools left behind when the sea retreats.
facts: It disguises its body as seaweed to avoid enemies.
At low tide, it can walk across exposed rocks for a short while.
It is most active hunting for food at night.

1123.name: Bellybutton Nautilus
1123.desc: This is a true 'living fossil' that has changed hardly at all in hundreds of millions of years. It fills the chambers of its spiral shell with air to float and drift through the water.
facts: Unlike other cephalopods, its shell is on the outside of its body.
It has around 90 arms but no suckers.
It fills and empties the chambers of its shell to float and sink.

1124.name: Longfin Inshore Squid
1124.desc: This squid has long fins running along the sides of its body and swims in large schools. It flashes colour changes across its skin to send signals to others.
facts: Scientists have studied its nerves extensively in research.
It changes colour to send signals to others in its group.
It swims together in large schools.

1125.name: Giant Clam
1125.desc: This is the largest clam in the world - when fully grown, it can weigh more than an adult person. It shares nutrients made by tiny algae living inside its body.
facts: It is the largest and heaviest clam, weighing over 200 kilograms.
It lives in a helpful partnership with algae inside its body.
Once it settles in one spot, it stays there for life.

1126.name: Fluted Giant Clam
1126.desc: This large clam has rows of scale-like, wavy folds along its shell. Like the giant clam, it relies on algae living inside its body for help.
facts: The scale-like ripples on its shell give it its name.
It lives with algae inside its body and gains nutrients from sunlight.
People love it for the brilliant colours of its mantle.

1127.name: Pacific Oyster
1127.desc: This oyster attaches itself firmly to rocks or other shells. It feeds by filtering seawater, and because it is so nutritious, people enjoy eating it.
facts: It filters dozens of litres of water a day, helping keep the sea clean.
It stays fixed in one spot for its entire life.
It is so nutritious it is often called 'milk of the sea'.

1128.name: European Flat Oyster
1128.desc: This round, flat oyster lives in the seas of Europe. People have enjoyed eating it since ancient times, and it filters water to keep the sea clean.
facts: Its round, flat shell is its most distinctive feature.
Even ancient Romans enjoyed eating this oyster.
It filters water as it feeds, helping keep the sea clean.

1129.name: Mediterranean Mussel
1129.nickname: The black shell that clings to rocks
1129.desc: This blue-black mussel spins fine threads to attach itself firmly to rocks. It lives packed tightly together in groups, filtering water to feed.
facts: It spins tough threads called byssus to anchor itself to rocks.
It lives packed closely together in large groups.
It filters water to feed, helping keep the sea clean.

1130.name: Douglas's Freshwater Mussel
1130.desc: This freshwater mussel lives buried in the muddy bottom of rivers and ponds. It has a curious way of growing up - its young attach briefly to a fish's body before growing further.
facts: Its young attach briefly to a fish's fins to grow.
It filters water to feed, helping keep rivers clean.
Bitterling fish lay their eggs inside this mussel's shell.

1131.name: Cuming's Pearl Mussel
1131.desc: This is a leading freshwater mussel used to produce pearls. It coats tiny particles that enter its body in layers of nacre, growing them into beautiful pearls.
facts: It is the mussel most commonly used to farm freshwater pearls.
A single mussel can produce several pearls at once.
It filters water to feed, helping keep rivers clean.

1132.name: Asian Clam
1132.nickname: The little clam that makes good broth
1132.desc: This small, round freshwater clam lives in the sandy mouths of rivers. It is famous for making a refreshing broth and has spread to live in many parts of the world.
facts: It is well known for making a refreshing, savoury broth.
It breeds quickly and has spread to rivers around the world.
It filters water to feed, helping keep rivers clean.

1133.name: Manila Clam
1133.desc: This clam lives in the sand of tidal flats and has a beautiful patterned shell. It is a popular clam to dig up on tidal flat trips and is used in soups and noodle dishes.
facts: Each shell has its own pattern, so no two are quite alike.
It buries itself in the sand when the tide goes out.
It filters water to feed, helping keep tidal flats clean.

1134.name: Common Orient Clam
1134.desc: This large clam has a smooth, glossy shell. It has long been considered a prized clam, valued for the delicious clear broth it makes.
facts: Its smooth, glossy shell gives it the name 'lily clam' in Korean.
It buries itself in the sand to live.
It filters water to feed, helping keep tidal flats clean.

1135.name: Purple Washington Clam
1135.desc: This thick, large clam has a shell lined with purple on the inside. It burrows deep into tidal flats or sandy mud to live.
facts: The inside of its shell has a beautiful purple tint.
It burrows deep into sandy mud to hide.
It is enjoyed grilled or in soups.

1136.name: Keen's Gaper Clam
1136.desc: This large clam has a thick siphon that extends far out of its body. It burrows deep into sandy mud, poking out only its siphon to draw in water and feed.
facts: Its thick, long siphon extends far beyond its shell.
It burrows deep into sandy mud to live.
It extends its siphon to draw in water and feed.

1137.name: Sakhalin Surf Clam
1137.nickname: Big clam of the cold seas
1137.desc: This large, thick-shelled clam lives in cold northern seas. Its firm, reddish flesh makes it a popular ingredient for sushi.
facts: It prefers the cold waters of the far north.
Its firm, reddish flesh makes it a popular sushi ingredient.
It buries itself in the sand to live.

1138.name: Japanese Dosinia
1138.desc: This sea clam has a round, thick, white shell. It buries itself in sandy tidal flats and filters tiny food particles from the seawater.
facts: Its shell is nearly a perfect circle, giving it a name meaning 'rice cake clam' in Korean.
It buries itself in the sand and extends a siphon to breathe.
It is eaten in soups or seasoned dishes.

1139.name: Gould's Razor Shell
1139.nickname: The tube-shaped clam of the mudflats
1139.desc: This clam has a long, tube-shaped shell. It digs deep burrows in tidal flats and vanishes into them in an instant when in danger.
facts: Sprinkling salt near its burrow makes it pop out in surprise.
Its thin, long body lets it burrow into mudflats extremely fast.
It is prized grilled or in soups for its chewy, sweet flavour.

1140.name: Razor Clam
1140.desc: This large razor clam has a long, straight, blade-like shell. It lives in the sandy shores of Europe and uses its foot to burrow extremely quickly.
facts: Its English name 'razor' comes from its straight, blade-like shell.
Its muscular foot can burrow several centimetres into sand in a single second.
It is a popular seafood ingredient in Europe.

1141.name: Great Scallop
1141.desc: This large scallop has a beautiful, ribbed shell shaped like a fan. By snapping its shell open and shut, it jets out water and can actually swim through the sea.
facts: Dozens of tiny blue eyes along the edge of its shell sense light.
It snaps its shell shut quickly to jet away from danger.
It is the very scallop shell that symbolises the Camino de Santiago pilgrimage.

1142.name: Bay Scallop
1142.desc: This small, charming scallop lives in shallow bays and seagrass beds. It has a short life but grows remarkably fast.
facts: Most complete their whole life within two years of hatching.
It grows attached to seagrass beds, so it needs clean water to thrive.
It has several small, blue eyes along the edge of its shell.

1143.name: Farrer's Scallop
1143.desc: This scallop has fine, silky ridges running across the surface of its shell. It is widely farmed in the seas of Korea and China.
facts: Its shell has fan-shaped ridges that give it colourful, varied patterns.
It attaches to rocks or nets using thread-like byssus.
It is farmed extensively in China and Korea.

1144.name: Yesso Scallop
1144.nickname: Great scallop of the cold seas
1144.desc: This very large scallop lives in the cold seas of the north. The scallop meat we often eat comes from this very species.
facts: Its large, sweet adductor muscle is a prized seafood ingredient worldwide.
It prefers cold water and is farmed extensively in northern seas.
It can snap its shell shut to jet through the water for a short swim.

1145.name: Electric Flame Scallop

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1145.desc: This remarkable shell has flashes of light that ripple like lightning along the edge of its shell. It doesn't actually produce light - a silvery tissue reflects light to create the flashing effect.
facts: The lightning-like flashes aren't real light - a reflective tissue bounces light back.
The flashes ripple between its red tentacles, giving it the name 'electric flame'.
It hides among rocks and crevices on coral reefs.

1146.name: Spiny File Clam

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1146.desc: This clam has rough, file-like ridges on its shell and a fringe of red tentacles along its edge. It waves its tentacles while hiding in crevices among the rocks.
facts: Its shell surface is rough like a file, giving it its name.
It extends red tentacles to gather food, and can shed them to escape danger.
It sometimes builds a nest with thread-like byssus and hides inside.

1147.name: Atlantic Thorny Oyster

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · North America · Atlantic Ocean

1147.desc: This oyster is covered in long, sharp spines all over its shell. It attaches firmly to rocks and uses its spines to protect its body.
facts: Seaweed often grows on its long spines, giving it natural camouflage.
It attaches one half of its shell firmly to rock and stays there for life.
Its striking shell made it a popular decoration in the past.

1148.name: Egg Cockle
1148.nickname: The clam with a bird's beak
1148.desc: This plump, round clam has a large foot shaped like a bird's beak tucked inside its shell. That beak-like foot is what gives it the name 'bird clam' in Korean.
facts: Its large living foot resembles a bird's beak, giving it its name.
It can flick its foot to hop across the mudflat.
It is a prized delicacy, especially popular in winter.

1149.name: Common Cockle
1149.desc: This small clam has a heart-shaped shell with fan-like ridges. It is extremely common on European tidal flats, making it an important food source for both birds and people.
facts: It lives in shallow tidal flats, making it a vital food source for wading birds.
It can hop across the sand using its short, strong foot.
Europeans have boiled and eaten this common clam for a long time.

1150.name: Heart Cockle

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1150.desc: Seen from the side, this beautiful clam forms a perfect heart shape. It keeps tiny algae inside its shell, gaining nutrients from sunlight.
facts: Seen from the side, it forms a perfect heart shape, giving it its name.
Its shell has clear, window-like patches that let sunlight through.
It keeps algae inside its body and gains nutrients through photosynthesis, like corals do.

1151.name: Blood Cockle
1151.desc: This tidal flat clam has a shell carved with ridged grooves. Its flesh contains red blood - haemoglobin - earning it the name 'blood clam'.
facts: Unlike most clams, it has red blood (haemoglobin), giving its flesh a reddish colour.
It buries itself in muddy tidal flats to live.
The blood cockles from Beolgyo in southern Korea are especially famous.

1152.name: Broughton's Ribbed Ark
1152.desc: This is the largest of the blood cockle family and is commonly called the 'blood clam'. Its flesh contains red blood, so when eaten raw it has a strikingly red colour.
facts: It is the largest of the blood cockle family, known simply as the great blood clam.
Its red blood gives its flesh a vivid red colour when sliced raw.
It is also widely farmed along Korea's southern coast.

1153.name: Green Ark Shell
1153.nickname: The clam that clings to rocks
1153.desc: This small clam anchors itself firmly to crevices in rocks with thread-like byssus. Its shell has a mossy green tint, giving it the name 'green ark shell'.
facts: It clings tightly to rocks with thread-like byssus, unmoved even by waves.
Its shell is covered in a moss-like green film.
It is commonly found on rocky shores.

1154.name: Noble Pen Shell
1154.desc: This enormous clam has a fan-shaped shell that can grow to half the height of an adult. It lives only in the Mediterranean, but disease has caused its numbers to plummet, leaving it critically endangered.
facts: It is the largest clam in the Mediterranean, growing over 1 metre long.
In the past, people wove a fabric called 'sea silk' from its golden byssus threads.
A parasitic disease spreading since 2016 has caused numbers to crash, leaving it Critically Endangered (CR).

1155.name: Saddle Tree Oyster

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1155.desc: This oyster has a flat, wrinkled shell shaped like a saddle or an ear. Many individuals grow layered together on mangrove roots or rocks.
facts: Its flat, wrinkled shell resembles a saddle.
Many individuals grow clustered together on mangrove roots.
It is a distant relative of pearl oysters.

1156.name: Black-lip Pearl Oyster

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1156.desc: This large oyster produces beautiful black pearls inside its body. The inside of its shell shimmers with rainbow colours, making it the true home of the black pearl.
facts: When a grain of sand or other particle enters its body, it coats it in nacre to form a black pearl.
The inside of its shell shimmers with rainbow colours.
Tahiti's famous black pearls come from this very oyster.

1157.name: Akoya Pearl Oyster
1157.desc: This small pearl oyster produces round, glossy white pearls. Most of the pearls we see in necklaces come from this very oyster.
facts: It was the first oyster used to successfully farm pearls, pioneered in Japan.
It produces the round, glossy 'akoya pearl'.
It is smaller than the black-lip pearl oyster, but its pearls are rounder and whiter.

1158.name: Common Hammer Oyster

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

1158.desc: This unusual oyster has a shell shaped like the letter T or a hammer. It uses the long wings extending from each side to anchor itself upright in the muddy seabed.
facts: Its shell shape closely resembles a blacksmith's hammer.
It anchors itself upright in the mud using its outstretched wing-like sides.
It is a relative of pearl oysters.

1159.name: Red Abalone
1159.desc: This is the largest abalone in the world, with a red shell that shimmers with rainbow colours on the inside. It clings tightly to rocks and feeds by grazing on seaweed.
facts: It is the largest abalone in the world, growing up to 30 centimetres.
The nacre inside its shell shimmers beautifully with rainbow colours.
Overfishing and disease have reduced its numbers, leaving it Vulnerable (VU).

1160.name: Pacific Abalone
1160.desc: This is the abalone most widely farmed and eaten in Korea. It clings to rocks and grows by eating seaweed such as sea mustard and kelp, prized as a healthy, nourishing food.
facts: It is farmed extensively in places like Wando, Korea.
It grows by grazing on sea mustard and kelp.
A row of small holes along the side of its shell allows water to pass through.

1161.name: Paua Abalone
1161.desc: This New Zealand abalone has a shell that shimmers with blue, green and purple on the inside. This beautiful nacre is called 'paua' and is used to make jewellery.
facts: The inside of its shell shimmers blue and green, earning it the name 'paua'.
It is a shell treasured by New Zealand's Maori people.
Its beautiful nacre is popular for rings and necklaces.

1162.name: Queen Conch
1162.desc: This large, beautiful conch has an opening that gleams with a pink glow. It crawls across the sandy seabed of the Caribbean, its eyes on stalks, grazing on seaweed.
facts: The inside of its shell opening shines a beautiful pink.
It extends two long eyestalks and rolls its eyes to look around.
Overharvesting has left it a protected Vulnerable (VU) species.

1163.name: Spider Conch
1163.desc: This sea snail has six long spines extending from the edge of its shell, making it look like a spider. It crawls across tropical coral reef floors, grazing on seaweed.
facts: Finger-like spines extending from its shell earn it the name 'spider conch'.
The long spines help it flip itself upright and fend off predators.
It uses a sickle-shaped claw, its operculum, to push off the sand and hop forward.

1164.name: Tiger Cowrie
1164.desc: This beautiful sea snail has a porcelain-smooth surface covered in black spots. While alive, its mantle wraps around the shell to keep it constantly glossy.
facts: Despite its name, its spots make it look more like a leopard pattern than tiger stripes.
While alive, its mantle covers the shell, keeping it always glossy.
It has long been popular as a decoration or charm in many cultures.

1165.name: Money Cowrie
1165.nickname: The shell that once was money
1165.desc: This cowrie has a small, yellowish shell and was once used as real currency in many countries. It lives among rocks and coral in shallow seas.
facts: It was used as shell money across many regions of Africa and Asia for a long time.
Though small, its shell is extremely hard and glossy.
It is often found gathered in groups under shallow rocks.

1166.name: Geography Cone Snail
1166.desc: This cone snail hides an extremely powerful venom behind its beautiful patterns. It fires a harpoon-like tooth to paralyse fish instantly before eating them.
facts: Its venom is dangerous enough to harm people, earning it the nickname 'cigarette snail'.
It fires a harpoon-like tooth to paralyse fish, then swallows them whole.
Compounds in its venom are even studied for use in pain medicine.

1167.name: Glory of the Sea Cone
1167.desc: This cone snail is named 'glory of the sea' for the beautiful, densely woven net pattern on its shell. It was once considered the most prized shell in the world.
facts: Once so rare that only a handful of specimens existed, it was famous as the world's most expensive shell.
A dense, net-like brown pattern covers the entire shell.
Like other cone snails, it hunts prey with a venomous tooth.

1168.name: Triton's Trumpet
1168.desc: This very large sea snail helps protect coral reefs by eating starfish that damage coral. In the past, people even blew its shell like a trumpet.
facts: It is one of the few natural predators of the crown-of-thorns starfish, which damages coral.
The tip of its large shell can be cut off and blown like a real trumpet.
It is one of the largest sea snails in the world, sometimes growing to 50 centimetres.

1169.name: Veined Rapa Whelk
1169.desc: This large, carnivorous whelk has reddish flesh. It drills or pries open clam shells to eat the meat inside. It has spread far beyond its original home to seas around the world.
facts: It is a well-known invasive species that has spread to many seas via ships.
It pries open or drills into clam shells to eat the meat inside.
Its reddish flesh gives it its Korean name, 'red iron whelk'.

1170.name: Venus Comb Murex
1170.desc: This remarkable sea snail has thin, long spines packed densely together like the teeth of a comb. These spines help it stay steady on the sand and fend off predators.
facts: Its rows of comb-like spines earn it the name 'Venus comb'.
The long spines help stop it sinking into soft ground.
It is a carnivorous snail that drills into other shells to eat the meat inside.

1171.name: Purple Dye Murex
1171.desc: This murex snail was once the source of a precious purple dye. This purple was so costly that only kings and nobles could use it. It lives in the shallow waters of the Mediterranean.
facts: The dye extracted from this snail, called Tyrian purple, was once reserved for royalty alone.
An enormous number of snails were needed to produce just a single drop of dye.
It is largely a scavenger and predator, hunting other shellfish or eating dead animals.

1172.name: Japanese Babylon
1172.desc: This sea snail has a beautiful, colourful brown pattern. It lives on the tidal flats and shallow seas of East Asia, feeding on dead animals and small creatures.
facts: Its brown, mottled pattern earns it the name 'harlequin whelk' in Korean.
It acts as a scavenger of the sea, drawn in by the smell of dead animals.
Some individuals carry toxins, and there have been past cases of food poisoning.

1173.name: Common Tower Shell
1173.desc: This small sea snail has a shell that coils into a long, pointed spire like a tower. It buries itself in mud and filters tiny particles from the seawater.
facts: Its tower-like, tapering shell gives it the name 'tower shell'.
It buries itself in mud and filters tiny food particles from the water.
It sometimes gathers together in dense groups on the seabed.

1174.name: Crown Conch
1174.nickname: The whelk wearing a crown of spines
1174.desc: This sea snail has sharp spines ringing its shell like a crown. It lives in the tidal flats of the south-eastern United States, preying on other clams and oysters.
facts: The crown of spines around its shell gives it its name.
It is a fierce hunter, drilling into oysters and clams to eat the meat inside.
The shape and colour of its spines vary widely from place to place.

1175.name: Common Periwinkle
1175.desc: This small sea snail has a round shell. It grazes on moss and seaweed growing on coastal rocks, and it is extremely common to spot.
facts: When the tide goes out, it seals itself with a trapdoor to survive until the water returns.
It scrapes seaweed off rocks with its tongue to feed.
People in Europe have long enjoyed eating this common snail.

1176.name: Common Limpet
1176.nickname: The hat that clings to rock
1176.desc: This sea snail has a cone-shaped, hat-like shell and clings extremely tightly to rocks. It is famous for always returning to exactly the same spot after going out to feed.
facts: It clings to rock so tightly that it's very hard to pry off by hand.
After going out to feed, it always finds its way back to exactly the same spot.
Its tongue has extremely hard teeth that let it scrape seaweed right off the rock.

1177.name: Rough Keyhole Limpet
1177.desc: This curious sea snail has a small hole at the top of its cone-shaped shell. It uses this hole to expel water and waste from its body. It clings to rocks and feeds on seaweed.
facts: The hole at the top of its shell looks like a keyhole, giving it its name.
It uses this hole to expel water and waste from its body.
It puffs up its mantle to protect itself when a starfish approaches.

1178.name: Toreuma Limpet
1178.nickname: The patterned hat of the rocky shore
1178.desc: This cone-shaped sea snail has a fan-like radiating pattern on its shell. It is commonly found clinging to rocky shores in East Asia, scraping off moss and seaweed to eat.
facts: It is a limpet commonly found on Korea's rocky shores too.
Its shell has fan-like ridges radiating outward.
It clings to rock at low tide to keep from drying out.

1179.name: Green Turban
1179.desc: This turban snail hides a pearly, rainbow-shimmering interior inside its heavy, large shell. Its shell is used to make mother-of-pearl crafts. It lives on coral reefs, feeding on seaweed.
facts: The inside of its shell shimmers like a pearl, prized for mother-of-pearl crafts.
It is one of the largest of all turban snails, with a heavy, sturdy shell.
The round cap that seals its shell opening looks like a cat's eye.

1180.name: Tapestry Turban
1180.nickname: The turban with a cat's-eye lid
1180.desc: This turban snail has a round, smooth shell and is famous for the round green lid that seals its opening, shaped just like a cat's eye. It lives among coral reef rocks, feeding on seaweed.
facts: The round lid sealing its shell resembles a cat's eye, making it popular for jewellery.
The surface of its shell has an ornate pattern, giving it the name 'tapestry turban'.
It is active at night, scraping seaweed off rocks to eat.

1181.name: Sunburst Star Turban
1181.desc: This turban snail has sharp spines radiating from its shell like a star. It lives in the seas around New Zealand, feeding on seaweed attached to rocks.
facts: The star-like spines radiating from its shell give it the name 'sunburst star'.
It is found mainly in the seas around New Zealand.
The spines help it stay anchored on soft seabeds.

1182.name: Bladder Moon Snail
1182.nickname: The clam hunter of the mudflats
1182.desc: This sea snail has a round, smooth shell. It wraps its large foot around a clam, drills a round hole through its shell, and eats the meat inside. It is a common sight on tidal flats.
facts: It drills a round hole into a clam's shell to eat the meat inside.
It shapes sand into a doughnut-shaped 'sand collar' to hold its eggs.
It glides across the sand on its large, inflated foot.

1183.name: Clear Sundial Shell
1183.desc: Seen from above, this flat sea snail shows a spiral pattern as clear as a sundial. It lives on shallow sandy seabeds, feeding on stinging animals like sea anemones.
facts: Seen from above, its spiral pattern is as clear as a sundial, giving it its name.
It fearlessly eats stinging animals such as sea anemones and coral.
Its shell is a low, flat disc shape.

1184.name: Blue Sea Dragon

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1184.nickname: The blue dragon that sails the sea
1184.desc: This nudibranch is no bigger than a fingernail and floats belly-up on the surface of the sea. It eats venomous jellyfish and stores their venom in its own body to use as a weapon.
facts: It holds an air bubble in its stomach and floats belly-up on the sea surface.
It eats venomous Portuguese man o' war and stores their stinging cells at the tips of its body to use later.
Though tiny, it can give a person a painful sting if touched.

1185.name: Spanish Dancer
1185.desc: This nudibranch gets its name from the way it flutters its body like waves as it swims, resembling a flamenco dancer. It is a large, red nudibranch that lives on coral reefs, feeding on sponges.
facts: The way it flutters the edges of its body while swimming resembles a Spanish dance.
It is one of the largest nudibranchs, growing up to 40 centimetres.
It keeps toxins from the sponges it eats to defend itself from predators.

1186.name: Leaf Sheep Sea Slug
1186.desc: This tiny nudibranch has horn-like antennae on its head and is covered in leaf-shaped bumps on its back, making it look like a little sheep. It uses chloroplasts from the seaweed it eats to photosynthesise on its own.
facts: Its horned antennae and leaf-like bumps give it the name 'leaf sheep'.
It stores chloroplasts from the seaweed it eats and uses them to photosynthesise.
It is a tiny sea creature less than 1 centimetre long.

1187.name: Blue Dragon Nudibranch
1187.desc: This nudibranch has a long body lined with rows of blue and purple projections, making it look like a small dragon. It keeps algae inside its body to gain nutrients from sunlight.
facts: It keeps tiny algae inside the projections on its back and gains nutrients from sunlight.
It stores stinging cells from the hydroids it eats and uses them as a weapon.
Its long body and blue projections give it the name 'blue dragon'.

1188.name: Sea Hare
1188.desc: This large sea snail lives in the ocean. It has antennae on its head shaped like rabbit ears, giving it the name 'sea hare'. When startled, it squirts a purple, ink-like liquid to hide itself.
facts: When startled, it squirts purple liquid to confuse its enemies.
It eats seaweed, which turns its body a similar colour.
It has no separate sexes, so any two individuals can lay eggs together.

1189.name: Sea Angel

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar regions · Arctic Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean

1189.desc: This tiny, transparent sea snail flutters two wing-like fins to swim, making it look just like an angel. It lives in the cold waters of the polar seas.
facts: Its body is transparent, so you can see right through it.
It swims using two wing-shaped fins.
It extends hook-like tentacles from its head only when catching prey.

1190.name: Eastern Emerald Elysia

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · North America · Atlantic Ocean

1190.desc: This slug is as green as a leaf. When it eats seaweed, it stores the green particles inside its own body, letting it make energy from sunlight just like a plant. It is a truly remarkable animal.
facts: It stores chloroplasts from the seaweed it eats and uses them to gain nutrients from sunlight.
Its leaf-like shape helps it hide from enemies.
It can survive on sunlight alone without eating for a while.

1191.name: Giant African Land Snail
1191.desc: This land snail is enormous among land-dwelling snails - its shell alone can be as long as an adult's palm. It loves vegetables and fruit, but it has spread so widely that it has become a nuisance in many countries.
facts: It is one of the largest land snails in the world.
It can lay hundreds of eggs at once.
It has spread to many countries and become a pest that damages crops.

1192.name: Garden Snail
1192.desc: This small snail is a common sight in gardens and fields. On rainy days, it carries its brown shell and crawls along slowly. It loves damp leaves and vegetables.
facts: It glides along on a trail of sticky mucus.
The mucus it produces helps keep its body from drying out.
When the weather is dry, it hides inside its shell and sleeps.

1193.name: Roman Snail
1193.nickname: The snail on the dinner table
1193.desc: This large snail lives in Europe and gets its name from the fact that the ancient Romans loved eating it. It is still famous today as the key ingredient in the French dish escargot. It carries a round, brown shell.
facts: It is famous as the main ingredient in the French dish escargot.
When winter comes, it seals itself inside its shell in the soil and hibernates.
The ancient Romans are said to have farmed and eaten this snail.

1194.name: Amber Snail
1194.desc: This snail has a shell that is thin and translucent, glowing a golden-yellow like amber gemstone. It lives on damp grass or leaves near water. Its shell is so thin that you can faintly see through it.
facts: Its shell is a translucent golden-yellow, just like amber gemstone.
It loves damp places near water.
Its shell is so thin it cannot fully hide its body inside.

1195.name: Leopard Slug
1195.desc: This slug is a snail without a shell. This species has leopard-like spots on its body, giving it the name 'leopard slug'. It comes out at night to crawl through damp places in search of food.
facts: It has leopard-like spots covering its body.
With no shell, it curls up its body to hide instead.
It is nocturnal, mainly active at night.

1196.name: Cuban Painted Snail
1196.desc: This snail is one of the most beautiful snails in the world. Its shell has colourful stripes of yellow, red and green. It lives only in the forests of Cuba, making it a very rare and precious animal.
facts: Its shell is as colourful as a rainbow.
It lives only in Cuba, making it a special, one-of-a-kind snail.
Its beautiful shell has led people to collect so many that its numbers have dropped.

1197.name: West Indian Fuzzy Chiton
1197.desc: This sea creature has eight hard shell plates lined up along its back. Around the edge of its shell are sharp, horn-like spines. It clings tightly to coastal rocks, grazing on seaweed.
facts: It has eight shell plates lined up along its back.
It has horn-like spines around the edge of its shell.
When threatened, it clamps down onto the rock so tightly it won't budge.

1198.name: Gumboot Chiton

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar regions · Pacific Ocean · North America · Asia

1198.nickname: The wandering meatloaf of the sea
1198.desc: This is the largest chiton in the world. Unlike other chitons, its shell plates are hidden beneath reddish skin, making it look like a giant slab of meat. It lives along the cold shores of the North Pacific.
facts: It is the largest chiton in the world.
Its shell plates are hidden beneath its reddish skin.
It moves very slowly and lives a long life.

1199.name: Lined Chiton

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · North America · Asia

1199.desc: This small chiton has beautiful blue, pink and orange stripes painted across the shell plates on its back. It clings to rocks along the cold shores of the North Pacific, grazing on red seaweed.
facts: Its shell has colourful, beautiful stripes.
Eating red seaweed gives its body attractive colours.
It clings tightly to rocks, unmoved even by waves.

1200.name: Common Tusk Shell
1200.desc: This shell is long and gently curved, shaped just like an elephant's tusk. Both ends are open. It lives buried in sand on the seafloor, catching small prey.
facts: Its shell is shaped just like an elephant's tusk.
It has an unusual shell that is open at both ends.
It lives buried in sand and catches prey with thin tentacles.

1201.name: Starfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1201.desc: The starfish is a remarkable animal that lives in the sea. It has five arms, making it look just like a star from the ocean. It has the amazing ability to regrow an arm even if one is cut off.
facts: Starfish usually have five arms, though some species can have even more.
A starfish's mouth is on the underside of its body, letting it easily eat food on the seafloor.
Starfish have separate males and females, but it's hard to tell them apart just by looking.

1202.name: Jellyfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean · Arctic Ocean · Southern Ocean

1202.desc: The jellyfish is a mysterious animal that lives in the sea. Its body is mostly made of water and is soft to the touch. It catches food with its tentacles and lays eggs to raise its young.
facts: Jellyfish are made up of 94 to 98 percent water.
Their tentacles contain stinging cells called nematocysts.
Jellyfish can regenerate if part of their body is cut off.

1203.name: Sea Anemone

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1203.desc: The sea anemone is an animal that lives in the sea and looks just like a flower. It catches food with its tentacles, and because it has venom, it should never be touched. Sea anemones live alone in the sea.
facts: Sea anemones use their tentacles to catch food.
A sea anemone's venom is not harmful to most people.
Sea anemones have separate sexes and reproduce sexually.

1204.name: Sea Urchin

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

1204.desc: The sea urchin is a round, spiny animal that lives in the sea. Because it looks like a chestnut burr, it is also called the 'sea chestnut'. Its mouth is on the underside of its body, and it uses its spines and tube feet to move around the seafloor in search of food.
facts: Sea urchins have a roe with a distinctive flavour that is used as food.
Sea urchins move using their spines and straw-like tube feet.
A sea urchin's shell is classified as an internal skeleton.

1205.name: Earthworm
1205.desc: The earthworm is a small friend that lives underground. Its long, soft body lets it burrow through the earth with ease. Earthworms help keep the soil clean and help plants grow well.
facts: Earthworms are hermaphrodites, meaning each individual has both male and female organs.
Their body length can range from 2 millimetres to 3 metres depending on the species.
Earthworms crawl through the soil, breaking down organic matter.

0001.name: Prairie Dog
0001.desc: Prairie dogs are adorable animals that build huge towns underground. They warn each other of danger with barking calls and live together with family and friends. They are great jumpers, too — like tiny superheroes!
facts: Prairie dogs are social animals that live in colonies of 500 or more.
They bark or chatter loudly to warn each other of danger.
They are known to be excellent jumpers.
0001.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Rodentia › Sciuridae › Cynomys

0002.name: Eurasian Otter
0002.desc: The otter is a water-dwelling animal that looks like a weasel. It hunts fish and frogs and digs dens along riverbanks or under rocks. Otters usually hunt alone, but sometimes catch food together as a family.
facts: Otters are wonderfully at home in the water.
They mostly eat fish, frogs and crabs.
They sometimes hunt alone, but often catch food as a family.
0002.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Mustelidae › Lutra

0003.name: Lesser Panda
0003.desc: The red panda looks like a small reddish bear. It mostly eats bamboo and is active at night. It is endangered and needs our protection.
facts: Red pandas are classified in their own family, Ailuridae.
They need to eat a great deal of bamboo.
Red pandas are an endangered species.
0003.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Ailuridae › Ailurus

0004.name: Meerkat
0004.desc: Meerkats are cute animals with long tails. They live in groups on the grasslands of southern Africa. Timon from Disney's The Lion King is a meerkat!
facts: Meerkats dig burrows easily with their strong claws.
They live on the plains of southern Africa.
They are sometimes called 'angels of the sun'.
