0004.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Herpestidae › Suricata

0005.name: Capybara
0005.desc: The capybara is a huge rodent that loves water and swims very well. It mainly eats grasses and fruit and lives in groups with its friends. You can find it in the forests and wetlands of South America.
facts: Capybaras are the largest rodents alive today.
They swim beautifully and can hold their breath underwater for up to five minutes.
They eat their own droppings to absorb nutrients more efficiently.
0005.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Rodentia › Caviidae › Hydrochoerinae › Hydrochoerus

0006.name: Alpaca
0006.desc: The alpaca is a lovable animal from South America. Its soft fleece is used to make warm clothes. Alpacas sometimes spit, but mostly they just graze and live in herds.
facts: About 3.1 kg of fleece can be shorn from an alpaca each year.
Their efficient oxygen transport suits them to high mountains.
Male alpacas are more aggressive than females.
0006.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Ungulata › Camelidae › Vicugna

0007.name: Arctic Fox

info.서식지: Polar regions · Arctic Ocean · North America · Europe · Asia

0007.desc: The arctic fox is a small fox that lives in the Arctic. Its coat turns brown in summer and white or bluish in winter. It hunts small animals such as lemmings and sometimes travels enormous distances.
facts: Arctic foxes can survive temperatures as low as -80°C.
Coat colour is inherited and varies by region.
They travel long distances to find new territory.
0007.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Canidae › Vulpes

0008.name: Golden Hamster
0008.desc: The hamster is a cute little animal that can store food in its cheek pouches. It is mostly active at night and eats seeds and insects. Hamsters come in many colors and sizes.
facts: Hamsters carry food by storing it in their cheek pouches.
They are nocturnal and mostly active at night.
The golden hamster was first discovered in Syria.
0008.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Rodentia › Cricetidae › Cricetinae › Mesocricetus

0009.name: Fennec Fox
0009.desc: The fennec fox is a very small fox that lives in the desert. Its big ears help it stay cool. It searches for food at night and rests during the day.
facts: The fennec is the smallest of all canids.
Its large ears help it regulate body temperature.
Furred foot pads are perfectly suited to sandy desert.
0009.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Canidae › Vulpes

0010.name: Zebra
0010.desc: Zebras live on the African savanna. Their black and white stripes make them easy to recognize. They roam in herds and graze on grass, and kick with their hind legs when a lion appears.
facts: Every zebra's stripe pattern is unique.
Zebras defend themselves from predators like lions with their hind legs.
Zebras have never been domesticated.
0010.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Perissodactyla › Equidae › Hippotigris

0011.name: Hippopotamus
0011.desc: The hippopotamus is a huge animal that lives in rivers and lakes. It spends most of its time in the water and eats plants, though it occasionally eats meat too. Its mouth is so wide that it looks scary when it opens — but hippos also have plenty of fun together in the water.
facts: Hippos are the third-largest land mammals on Earth.
Their mouths are enormous, with powerful canine teeth.
They live in groups in the water and feed on land mostly at night.
0011.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Hippopotamidae › Hippopotamus

0012.name: Rhinoceros
0012.desc: The rhinoceros is a very large animal with a big horn on its nose. It eats grass and leaves and lives in the forests of Africa and Asia. The horn keeps growing for life, and rhinos are excellent swimmers.
facts: A rhino's horn keeps growing throughout its life.
Rhinos are strong swimmers and dive well.
White rhinos live in groups.

0013.name: Lion
0013.desc: The lion looks like a giant cat. It lives mainly in Africa and India and stays in groups called prides. Lionesses do the hunting while the males guard the pride.
facts: The lion is known as the king of the beasts.
Lionesses do most of the hunting.
Lions live together in prides.
0013.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Felidae › Panthera

0014.name: Tiger
0014.desc: The tiger is a very large member of the cat family. It has orange fur with black stripes and hunts alone in the forest.
facts: Tigers play an important role in Korean folklore.
They hunt mainly by ambush.
Several distinct tiger subspecies exist.
0014.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Felidae › Panthera

0015.name: Elephant
0015.desc: The elephant is an enormous animal. It drinks water and pulls down leaves with its long trunk. Elephants travel and live together in family herds.
facts: Elephants eat up to 300 kg of food a day.
An elephant's trunk is sensitive enough to pick up small objects.
Their hearing is so sharp they can catch sounds 3 km away.

0016.name: Giraffe
0016.desc: The giraffe's neck is so long that it can easily reach leaves high up in the trees. It lives mostly in savannas and grasslands and has an enormous body. Every giraffe's coat pattern is different.
facts: Giraffes are the tallest land animals on Earth.
A giraffe's neck is made of seven long bones.
Both males and females have horns, called ossicones.
0016.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Giraffidae › Giraffa

0017.name: Cheetah
0017.desc: The cheetah can run incredibly fast — as fast as a car. Its spotted coat makes it easy to recognize, and it lives mainly on the African grasslands.
facts: Cheetahs are the fastest animals on land.
Their claws cannot be fully retracted.
Among the cat family, cheetahs are especially good at purring.
0017.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Felidae › Acinonyx

0018.name: Leopard
0018.desc: The leopard is a big cat covered in black rosettes. It prefers to live alone and hunts mostly at night. It climbs so well that it often hauls its prey up into a tree.
facts: Leopards climb well and carry prey up into trees to eat.
They adapt easily and live in a wide range of habitats.
Their dark markings form distinctive rosettes.
0018.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Felidae › Panthera

0019.name: Gray Wolf
0019.desc: The wolf belongs to the dog family and lives in the forests and fields of North America, Eurasia and North Africa. It travels in packs and mainly hunts large animals. Wolves look like dogs but have bigger bodies and longer legs.
facts: Wolves are the ancestors of domestic dogs.
They communicate with each other in remarkably complex ways.
As apex predators, adult wolves have few natural enemies.
0019.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Canidae › Canis

0020.name: Red Fox

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · North America · South America · Europe · Asia · Africa

0020.desc: The fox is a small member of the dog family. Its narrow snout and furry ears give it a charming look. Foxes eat all kinds of food and have a habit of burying leftovers.
facts: Foxes are omnivores and eat a wide variety of food.
They often bury food to save it for later.
Many cultures treat the fox as a symbol of cunning.
0020.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Canidae › Vulpes

0021.name: Polar Bear

info.서식지: Polar regions · Arctic Ocean · North America · Asia · Europe

0021.desc: The polar bear is a large white bear that lives in the Arctic. Its fur is actually see-through and only looks white because of the light. Polar bears mainly hunt seals and swim very well, but global warming is shrinking the place they call home.
facts: Polar bear hairs are actually transparent — they look white because of how they reflect light.
Polar bears are endangered by global warming.
Having rarely met people, they may regard humans as prey.
0021.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Ursidae › Ursus

0022.name: Giant Panda
0022.desc: The panda is an adorable bear with black and white fur. It mostly eats bamboo and lives in the mountains of China. Pandas like being alone and do not hibernate.
facts: Pandas eat mostly bamboo, yet their digestive system is still much like a carnivore's.
The panda is the well-known symbol of the World Wide Fund for Nature.
It is one of the animals most associated with China around the world.
0022.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Ursidae › Ailuropoda

0023.name: Koala
0023.desc: The koala lives in Australia and spends most of its life up in the trees. It eats eucalyptus leaves and sleeps for most of the day. Its cute looks have made it a favorite around the world.
facts: Koalas sleep for up to 20 hours a day.
They live on eucalyptus leaves, which are toxic to most other mammals.
Koalas are not very social and mostly live alone.
0023.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Marsupialia › Phascolarctidae › Phascolarctos

0024.name: Kangaroo
0024.desc: The kangaroo is a large animal that lives in Australia. It mainly eats grass and bounds along on its hind legs. Its tail keeps it balanced, and it can leap a very long way!
facts: Kangaroos can bound along at 56 km/h.
They hop on their hind legs, moving both together.
The kangaroo is a symbol of Australia.

0025.name: Gorilla
0025.desc: Gorillas are great apes that live deep in the forest. They stay together as families and eat mostly plants, with the occasional insect. Gorillas have a lot in common with people — some have even learned simple sign language.
facts: Gorillas share 97–98% of their DNA with humans.
Some gorillas have learned simple sign language to communicate with people.
Gorillas are afraid of leopards.
0025.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Primates › Hominidae › Gorilla

0026.name: Chimpanzee
0026.desc: Chimpanzees are clever apes that live in Africa. They spend their days in the trees eating fruit, and they use tools to get food. They are the animals most like us, and they chatter to their friends with all kinds of interesting sounds.
facts: Chimpanzees are famous for using tools.
They live in groups with a strict social hierarchy.
Genetically, chimpanzees are our closest animal relatives.
0026.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Primates › Hominidae › Pan

0027.name: Orangutan
0027.desc: Orangutans are large apes with long arms and reddish fur. They spend most of their lives up in the trees eating fruit. Their name means 'person of the forest'.
facts: Orangutans spend most of their time in the trees.
Their arms are so long they reach their ankles when standing.
They are endangered and urgently need protection.
0027.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Primates › Hominidae › Pongo

0028.name: Sloth
0028.desc: Sloths are the slowest animals in the world. They hang from branches all day, and green algae even grows in their fur. A sloth nibbles leaves slowly, and takes a very long time to digest them.
facts: Sloths hang upside down, so their fur grows in the opposite direction to other mammals'.
Algae and bacteria grow in their fur, which helps camouflage them.
Their metabolism is so slow that digesting a meal can take over a month.

0029.name: Anteater

info.서식지: Rainforest · Grassland · South America · North America

0029.desc: Anteaters have a long snout and an even longer tongue. They eat ants and termites — up to 30,000 in a single day. Anteaters like to live alone, and babies ride along on their mother's back.
facts: A giant anteater can flick its tongue more than 150 times a minute.
Having no teeth, it swallows grit to help grind up its food.
Its sense of smell is about 40 times sharper than ours.
0029.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Pilosa › Myrmecophagidae › Myrmecophaga

0030.name: Armadillo

info.서식지: Grassland · Desert · North America · South America

0030.desc: Armadillos wear bony plates that shield them from danger. They dig burrows to live in and get by alone or in small groups. They eat insects and small animals, sniffing them out with a superb sense of smell.
facts: Armadillos can roll into a ball to protect themselves from predators.
They dig their burrows with powerful claws.
Some species curl up so tightly a person cannot pry them open.
0030.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Pilosa › Cingulata › Dasypodidae

0031.name: Sea Otter
0031.desc: Sea otters are charming animals that live in the ocean. They crack shellfish open with stones, and their fur is wonderfully thick and soft. Sea otters spend almost all their time at sea and are skilful divers.
facts: Sea otters use stones to crack open shellfish.
Their fur is so thick and dense it keeps them warm in cold water.
They spend most of their lives in the water.
0031.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Carnivora › Mustelidae › Enhydra

0032.name: Beaver

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · North America · Europe · Asia

0032.desc: Beavers are endearing animals that gnaw down trees to build dams. They live along streams and wetlands and come out at night. They stay together as families and eat bark and leaves.
facts: Beavers gnaw down trees to build dams and reshape streams.
Webbed hind feet make them excellent swimmers.
They slap the water with their tails to warn of danger.
0032.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Rodentia › Castoridae › Castor

0033.name: Amur Hedgehog
0033.desc: Hedgehogs are small animals covered in spines. They come out at night to eat insects and fruit. When danger comes, a hedgehog rolls into a ball so its spines protect it.
facts: Hedgehogs roll into a ball so their spines shield them.
They hibernate through the winter.
They eat all sorts of insects and small animals.
0033.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Eulipotyphla › Erinaceidae › Erinaceus

0034.name: Mole
0034.desc: Moles are small animals that live beneath the ground. They tunnel through the soil eating grubs and earthworms. They look sweet, but they have to watch out for predators such as owls.
facts: Moles have a very keen sense of smell and hearing.
You can spot a mole's home by the mound of soil above it.
Owls are one of the mole's main predators.
0034.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Eulipotyphla › Talpidae › Talpa

0035.name: Bat

info.서식지: Caves · Near cities · Africa · Asia · Europe · North America · South America · Oceania

0035.desc: Bats are the only mammals that can truly fly. They come out at night and find food using sound. By day they rest hanging in caves or trees.
facts: Bats are the only mammals capable of true flight.
They use echolocation to find food and avoid obstacles.
Bats are known to be natural hosts for several diseases.

0036.name: Siberian Chipmunk
0036.desc: Chipmunks are small animals with stripes down their backs. In winter they store food in underground burrows and sleep. A chipmunk can carry food in its cheek pouches.
facts: Chipmunks carry food in their cheek pouches.
The Siberian chipmunk is considered an invasive species in Europe.
Chipmunks can act as intermediate hosts for Lyme disease.
0036.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Rodentia › Sciuridae › Tamias

0037.name: Japanese Giant Flying Squirrel
0037.desc: Flying squirrels are special squirrels that sail between the trees. They live in Japan's forests and eat fruit and seeds, raising their young in autumn. They glide so well that moving from tree to tree is easy.
facts: A membrane between wrist and ankle lets them glide from tree to tree.
They are native to Japan, living on Honshu, Shikoku and Kyushu.
They face no particular threats and are fairly common.
0037.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Rodentia › Sciuridae › Petaurista

0038.name: Rabbit

info.서식지: Europe · Africa · Asia · North America · Oceania

0038.desc: Rabbits are lovable animals with long ears and big eyes. They mostly eat grass and herbs, and they can be active at night too. Rabbits have many babies and can run very fast.
facts: Rabbits have long ears they can swivel to listen.
They are nocturnal and stay active at night.
They are herbivores, though a few will eat carrion.

0039.name: Deer
0039.desc: Deer are animals with long, slender legs that live in forests and grasslands. They usually move in herds, eating grass and bark. A deer's antlers fall off and grow back every year.
facts: A deer's antlers drop off and regrow every year.
Deer have outstanding hearing and sense of smell.
They are most active around dawn and dusk.

0040.name: Reindeer

info.서식지: Polar regions · Arctic Ocean · Asia · Europe · North America

0040.desc: Reindeer are deer that live in the cold Arctic. What makes them special is that females grow antlers too. Many reindeer travel in large herds, moving long distances to find food.
facts: Reindeer are the only deer species in which females also grow antlers.
Their bodies are well adapted to bitterly cold climates.
Thousands of reindeer migrate together in vast herds.
0040.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Cervidae › Rangifer

0041.name: Moose
0041.desc: The moose is the largest deer of all. Males carry huge antlers, which drop off each winter. Moose can hold their breath underwater and are happy grazing on water plants.
facts: The moose is the largest deer species alive today.
Males shed their antlers each winter and grow new ones in spring.
Broad hooves make moose strong swimmers.
0041.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Cervidae › Alces

0042.name: Camel
0042.desc: Camels are remarkable animals built for desert life. The humps on their backs store water and nourishment, so they manage well where water is scarce. Camels eat leaves and plants like cactus, and their wide feet carry them easily over sand.
facts: Their humps store water and nourishment.
Broad footpads make walking on sand easy.
They can eat all kinds of desert plants, thorns included.
0042.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Camelidae › Camelus

0043.name: Llama
0043.desc: Llamas look like camels but have no humps. In South America they are used to carry loads. A llama can also spit to defend itself.
facts: When a llama is tired it lies down and refuses to move.
Llamas spit to fend off anything that threatens them.
They can carry loads even at high altitude.
0043.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Camelidae › Lama

0044.name: Domestic Goat

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Europe · Africa

0044.desc: Goats are endearing animals that eat grass and leaves. They have horns and like to live in groups. You will often find them on farms and pastures.
facts: Goats are remarkable jumpers and can leap straight up.
They have lived alongside people for around 9,000 years.
Goats have horizontal, slot-shaped pupils.
0044.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Bovidae › Capra

0045.name: Domestic Sheep
0045.desc: Sheep are animals with thick, fluffy wool. That wool is used to make the clothes we wear. Sheep live on grass.
facts: Sheep are famous for their thick fleece, an important material for the textile industry.
They have keen hearing and smell, and are sensitive to noise.
Their horizontal, slot-shaped pupils give them a wide field of view.
0045.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Bovidae › Ovis

0046.name: Domestic Pig
0046.desc: Pigs are animals that provide people with important food. They are clever, with intelligence comparable to a three-year-old child. They love wallowing in mud to keep cool.
facts: Pigs are an important food source for people.
They are highly intelligent — roughly on a par with a three-year-old child.
They wallow in mud to cool their bodies down.
0046.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Suidae › Sus

0047.name: Cattle
0047.desc: Cattle are grazing animals you will often see on farms. Their babies are called calves. Cattle grow to a great size, and their call sounds like a long 'moo'.
facts: Cattle were domesticated around 7000 BCE.
There are roughly 1.4 billion cattle on Earth.
Many cultures regard cattle as sacred animals.
0047.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Artiodactyla › Bovidae › Bos

0048.name: Horse
0048.desc: Horses are animals that people ride. They live on grasslands eating grass, and they can run very fast. Horses work alongside people and take part in races.
facts: Horses have shared a long history with people and were used for transport.
They live in herds with a complex social structure.
Their coats come in a wide range of colours and patterns.
0048.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Perissodactyla › Equidae › Equus

0049.name: Donkey
0049.desc: Donkeys are animals that look much like horses. They have long ears and a tufted tail. They eat grass and vegetables, and they are strong enough to be a great help carrying loads.
facts: The donkey is the symbol of the Democratic Party in the United States.
Breeding a male donkey with a mare produces a mule.
They cope well with coarse food and dry conditions.
0049.taxonomy: Animalia › Chordata › Mammalia › Perissodactyla › Equidae › Equus

0050.name: Humpback Whale

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean · Southern Ocean · Arctic Ocean

0050.desc: Humpback whales are enormous whales. They grow from 11 to 17 metres long and can weigh 40 tonnes. Humpbacks are famous for their singing, and they travel far across the open ocean.
facts: Male humpback whales sing long, complex songs.
They migrate up to 16,000 km every year.
They are a great favourite with whale watchers.

0051.name: Orca

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean · Arctic Ocean · Southern Ocean

0051.desc: Orcas are among the largest whales, and they hunt fish and sea lions. They live in family pods and talk to one another with special calls. You can find orcas in oceans all around the world.
facts: Orcas are apex predators that hunt a wide range of prey.
They are famous for their complex social structure and distinctive songs.
They live in every ocean, from the poles to the tropics.

0052.name: Beluga Whale
0052.desc: Belugas are white whales that live near the Arctic. They travel in groups and can make wonderfully high-pitched sounds. Young belugas are grey and turn white as they grow up.
facts: Belugas are nicknamed 'canaries of the sea' for their high-pitched calls.
A thick layer of blubber keeps them warm.
Their neck bones let them turn their heads freely.

0053.name: Korean Sea Lion
0053.desc: Sea lions were large animals that lived in the sea and ate squid and fish. They once gathered in great numbers around Dokdo, but they are now extinct. They lived in groups and were hunted heavily for their hide and oil.
facts: The Korean sea lion was declared extinct in 1994.
Its natural predators were sharks and orcas.
Dokdo was once a major breeding ground for these sea lions.

0054.name: Spotted Seal
0054.desc: Spotted seals live in the sea and have grey bodies covered in patterned spots. They live in the cold waters of the North Pacific and mostly eat fish and other sea creatures. Newborn pups are covered in soft white fur.
facts: Spotted seals give birth and raise their pups on drifting ice.
Adults are silver-grey with oval spots.
Pups are born with white fur that works as camouflage.

0055.name: Walrus
0055.desc: Walruses are huge animals that live in the cold Arctic seas. They are named for the long tusks that remind people of an elephant's. They gather in big groups with their companions and feed on shellfish.
facts: A male walrus's ivory tusks can reach a metre long.
Among pinnipeds, only elephant seals are bigger.
They use their tusks to haul themselves up onto the ice.

0056.name: Raccoon Dog
0056.desc: Raccoon dogs are about the size of a cat and mostly live on grassland. They are omnivores that eat all sorts of food, and they are the only members of the dog family that sleep through winter. They look like raccoons, but they are a different animal.
facts: The raccoon dog is the only canid that hibernates.
It looks like a raccoon but is only distantly related.
It is an omnivore that eats a wide variety of food.

0057.name: Raccoon
0057.desc: Raccoons are endearing animals with fur that looks like a mask across their faces. They come out at night, climb well and are wonderfully nimble with their paws. They eat all kinds of food and can even be found near cities.
facts: Raccoons are remarkably skilled at handling objects with their front paws.
The black mask-like fur around their eyes gives them a mischievous look.
They climb well and can move surprisingly fast.

0058.name: Skunk

info.서식지: Grassland · Desert · Near cities · North America · South America

0058.desc: Skunks are animals with black and white stripes. When they sense danger they spray a terribly smelly liquid to defend themselves. They can live in forests, on grassland and even near cities.
facts: When threatened, a skunk sprays a foul-smelling liquid to defend itself.
Its black and white stripes work as a warning colour.
Skunks live in many habitats and sometimes dig burrows.

0059.name: Asian Badger
0059.desc: Badgers are endearing little animals that come out mostly at night. They sleep for a long stretch through winter and wake in spring. Badgers swim well and love resting in the undergrowth.
facts: Badgers hibernate, sleeping right through from December to March.
A keen sense of smell helps them find food.
They enjoy swimming and often take to the water in summer.

0060.name: Siberian Weasel
0060.desc: Weasels are animals with long, slim bodies. They have glossy yellowish-brown fur and love to climb trees. Weasels eat a wide variety of food.
facts: Their long, slender bodies let them slip through narrow spaces.
Their yellowish-brown fur is glossy, with white on the chin and lips.
Weasels are keen climbers.

0061.name: Yellow-throated Marten
0061.desc: Martens are about the size of a cat. Their throats are yellow, which gives the yellow-throated marten its name. They live happily up in the trees, move about in groups and eat many kinds of food.
facts: Martens are equally at home living up in the trees.
Their colouring varies, but the throat is distinctively yellow.
They live mainly across Asia.

0062.name: Mongoose
0062.desc: Mongooses look rather like cats. They are famous for bravely taking on snakes, but they mostly eat mice and birds' eggs. They have long tails and grey bodies, and they are lively animals active by day and night.
facts: Mongooses are famous snake fighters, but they prefer mice and birds' eggs.
They move with astonishing speed and agility when facing a snake.
They are fierce by nature but can be tamed.

0063.name: Jaguar
0063.desc: Jaguars are big cats that live in the Americas. They have large dark rosettes on their coats and prefer to live alone. They swim well and catch prey easily with their powerful jaws.
facts: The jaguar is the largest cat in the Americas.
Jaguars swim well and often hunt near water.
Their bite is so powerful they can subdue prey in a single go.

0064.name: Puma

info.서식지: Desert · Rainforest · North America · South America

0064.desc: Pumas are big cats that live across the Americas. They mostly hunt deer and smaller animals, and they rarely attack people. Pumas are wonderfully quick and agile!
facts: Pumas do not see humans as prey, and attacks are rare.
Cubs are born with dark spots that fade after about three months.
They are so agile they can defend themselves from most predators.

0065.name: Eurasian Lynx
0065.desc: Lynx look like large cats. The long tufts on the tips of their ears help them hear extremely well. They mostly live alone in forests, hunting smaller animals.
facts: Lynx have strikingly distinct spotted markings.
The long tufts on their ear tips sharpen their hearing.
They live alone and hold their own territories.

0066.name: Snow Leopard
0066.desc: Snow leopards are magnificent cats that live high in the mountains of Central Asia. They balance with their long tails, and their grey coats hide them beautifully in the snow. They hunt animals such as wild sheep and hares.
facts: Snow leopards use their long tails to balance on cliff edges.
Their grey coats camouflage them in snowy terrain.
They live high in the mountains of Central Asia.

0067.name: Hyena
0067.desc: Hyenas live on the plains of Africa. They move in groups and their jaws are strong enough to crush bone. Among spotted hyenas the females are larger and lead the clan.
facts: Hyenas are related to cats and civets, but behave much like dogs.
Female spotted hyenas are larger than males and dominate the clan.
Their powerful jaws and teeth can crush bone.

0068.name: Coyote
0068.desc: Coyotes look much like dogs. They are mostly active at night and hunt smaller animals. They live across North and Central America.
facts: Coyotes can run at 64 km/h.
A pup born to a coyote and a dog is called a coydog.
They are found widely across North and Central America.

0069.name: Jackal
0069.desc: Jackals look like small wolves. They live in Africa, southern Asia and Europe. Jackals usually live with family rather than alone, and they will eat what other animals leave behind.
facts: Jackals generally live in monogamous pairs.
They mark their territory with urine and droppings.
They act largely as scavengers, feeding on carcasses.

0070.name: Dingo
0070.desc: Dingoes are wild dogs that live in Australia. Their coats come in many shades — tan, dark brown, cream and white. Dingoes live in family groups and are mostly found on open grassland.
facts: The dingo is Australia's wild dog, sometimes classed as a subspecies of the grey wolf.
It lives in open country such as grassland, in family groups.
Coats are usually tan or dark brown, with many colour variations.

0071.name: Brown Bear
0071.desc: Brown bears are large bears. Their fur comes in many shades and they mostly live in forests. They prefer to live alone, though mothers stay with their cubs. Brown bears are omnivores that eat both plants and animals.
facts: Brown bears adapt to a wide range of environments.
They dig for food with powerful front paws.
They live alone, except when raising cubs.

0072.name: Asian Black Bear
0072.desc: Asian black bears have a white V-shape on their chests. They live in forests and mountains and mostly eat plants and fruit. They hibernate through the winter, too!
facts: They are named for the white V-shaped patch on the chest.
They hibernate, and eat mostly grasses, fruit and nuts.
Once widespread across the Korean peninsula, they are now a protected endangered species.

0073.name: Japanese Macaque
0073.desc: Japanese macaques are remarkable monkeys that live in Japan. They cope well with bitter cold and even play in the snow. They groom one another's fur and get along happily with their friends.
facts: Japanese macaques can endure temperatures of -20°C.
They can swim more than 500 metres.
Females stay in the group they were born into for life.

0074.name: Baboon
0074.desc: Baboons are large monkeys that live in Africa. They move in troops and eat all kinds of food. Amusingly, they have pouches inside their mouths for storing food.
facts: Baboons can store food in their large cheek pouches.
They are known for the colouring on their rumps and legs.
A chacma baboon can weigh up to 40 kg.

0075.name: Mandrill
0075.desc: Mandrills are monkeys with wonderfully vivid colours. A male's face and rump are blue and red. They live in troops and eat fruit and insects. Rafiki from The Lion King is a mandrill, too.
facts: Male mandrills have extraordinarily vivid colouring.
They live in the forests of West Africa.
The colour on their rumps grows brighter when they are excited.

0076.name: Lemur
0076.desc: Lemurs are remarkable primates found only on the island of Madagascar. They have long tails and are active at night. They live in the trees, eating fruit and insects.
facts: Lemurs are found nowhere in the world except Madagascar.
They are nocturnal, active through the night.
One toe on each hind foot is a specialised grooming claw.

0077.name: Spider Monkey
0077.desc: Spider monkeys live in the trees and can grip branches with their tails. They eat fruit and nuts and live in troops of 20 to 30. Their coats come in many colours.
facts: Spider monkeys can grip branches with their tails.
They move through the treetops with remarkable agility.
They have almost no thumbs.

0078.name: Tarsier
0078.desc: Tarsiers are very small primates that come out at night. They cannot move their eyes, so they turn their heads to look around. Pads on their fingertips help them cling to trees.
facts: Tarsiers cannot move their eyes, so they rotate their heads up to 180°.
Pads on their fingertips grip trees firmly.
They sleep by day and are active at night.

0079.name: Gibbon
0079.desc: Gibbons have such long arms that they swing between trees beautifully. They live in tropical rainforest and spend most of their lives up in the canopy. They are small apes, and rather charming ones.
facts: Gibbons swing rapidly between trees using their arms.
They are classified as lesser apes.
They live in the rainforests of India and Southeast Asia.

0080.name: Wild Boar
0080.desc: Wild boar are pigs that live in the wild, mostly in forests and hills. They are omnivores and will eat fruit and roots. Their piglets have stripes that help them hide in the forest.
facts: Wild boar are among the most widely distributed mammals on Earth.
Their canine teeth keep growing throughout life, forming large tusks.
Piglets have yellow and white stripes that act as camouflage.

0081.name: Pangolin
0081.desc: Pangolins are remarkable animals covered in scales. The scales are so tough they keep the pangolin safe. Pangolins catch ants with their long tongues, and they are active at night.
facts: Pangolins roll into a ball to defend themselves.
Their scales are made of keratin, like our fingernails.
They catch ants and termites with their long tongues.

0082.name: Dugong
0082.desc: Dugongs are large animals that live in the sea. They feed on seagrass, and their tails look rather like a whale's. People are fond of dugongs, but they are now endangered, and many are working hard to protect them.
facts: The dugong is the only surviving plant-eating marine mammal in its order.
Its whale-like fluked tail sets it apart from other sirenians.
It grazes on seagrass, tearing it up with its lips.

0083.name: Manatee
0083.desc: Manatees are big sea animals. They are herbivores that eat great quantities of sea plants. Now and then they rise to the surface for a breath. People have nicknamed them 'mermaids of the sea'.
facts: Manatees nurse their young at the surface, which earned them the 'mermaid' nickname.
A manatee can eat up to 50 kg of water plants a day.
They took to the sea largely to escape predators on land.

0084.name: Gazelle
0084.desc: Gazelles are graceful animals that can run beautifully across the plains. They mostly eat grass and cope well even in dry places like deserts. Their tails are tipped with black and their coats carry handsome markings.
facts: Gazelles live mainly in dry country such as savannah and desert.
They get their water from plants, browsing on shoots and grass in the evening.
Both males and females carry lyre-shaped horns.

0085.name: Impala
0085.desc: Impalas are handsome animals of the grasslands. They are active during the day and can leap high on their long legs. Impalas eat grass and fruit and move about with the herd.
facts: Impalas are superb jumpers, which helps them escape predators.
They are active by day and live in a range of habitats.
There are two subspecies, one of which is the black-faced impala.

0086.name: Wildebeest
0086.desc: Wildebeest are large animals of the African plains. They gather in herds alongside zebras and travel great distances. They have dark grey bodies and distinctive curving horns.
facts: Wildebeest often herd together with zebras.
Both males and females have horns, curved into a distinctive shape.
They travel up to 2,000 km in search of grass.

0087.name: American Bison
0087.desc: American bison are enormous animals that live on the plains of North America. They have shaggy coats, move in herds and graze on grass. They were once close to extinction, but their numbers have recovered.
facts: Bison once numbered over 60 million, but fell to just 541 in the 19th century.
They were a vital resource on the North American plains.
Bison now live again across the United States, Canada and Mexico.

0088.name: Water Buffalo
0088.desc: Water buffalo are animals with great horns and powerful bodies. They live in herds around lakes and marshes, and they bathe in mud to get rid of parasites. They also help people with farm work.
facts: A water buffalo's horns can grow up to two metres long.
They bathe in mud to remove parasites.
Their milk is rich in fat and highly nutritious.

0089.name: Yak
0089.desc: Yaks look like large cattle and live high in mountains such as the Himalayas. Their coats are so long and warm that they cope well with the cold. Yaks can carry heavy loads a long way, which is a great help to people.
facts: Long hair lets yaks endure the cold of high mountains.
A yak can carry a heavy load up to 32 km in a day.
Yak milk is very rich, and the meat is dried or grilled.

0090.name: Long-tailed Goral
0090.desc: Gorals live in the mountains. Their sturdy legs carry them up steep ground, and they live in small groups. They graze on grass and sometimes come down closer to people.
facts: Gorals live mostly among rugged rocks and caves.
In Korea they are protected as a natural monument.
Sharp sight, hearing and smell let them navigate their territory precisely.

0091.name: Chinchilla
0091.desc: Chinchillas are small animals from the Andes of South America. Their fur is so soft and thick that stroking it feels wonderful. They jump so well they sometimes look like little rabbits. Chinchillas must never be bathed in water — they take dust baths instead.
facts: Chinchilla fur is so dense they must bathe in dust rather than water.
Pikachu was modelled on a chinchilla.
Short front legs and long hind legs make them excellent jumpers.

0092.name: Marmot
0092.desc: Marmots are squirrel relatives that live in the mountains. They sleep through winter, and they whistle loudly to warn the others of danger. They live on grass and leaves.
facts: Marmots hibernate through the winter.
They whistle loudly to signal danger to the rest of the colony.
They dig burrows or shelter among rocks.

0093.name: African Porcupine
0093.desc: Porcupines are animals covered in quills. They defend themselves with those quills and eat nuts and roots. They are active mostly at night and hide away in burrows during the day.
facts: A porcupine's quills detach easily and lodge in an attacker's skin.
Lost quills grow back over time.
They drive off attackers with their quills to defend themselves.

0094.name: Civet
0094.desc: Civets look rather like cats. They live mostly up in the trees and eat many kinds of food. They have the unusual ability to produce a distinctive scent called musk.
facts: Civets are sometimes farmed for the musk scent they produce.
Most civets spend their lives in the trees.
They adapt well to a variety of environments.

0095.name: Opossum
0095.desc: Opossums carry their babies in a pouch. They mostly live on grassland and eat all sorts of food. About the size of a cat, they get along perfectly well on their own.
facts: As marsupials, opossums raise their young in a pouch on the belly.
Their predators include pumas, jaguars and ocelots.
They survive in a wide range of environments.

0096.name: Wombat
0096.desc: Wombats are endearing animals from Australia. They are stocky, dig superb burrows and live on grass. Curiously, their droppings come out cube-shaped.
facts: Wombat droppings are unusually cube-shaped.
Wombats glow under ultraviolet light.
They dig long burrows with powerful claws.

0097.name: Tasmanian Devil
0097.desc: Tasmanian devils are small animals from the island of Tasmania. They have black fur with white markings. They are active at night and eat many kinds of food. A facial cancer cut their numbers sharply, though more resistant animals are now appearing.
facts: The Tasmanian devil is the largest carnivorous marsupial.
Its unsettling cry earned it the name 'devil'.
Devil facial tumour disease has pushed it towards extinction.

0098.name: Platypus
0098.desc: Platypuses are wonderfully odd animals with a bill like a duck's and a tail like a beaver's. They lay eggs and swim beautifully. Platypuses live in Australia.
facts: The platypus is a monotreme — a mammal that lays eggs.
Males carry venom in a spur on their hind legs.
It combines features of a duck, a beaver and an otter.

0099.name: Echidna
0099.desc: Echidnas are remarkable mammals that lay eggs. Their spiny bodies protect them well from predators. They eat mostly ants and termites, and they live in Australia and Papua New Guinea.
facts: As a monotreme, it is one of the very few mammals that lay eggs.
Electroreceptors in its snout help it find food.
It curls into a ball and defends itself with its spines.

0100.name: Tapir
0100.desc: Tapirs are endearing animals with long noses. They live alone in the forest, eating leaves and fruit. Newborn tapirs are covered in white stripes.
facts: Newborn tapirs have white stripes, rather like wild boar piglets.
Tapirs love to swim.
The nose is a short, flexible trunk they can move freely.

0102.name: Guinea Pig
0102.desc: Guinea pigs are small animals that came from South America. They love company and whistle to talk with their friends. Their soft fur and gentle nature make them lovely pets for children.
facts: Guinea pigs are social animals that live in groups.
Their eyes are set wide apart, letting them see behind themselves.
They communicate with distinctive whistling sounds.

0103.name: Mouse

info.서식지: Near cities · Asia · Africa · Europe · North America

0103.desc: House mice are small, endearing rodents found almost everywhere in the world except the poles. They are active mostly at night, and their tiny bodies run and climb remarkably well. Mice live close to people and come in many coat colours.
facts: House mice live close to people, across nearly every region except the poles.
They are most active at dusk and through the night.
Their coats come in many colours, and albino mice exist too.

0104.name: Brown Rat
0104.desc: Brown rats are the rats you often see in cities. They have brown or grey fur and are mostly active at night. They move in groups and eat many kinds of food.
facts: Brown rats live wherever people live, all around the world.
They are omnivores that eat almost anything, but grain matters most.
Males lead the group, which can reach up to 200 rats.

0105.name: Kangaroo Rat
0105.desc: Kangaroo rats are small rodents that stand on two legs and bound along in hops. They live in western North America and mostly eat seeds. Snakes sometimes hunt them.
facts: Kangaroo rats hop along much like a full-sized kangaroo.
They are found mainly in western North America.
Snakes are their main predator.

0107.name: Common Squirrel Monkey
0107.desc: Squirrel monkeys are small monkeys that live in the trees of the rainforest. They eat fruit and insects and scamper up and down branches at speed. Living in troops, they enjoy adventures together.
facts: Squirrel monkeys have the largest brain-to-body ratio of any primate.
They move through the branches remarkably quickly.
They mark their territory by rubbing urine on their bodies.

0108.name: Capuchin Monkey
0108.desc: Capuchin monkeys are small monkeys that live in the forests of Central and South America. They are highly intelligent and can eat a wide range of foods. They travel in troops and live up in the trees, foraging by day and sleeping in the branches at night.
facts: Capuchins eat an extremely varied diet.
They live across Central and South America.
They are clever and highly adaptable.

0109.name: Bonobo
0109.desc: Bonobos are among our closest living relatives. They have long arms and legs and are especially fond of fruit. They live in the forests of Central Africa.
facts: Bonobos are one of our closest living relatives.
They eat mostly fruit, with a diet different from chimpanzees'.
They live only in the Congo Basin of Central Africa.

0110.name: Galago
0110.desc: Galagos are small primates that come out at night. They also go by the charming nickname 'bushbaby'. They live in the rainforests of Africa.
facts: Galagos are active mainly at night.
The nickname 'bushbaby' comes from their call.
They live in the rainforests of the African continent.

0111.name: Slow Loris
0111.desc: Slow lorises are small primates from Southeast Asia. They move slowly and have big eyes that make them look endearing. But do be careful — the slow loris is a venomous primate!
facts: The slow loris is the only venomous primate.
It moves slowly and has strikingly large eyes.
It is prized in traditional medicine, which puts it at risk from hunting.

0112.name: Aye-aye
0112.desc: Aye-ayes are remarkable primates from Madagascar. They are active at night, tapping on wood to find grubs to eat. Their long, sharp fingers let them dig insects out of the trees.
facts: The aye-aye is the world's largest nocturnal primate.
It taps on wood to locate grubs — a hunting method all its own.
Its unusual look and behaviour make some locals treat it as a bad omen.

0114.name: Kinkajou
0114.desc: Kinkajous are animals that live in the trees and come out at night. They live in tropical rainforest and love fruit. About the size of a cat, they are wonderfully charming.
facts: Kinkajous are often mistaken for ferrets or monkeys, but are related to neither.
They are arboreal, spending their lives in the trees.
They have been hunted for their fur.

0115.name: Wolverine

info.서식지: Polar regions · North America · Europe · Asia

0115.desc: Wolverines are the largest members of the weasel family, with sturdy bodies and powerful claws. They live mostly on the tundra and hunt a wide range of animals. Their special coats keep them comfortable even in bitter cold.
facts: The wolverine is the largest land member of the weasel family.
Its thick coat keeps out cold and frost.
It is strong enough to hunt animals far larger than itself.

0116.name: Mink
0116.desc: Minks are small animals in the weasel family. They live near water and mostly catch mice and fish. Their fur is wonderfully soft, which people prize.
facts: Minks live mainly near water.
They have a habit of catching more prey than they can eat.
Their fur is so valuable that minks are farmed.

0117.name: Ferret
0117.desc: Ferrets are endearing pets in the weasel family. They sleep for most of the day, but play energetically when awake. Being carnivores, they need a special diet.
facts: Ferrets are domesticated and cannot survive in the wild.
They sleep 15–18 hours a day.
They are even more strictly carnivorous than cats.

0118.name: Northern Fur Seal
0118.desc: Fur seals are endearing animals of the sea. Males have a handsome mane around the neck. They eat fish and squid and swim swiftly through the ocean.
facts: Males develop a mane over the neck and shoulders.
They form harems, with 30–50 females to a single male.
They live about 25 years.

0119.name: Harp Seal
0119.desc: Harp seals are seals that live in the sea. They are named for the harp-shaped marking on their bodies. Harp seal pups are born covered in white fur and look wonderfully sweet. They hunt underwater and can live up to 30 years.
facts: Harp seals are named for the harp-shaped marking on their backs.
Pups are born covered in soft white fur.
Males tend to grow larger than females.

0120.name: Narwhal
0120.desc: Narwhals are remarkable whales of the Arctic Ocean. They carry a tusk that looks like a long horn, which is why they are called 'unicorns of the sea'. They eat mostly fish and travel together in pods.
facts: A narwhal's tusk is actually a hugely elongated canine tooth.
They live in the cold waters of the Arctic.
Orcas and polar bears are their main predators.

0121.name: Sperm Whale

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0121.desc: Sperm whales are whales with enormous heads. They can dive to great depths of the ocean. They eat squid and fish and can live more than 70 years.
facts: The sperm whale has the largest brain of any animal on Earth.
It can dive to depths of 2,250 metres.
It makes the loudest sound of any animal underwater.

0122.name: Blue Whale

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0122.desc: Blue whales are the largest animals in the world. They live on krill and small fish in the ocean. Blue whales can live a very long time — some more than 100 years.
facts: The blue whale is the largest animal that has ever lived.
It feeds mainly on krill.
It can swim at up to 48 km/h.

0123.name: Harbor Porpoise

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Arctic Ocean

0123.desc: Harbour porpoises are small whales with short snouts and a charming look. They swim alone or in small groups and eat fish. Females grow a little larger than males.
facts: Harbour porpoises usually live alone or in small groups rather than large pods.
Females are larger, reaching up to 1.7 m.
They are found mainly in shallow coastal waters.

0124.name: Nutria

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · South America · North America · Europe · Asia

0124.desc: Nutria look rather like large rats. They swim beautifully and live on plants. Their fur is warm and has been used to make clothing.
facts: Nutria are large rodents, growing up to 100 cm long.
They live in the water and are skilled swimmers and divers.
Their fur is warm and hard-wearing, and used for fur products.

0125.name: Elephant Shrew
0125.desc: Elephant shrews are small animals from Africa. Despite the name, they are not related to elephants at all — but their noses do look rather like a trunk. They eat mostly insects and can move remarkably fast.
facts: Elephant shrews are not actually related to elephants.
They move very fast, which helps them escape predators.
They live mainly on insects.

0126.name: Agouti
0126.desc: Agoutis are rodents that look rather like small deer. They grow to about 60 cm and have short tails. They live in the forest, eating nuts and leaves.
facts: The agouti gave its name to a gene that determines coat colour.
Agouti young are born already furred and with their eyes open.
Their bounding run looks much like a deer's.

0127.name: Saiga Antelope
0127.desc: Saiga are animals with a truly remarkable nose. They live mainly on the steppe, grazing on grass. That distinctive nose makes them easy to recognise.
facts: The saiga has a uniquely shaped, bulbous nose.
It lives mainly on open steppe.
It grazes mostly on grasses.

0128.name: Kudu
0128.desc: Kudu are magnificent antelope from Africa. They carry long, spiralling horns. Their brown coats and stripes hide them well among the trees.
facts: Kudu carry long, spiralling horns.
Their brown coats and stripes help camouflage them.
They live mainly in acacia and commiphora scrubland.

0129.name: Oryx
0129.desc: Oryx are antelope that live in the desert. The black markings on their faces and bodies make them look magnificent. They get by beautifully on very little water!
facts: Oryx are adapted to the desert and can survive a long time without water.
They are marked by white faces with black striping.
Their long horns serve as a means of defence.

0130.name: Springbok
0130.desc: Springbok are fast animals from Africa. They live mainly on grassland eating grass, and can run at 88 km/h. Their name comes from the word for 'to leap'.
facts: Springbok can run at 88 km/h, making them remarkably fast.
Their name comes from the word meaning 'to leap'.
They can bound up to 4 m into the air.

0131.name: Alpine Ibex
0131.desc: Ibex are goats that live high in the mountains. Males carry great horns, and their coats are brown and grey. They bound about with ease even on high ground.
facts: The ibex is a mammal with impressively large horns.
Males are larger, with sweeping curved horns.
They live high in the Alps.

0132.name: Gaur
0132.desc: Gaur look like enormous cattle. They live mostly in forests and grassland, eating grass and leaves. They move in herds and are wonderfully strong.
facts: The gaur is the largest wild cattle species in the world.
It lives in the forests and grasslands of Asia.
It feeds mainly on grass and leaves.

0133.name: European Bison
0133.desc: European bison are the heaviest animals in Europe. They roam the forests with their great bodies and long horns. They were once hunted heavily, but are specially protected today.
facts: The European bison is the heaviest land mammal in Europe.
Most now roam in forests.
A past population bottleneck leaves them vulnerable to certain diseases.

0134.name: Takin
0134.desc: Takin look rather like large goats. Look at the flag of Bhutan and you will find a takin on it. They live high in the mountains and gather in family herds.
facts: The takin is the national animal of Bhutan.
In summer, takin gather in herds of up to 300.
Their coat length changes with the seasons.

0135.name: Pronghorn
0135.desc: Pronghorn are animals of the open plains. Their coats are reddish-brown with white around the tail. They are related to giraffes, though they look like antelope. Pumas, coyotes and wolves are their predators.
facts: Pronghorn live on the prairies of North America.
Their closest relatives are giraffes and okapi.
Both sexes have horns, and the sheaths are shed each year.

0136.name: Okapi
0136.desc: Okapi are relatives of the giraffe. The stripes on their legs make them look rather like zebras. They pull leaves down with long tongues and live in the forests of the Congo.
facts: The okapi belongs to the giraffe family and is closest to the giraffe.
Its striped legs cause it to be mistaken for a zebra.
Its long tongue lets it reach leaves high above.

0137.name: Wallaby
0137.desc: Wallabies are smaller members of the kangaroo family. They mostly eat grass and plants and bound high on powerful hind legs. A young wallaby is called a joey.
facts: Wallabies leap high on powerful hind legs.
They are herbivores that eat a variety of grasses and plants.
A young wallaby is called a joey.

0138.name: Quokka
0138.desc: Quokkas are famous for faces that always look like they are smiling. They live only on small islands in Australia. They are active mostly at night and live on grass.
facts: The quokka's permanent smile earned it the nickname 'happiest animal in the world'.
It is found only on Rottnest Island and a few other spots in Australia.
It is nocturnal, active mainly at night.

0139.name: Greater Bilby
0139.desc: Bilbies are endearing animals that live in the desert. Their long ears and noses help them explore their surroundings. They are active at night and eat many kinds of food.
facts: The bilby is a desert-dwelling marsupial.
It is known for its long ears and nose, and is active mainly at night.
There were once two species, but only one survives today.

0140.name: Sugar Glider
0140.desc: Sugar gliders are small animals active at night. They live in the trees and can glide a long way. They look much like flying squirrels, but they are quite different animals.
facts: Sugar gliders are lively and live up in the trees.
Convergent evolution gave them a look much like flying squirrels'.
Despite their small size, they can glide considerable distances.

0141.name: Elephant Seal

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Southern Ocean

0141.desc: Elephant seals are sea animals with big noses and bodies, just as the name suggests. They spend most of their time at sea, eating fish and squid. Males fight one another to win mates.
facts: Elephant seals dive longer than any other mammal, staying under for up to 80 minutes.
Males fight fiercely for mates, and the winner breeds with many females.
They are named for their size and their large, trunk-like noses.

0142.name: Star-nosed Mole
0142.desc: Star-nosed moles are remarkable moles with a star-shaped nose. They live in damp places across Canada and the United States. These small animals eat insects, using that nose to find food astonishingly fast.
facts: The star-shaped nose is made up of 22 touch-sensitive appendages.
They can even hunt underwater.
They are among the fastest foragers in the world.

0143.name: White-winged Flying Fox
0143.desc: Flying foxes are remarkable bats with white wings. They live only in the Philippines and feed on fruit. Their fox-like faces are wonderfully endearing!
facts: They have faces that look much like a fox's.
They are named for their white wings.
They are found only in the Philippines.

0144.name: Vicuña
0144.desc: Vicuñas look like small camels. They have no humps and wonderfully soft fleece. They live in herds high in the mountains, grazing on grass. Their fleece is so fine it is used for luxury clothing.
facts: The vicuña is the smallest camelid and has no hump.
In the Inca Empire, vicuña wool was reserved for nobility.
Declared endangered in 1974, numbers have since recovered to about 125,000.

0145.name: Howler Monkey
0145.desc: Howler monkeys are large monkeys from the forests of South and Central America. They are famous for their tremendously loud calls. They live in troops of around 18.
facts: Howler monkeys are the largest of the New World monkeys.
They are famous for their extremely loud calls.
Poaching has put them at risk.

0146.name: Colugo
0146.desc: Colugos are remarkable animals that glide from tree to tree. They look rather like a cross between a lemur and a flying squirrel. They live in the forests of Southeast Asia.
facts: They glide between trees much like flying squirrels.
They are close relatives of the primates.
They are found mainly in the rainforests of Southeast Asia.

0147.name: Giant Otter
0147.desc: Giant otters are large otters from South America. They travel in family groups and catch fish in the rivers. They talk to one another with calls, and their coats are specially built to shed water.
facts: The giant otter has the longest body of any member of the weasel family.
It is famous for its complex calls, with distinct sounds for communicating.
Its coat is specially adapted to keep water out.

0148.name: Korean Hare
0148.desc: Korean hares live on the Korean peninsula and in northeastern China. Their coats change with the seasons — white in winter and brown in summer. They come out mostly in the evening to graze and roam.
facts: Korean hares moult to white in winter and brown in summer.
Leverets can open their eyes and walk as soon as they are born.
They are active mainly in the evening and feed on plants.

0149.name: Naked Mole Rat
0149.desc: Naked mole-rats are small animals with almost no hair and smooth, bare skin. They dig tunnels underground and live together in large colonies. They are found in East Africa.
facts: Naked mole-rats have almost no hair and wrinkled skin.
They live in colonies with a social structure inside their burrows.
Remarkably, they feel almost no pain.

0150.name: Javan Lutung
0150.desc: Javan lutungs live mainly on the island of Java in Indonesia. They have long tails and black fur. They live in the trees, eating leaves and fruit. Their babies are born with orange fur!
facts: Javan lutungs live mostly in the trees.
Infants are born with orange fur.
A specialised stomach lets them digest leaves.

0151.name: Emperor Penguin
0151.desc: Emperor penguins are the largest and heaviest of all penguins. They live in Antarctica, and the fathers are magnificent — guarding their eggs through the bitterest winter. Emperor penguins live together in enormous colonies.
facts: Emperor penguins are the largest and heaviest of all penguins.
They are the only penguin that lays eggs during the Antarctic winter.
The male balances the egg on his feet and keeps it warm for about four months.

0152.name: Cinereous Vulture
0152.desc: Vultures are very large birds. They mostly keep to themselves, though small groups gather in winter. They are not good hunters and feed mainly on carcasses. In Korea they are specially protected.
facts: Vultures remember nests they have used and return to them.
In Korea they are protected as a natural monument.
They do not hunt, feeding instead on carrion.

0153.name: Peregrine Falcon

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Europe · Africa · North America · South America · Oceania

0153.desc: Peregrine falcons are the fastest-flying birds in the sky. Their wingspan reaches 120 cm, and they can spot prey from a great distance. They nest on high coastal cliffs all around the world.
facts: A falcon's eyesight is about eight times sharper than ours.
They catch prey in dives exceeding 300 km/h.
They are found across the entire world.

0154.name: Himalayan Owl
0154.desc: Owls are birds that come out at night. They live in forests across Asia, from the Himalayas to Korea. Owls have wonderfully distinctive calls.
facts: Owls hunt mainly at night.
They live in forests from the Himalayas to Korea and Taiwan.
Their distinctive call sets them apart from related species.

0155.name: Eurasian Eagle-owl
0155.desc: Eagle-owls are large birds active at night. The ear-like tufts on their heads look magnificent. They live mostly in mountains and along rivers, hunting small animals.
facts: The eagle-owl is known for its ear-like tufts.
It is nocturnal, active mainly at night.
Its cat-like face earned it an old Korean name meaning 'cat-headed hawk'.

0156.name: Parrot

info.서식지: Rainforest · Near cities · South America · Africa · Asia · Oceania

0156.desc: Parrots are birds with beautiful colours. They can copy human speech, which makes them wonderful companions. Some parrots live a remarkably long time!
facts: Parrots can imitate human speech.
Their intelligence is comparable to a two- or three-year-old child's.
Some parrots have lived to 120 years old.

0157.name: Flamingo

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Africa · Asia · South America · Europe

0157.desc: Flamingos are birds that live by the water, with long legs and long necks. Their bodies are pink or red, and they have the amusing habit of standing on one leg. They live in flocks and eat small creatures and plants.
facts: Flamingos have the distinctive habit of standing on one leg.
Their bright colour comes mainly from their diet.
They live together in enormous flocks.

0158.name: Peacock
0158.desc: Peacocks are large birds with magnificent feathers. They spend most of their time on the ground, though they can fly. Males fan out their beautiful trains and dance.
facts: Male peacocks fan their spectacular trains to attract females.
The pattern on the feathers looks like a series of eyes.
Peacocks can fly, but spend most of their lives on the ground.

0159.name: Common Ostrich
0159.desc: Ostriches are large birds that live in Africa. They cannot fly, but they can run astonishingly fast. An ostrich egg is among the largest eggs in the world. Ostriches live together in groups.
facts: Ostriches cannot fly, but they run at over 90 km/h.
The ostrich egg is the largest of any living bird.
They outrun lions and have more stamina than cheetahs.

0160.name: Kiwi
0160.desc: Kiwis are endearing birds from New Zealand. They cannot fly, but they scurry about the ground beautifully. They are active at night and probe for food with their pointed bills.
facts: The kiwi is a flightless bird.
It is nocturnal, active mainly at night.
It is the symbol of New Zealand.

0161.name: Hummingbird
0161.desc: Hummingbirds are very small birds that drink nectar from flowers. Like bees, they can hold perfectly still in mid-air. Their feathers shimmer beautifully.
facts: Hummingbirds are the smallest birds in the world.
They are the only birds that can hover in place.
Their feathers carry a strong metallic sheen.

0162.name: Woodpecker

info.서식지: Rainforest · Grassland · Asia · Europe · North America · South America · Africa

0162.desc: Woodpeckers drill holes in trees to catch the insects inside. They live in forests and woodlands, and a few even in deserts. Their long tongues let them pull food easily out of the wood.
facts: Woodpeckers use powerful bills to drill into wood.
Their tongues are very long and sticky, ideal for catching insects inside trees.
They often appear in popular culture — Woody Woodpecker being the most famous.

0163.name: Carrion Crow
0163.desc: Crows are intelligent birds. Their black feathers gleam, and they live in groups. Crows build nests in trees and on buildings to lay their eggs. You can find them in many parts of the world.
facts: Crows are highly intelligent and can remember individual people.
The origin of the Korean dish yaksik is linked to a crow.
The legendary three-legged crow is thought to be based on the bird.

0164.name: Tundra Swan

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · North America · Asia · Europe

0164.desc: Swans are large birds that live on water. Their white feathers and long necks make them easy to spot from far away. In winter they travel to warmer places. Swans stay with their families and look after one another well.
facts: Swans are protected in Korea as a natural monument.
They are strongly territorial and can be aggressive.
They keep strong bonds within the family.

0165.name: Duck

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Ocean · Asia · Europe · North America · South America · Africa · Oceania

0165.nickname: The bird that plays on water
0165.desc: Ducks are birds that play on the water. Their webbed feet make them fine swimmers, and they sift the water with their bills to find food. Drakes show off colourful plumage to attract a mate.
facts: Webbed feet make ducks strong swimmers and divers.
Drakes attract females with colourful plumage in the breeding season.
Ducks look elegant on water but waddle on land.

0166.name: Chicken

info.서식지: Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America · South America · Africa · Oceania

0166.desc: Chickens are animals we see all the time. They are important farm animals because they give us eggs and meat. The red comb on a chicken's head makes it easy to recognise.
facts: Chickens are among the most widely kept farm animals in the world.
Depending on the breed, a hen lays around 200 eggs a year.
Their intelligence is comparable to a seven-year-old child's.

0167.name: Pelican

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Africa · Asia · Europe · North America

0167.desc: Pelicans are birds known for their enormous bills and the pouch beneath. They mostly eat fish and live around rivers and lakes. Pelicans lay eggs and bring food back up from their throats to feed their chicks.
facts: The large pouch under the bill makes catching fish easy.
There are eight species of pelican worldwide.
They regurgitate food from the pouch to feed their young.

0168.name: Common Gull
0168.desc: Gulls are birds you often see by the sea. Their bodies are white and their wings grey. In winter, brown streaks appear on their heads. They eat fish, squid and insects.
facts: Gulls filter salt from seawater through glands above their eyes.
In Korea they are seen mainly as winter visitors.
The gull is the official bird of Busan.

0169.name: Albatross

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean · Southern Ocean

0169.desc: Albatrosses are large birds that fly over the ocean. Their wings are so long they can travel enormous distances. They live together in colonies.
facts: The albatross has one of the largest wingspans of any bird.
It travels vast distances gliding over the sea.
Albatrosses pair for life.

0170.name: Red-crowned Crane
0170.desc: Red-crowned cranes are large birds with long necks and legs, white bodies and a red crown. They communicate by dancing and live together in family groups.
facts: Red-crowned cranes live in family groups.
They court one another by dancing.
Apart from humans, they have almost no predators.

0171.name: Oriental Stork
0171.desc: Storks are large birds. Their bodies are white with black wingtips. They live in wetlands and eat fish and small animals. Storks lay eggs and care for their chicks.
facts: The oriental stork is protected in Korea as a natural monument.
It lives mainly in wetlands.
It is a carnivore that eats a wide range of animals.

0172.name: Grey Heron
0172.desc: Grey herons are large birds with long legs and necks. They stand by the water catching fish and frogs. In Korea you often see them in summer.
facts: Herons use their long legs and necks to catch food at the water's edge.
In the breeding season their bills and legs turn bright red.
They are a common summer sight in Korea.

0174.name: Common Kingfisher
0174.desc: Kingfishers are small, beautiful birds with brilliant blue bodies. They dive swiftly into the water to catch fish. In Korea they lay eggs in spring and travel to warmer places in autumn.
facts: A protective membrane shields their eyes as they dive for fish.
Males court females by presenting them with a fish.
They feed their chicks around 50 fish a day.

0175.name: Barn Swallow

info.서식지: Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa · North America · South America · Oceania

0175.desc: Swallows are birds that catch insects on the wing. They build their nests from mud and plant material. There is an old saying that when swallows fly low, rain is coming.
facts: Swallows build their nests from mud and plant matter.
An old saying holds that swallows flying low means rain.
They repair and reuse the same nest year after year.

0176.name: Eurasian Tree Sparrow
0176.desc: Sparrows are small brown birds you can find anywhere, in cities and countryside alike. They mostly eat seeds, and they catch pest insects too. You can watch them build nests, lay eggs and raise their chicks.
facts: Sparrows are resident birds, usually found near where people live.
They can damage crops, but they also help farming by eating pests.
Pairs build the nest together in the breeding season.

0177.name: Pigeon

info.서식지: Near cities · Asia · Europe · Africa · North America · South America · Oceania

0177.desc: Pigeons are birds you can easily spot around us. They eat seeds and beat their wings across the sky. Pigeons are also well known as a symbol of peace.
facts: The pigeon is often used as a symbol of peace.
Pigeons feed their young a special 'crop milk'.
City pigeons are sometimes considered a nuisance.

0178.name: Magpie
0178.desc: Magpies are birds with black and white feathers. Their long black tails make them easy to recognise. Magpies eat fruit and insects, and sometimes help people out. In Korea, a calling magpie is said to bring good news.
facts: Magpies have intelligence comparable to a six-year-old child's.
In Korea the magpie is considered a bird of good fortune.
It was the first non-mammal shown to recognise itself in a mirror.

0179.name: Common Cuckoo
0179.desc: Cuckoos slip their eggs into other birds' nests. You can hear a cuckoo's call in a cuckoo clock. When a cuckoo chick grows up, it lays its own eggs in another bird's nest in turn.
facts: Cuckoos practise brood parasitism, leaving eggs in other birds' nests.
Their call is used as the hourly chime in cuckoo clocks.
After hatching, the chick pushes the host's eggs and young out of the nest.

0180.name: Turkey
0180.desc: Turkeys are large birds originally from North America and Mexico. Amusingly, the colour of their skin can change. In the United States, turkey is eaten at Thanksgiving.
facts: Turkeys are native to North America and Mexico.
Males have fleshy, stretchable growths on the head.
In the United States it is traditional to eat turkey at Thanksgiving.

0181.name: Domestic Goose
0181.desc: Geese are birds with long necks and white bodies. They usually live alongside people, and they call out when a stranger approaches. Geese can live nearly 20 years and will eat almost anything!
facts: Geese are omnivores that will eat almost anything.
Their down is so warm it is used in duvets and winter clothing.
They call out at strangers, acting rather like guards.

0185.name: Emu
0185.desc: Emus are large birds that live in Australia. They cannot fly, but they run and swim beautifully. Emus eat fruit and insects and live in groups. Their plumage is dark greyish-brown and their necks are long.
facts: The emu is flightless and the second-largest bird after the ostrich.
Its feathers are coarse, rather like animal hair.
It appears on Australia's coat of arms alongside the kangaroo.

0186.name: Macaw
0186.desc: Macaws are large parrots with wonderfully vivid colours. They often live in flocks and eat a variety of seeds and fruit. Macaws recognise one another by the unique feather patterns on their faces.
facts: Each macaw has a unique facial feather pattern that identifies it.
They eat a wide range of foods, including seeds, fruit and bark.
They lick clay to take in sodium.

0187.name: Adelie Penguin
0187.desc: Adélie penguins are endearing penguins that live in Antarctica. They eat mostly krill and build nests on thick ice in summer. The white rings around their eyes make them easy to recognise.
facts: Adélie penguins have the widest range of any penguin on the Antarctic continent.
They eat mostly krill, along with fish and cephalopods.
White rings around the eyes are their distinguishing feature.

0188.name: Andean Condor
0188.desc: Condors are very large birds. With wings spread they reach three metres across. They live mainly in the Andes and feed on carrion. They look magnificent in flight!
facts: A condor's wingspan reaches about three metres.
It lives mainly in the Andes of South America.
It acts as a scavenger, feeding on carrion.

0189.name: Eurasian Hoopoe
0189.desc: Hoopoes are birds with a magnificent crest on the head. Black and white striped wings and tails are their trademark. They search the ground for insects, and when attacked they can release a distinctive smell.
facts: It has a striking crest of feathers on its head.
Its wings and tail carry black and white stripes.
When attacked it secretes a foul-smelling liquid.

0190.name: Eurasian Skylark
0190.desc: Skylarks are small brown birds, with white edges showing at the wingtips when they spread their wings. They build nests on the ground and lay their eggs there. In winter they gather in flocks.
facts: Skylarks raise their crest when excited.
They build their nests on the ground.
They gather in flocks through the winter.

0192.name: Toucan
0192.desc: Toucans are birds with enormous bills. They live mostly in the trees and eat seeds. They lay several white eggs at a time.
facts: Toucans are famous for their enormous bills.
They live mainly up in the trees.
They usually lay two to four white eggs in the nest.

0193.name: Bird-of-paradise
0193.desc: Birds-of-paradise have wonderfully splendid plumage. They live mainly on the island of New Guinea, laying eggs and raising their chicks. They are so special they appear on the flag of Papua New Guinea.
facts: Birds-of-paradise are famous for their splendid plumage and symbolise Papua New Guinea.
When first brought to Europe in the 16th century they were called 'birds of heaven'.
Their feathers are used in traditional New Guinean decoration.

0194.name: Oriental Dollarbird
0194.desc: Dollarbirds visit Korea in summer. They catch insects from the treetops, and their blue and green bodies are beautiful. With wings spread they look wonderful in flight.
facts: Dollarbirds live mainly in the treetops, catching insects.
In Korea they are summer migrants only.
Their flight has a distinctive rolling, tumbling motion.

0196.name: Japanese White-eye
0196.desc: White-eyes are small green birds with a white ring around each eye. They mostly drink nectar from flowers and build nests in the trees. You can see many of them on Jeju and Ulleung islands.
facts: White-eyes live alongside camellias, drinking nectar and pollinating the flowers.
The white ring around the eye makes them easy to identify.
They build basket-shaped nests on branches.

0197.name: Eurasian Wren

info.서식지: Near cities · Europe · Asia · North America

0197.desc: Wrens are very small birds, no bigger than the palm of your hand. In summer they live in the mountains, and in winter they come closer to houses. They eat small insects, and they slip through the undergrowth, so they are hard to spot.
facts: The wren is a tiny bird, about 10 cm long.
It lives in the mountains in summer and near houses in winter.
It sings softly and stays in the undergrowth, so it is easily missed.

0198.name: White-cheeked Starling
0198.desc: Starlings are small birds in grey and black. They have white feathers around the face and live in flocks. They nest in trees and eat earthworms and berries.
facts: Starlings tend to live in flocks.
In the breeding season they sing in clear, high notes.
Males and females take turns incubating the eggs.

0199.name: Common Hill Myna
0199.desc: Hill mynas are amusing birds that copy human speech. Their bodies are black with white patches on the wings. They mostly eat fruit and insects and live in forests.
facts: Hill mynas are excellent mimics of human speech.
Their black plumage has a purple sheen, with white patches on the wings.
They are native to India, Southeast Asia and China.

0200.name: Cinereous Tit
0200.desc: Tits are small, endearing birds. You often see them near towns. They eat insects and tend to move about in flocks.
facts: Tits adapt well to a wide range of environments.
They are often found close to people, even in cities.
They live mainly on insects.

0201.name: Grey Parrot
0201.desc: Grey parrots are intelligent birds that live in Africa's tropical rainforests. Their feathers are grey but their tails are red. They are remarkably good at copying human speech, so they make wonderful companions.
facts: Grey parrots are highly intelligent, comparable to a four- to six-year-old child.
They can harm themselves when stress builds up.
They can learn 100 words and identify 50 different objects.

0202.name: Budgerigar
0202.desc: Budgerigars are small parrots that come in many colours. They can copy human speech, so they make good friends. They come from Australia and grow up eating seeds.
facts: Budgerigars can imitate human speech.
They have been bred into many different colour varieties.
They are intelligent and can learn many behaviours through training.

0203.name: Lilian's Lovebird
0203.desc: Lovebirds are small and endearing parrots. Green and orange blend beautifully across their bodies. They eat seeds and build nests in tree crevices to lay their eggs. They really are friendly little parrots!
facts: Lilian's lovebird is one of the smallest parrots in Africa.
They come in a variety of colour patterns.
Once they warm to their keeper, they become very attached.

0204.name: Gentoo Penguin
0204.desc: Gentoo penguins are endearing penguins with a white patch on the head. They are the third largest penguin after the emperor and king penguins, and they live on fish. They are wonderful friends who spend most of their lives at sea.
facts: The gentoo is the third-largest penguin, after the emperor and king penguins.
The white patch on its head is said to resemble a turban.
Its scientific name 'papua' is a misnomer — it refers to New Guinea.

0205.name: Kakapo
0205.desc: Kakapos are very special parrots that live in New Zealand. They cannot fly, but they live a long time and eat seeds. Kakapos are nocturnal, active at night.
facts: The kakapo is a flightless parrot.
It is one of the longest-lived birds in the world.
Every single kakapo alive has its own name.

0206.name: Lesser Frigatebird
0206.desc: Frigatebirds are birds with enormous wings. They live mainly near warm islands and eat fish and squid. In the breeding season the pouch at the throat swells up bright red.
facts: A frigatebird's wingspan reaches 2.5 metres.
Males inflate a bright red throat pouch in the breeding season.
They sometimes snatch food that other birds have caught.

0207.name: Bald Eagle
