0207.desc: Bald eagles are large birds that symbolise the United States. Their heads and tails are white and their bodies brown. They are excellent at catching fish, and they build enormous nests.
facts: The bald eagle is the national bird of the United States.
It builds the largest nests of any bird in North America.
Adults have brown bodies, white head and tail, and bright yellow eyes.

0208.name: Osprey

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · North America · Africa · Oceania

0208.desc: Ospreys are special birds that are very good at catching fish. They live near water, and with wings spread they reach 1.8 metres across. Their heads are white, with a handsome brown band around the eyes.
facts: Ospreys have specially adapted toes for gripping fish.
They live along rivers, lakes and coasts all over the world.
Their wingspan can reach 1.8 metres.

0209.name: Black Kite

info.서식지: Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa · Oceania

0209.desc: Black kites spread their broad wings and glide across the sky. They eat mainly carrion and dead fish, and they live in groups. They live about 24 years, and they look magnificent circling for food.
facts: Black kites eat mainly carrion and dead fish.
They circle high on spread wings while searching for food.
They live in groups, and some populations migrate.

0210.name: Golden Eagle
0210.desc: Golden eagles are large birds that fly very fast. The golden sheen on their heads and necks is their trademark. They eat mostly small animals like rabbits and squirrels, and they build their nests high up.
facts: Golden eagles can reach speeds of 240 km/h in a dive.
They nest mainly on cliffs, in trees, or on utility poles.
They sometimes hunt large mammals and birds.

0211.name: King Penguin

info.서식지: Ocean · Southern Ocean · South America · Oceania · Africa

0211.desc: King penguins are tall, magnificent penguins. They live mainly on islands near Antarctica, eating squid and small fish. They cannot fly, but they swim beautifully underwater!
facts: The king penguin is the second-largest penguin, after the emperor.
It eats mainly squid and krill.
It cannot fly, but it is a superb swimmer.

0212.name: Siberian Pipit
0212.desc: Pipits are small birds that eat mostly insects. In summer their undersides are pink; in winter their streaks darken. They live mainly in grassland and move to warmer places for winter.
facts: Pipits feed mainly on insects.
In summer the underside turns a pale pink.
They winter in southeastern China, Korea and Japan.

0213.name: Secretary Bird
0213.desc: This is a large bird that strides across the grassland on long legs, hunting snakes. It is a very special hunter that catches prey by stamping hard with its feet.
facts: It is called the 'secretary bird' because its head plumes resemble quill pens once tucked behind a clerk's ear.
It stamps on snakes several times a second with powerful feet to stun them.
It is a bird of prey, yet it spends most of its time hunting on foot.

0215.name: Resplendent Quetzal
0215.desc: This is a beautiful bird with shimmering green feathers and a very long tail. It lives in the cloud forests of Central America.
facts: The male's tail streamers are far longer than his body, reaching up to 90 cm.
The ancient Maya and Aztecs considered this bird sacred.
It is the national bird of Guatemala, whose currency is also called the quetzal.

0216.name: Laughing Kookaburra
0216.desc: This is a bird whose call sounds like a person laughing out loud. It calls 'wa-ha-ha' through the Australian bush at dawn and dusk.
facts: Its laughing call announces its group's territory.
Despite the name, it prefers insects and reptiles to fish.
It beats prey hard against a branch before swallowing it.

0221.name: Northern Gannet
0221.desc: This is a large seabird that drops from high in the sky into the sea like an arrow to catch fish. It lives in colonies on the cliffs of the North Atlantic.
facts: It dives into the sea from up to 40 metres at 100 km/h.
Air sacs in its face and chest cushion the impact.
Thousands gather on a single cliff to form an enormous breeding colony.

0222.name: European Robin
0222.desc: This is a small, round bird with an orange breast. It lives in the gardens and woods of Europe, singing a clear song.
facts: Both males and females have the orange breast.
It is famous for singing at night under streetlights.
In Europe it is loved as a symbol of Christmas.

0223.name: European Goldfinch

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa

0223.desc: This is a small bird with a pretty red face and yellow wing bars. It loves thistle seeds and sings in clear, bright notes.
facts: It picks thistle seeds out with its sharply pointed bill.
A flock of goldfinches is called a 'charm'.
It is loved for its bright mix of red, yellow and black.

0224.name: Eurasian Jay
0224.desc: This is a relative of the crow, with a pinkish body and beautiful blue wing patches. It is famous for burying acorns in the ground.
facts: It buries thousands of acorns in the ground to prepare for winter.
Acorns it forgets sprout into trees, helping the forest spread.
It is very good at mimicking the calls of other birds and animals.

0225.name: Superb Lyrebird
0225.desc: This is a bird with an extraordinary talent for copying sounds exactly. The male fans out a magnificent tail shaped like a harp to show off.
facts: It imitates camera shutters, chainsaws and other birds with astonishing accuracy.
The male fans a spectacular tail shaped like a lyre to court females.
It rakes the soil with its feet to find insects among the leaf litter.

0226.name: Rainbow Lorikeet
0226.desc: This is a brilliantly coloured parrot in blue, green and orange. It laps up nectar from flowers with a brush-tipped tongue.
facts: The tip of its tongue is split like a brush, ideal for sweeping up nectar.
It moves noisily through the trees in flocks.
Its whole body is rainbow-coloured, which is how it got its name.

0227.name: Cockatiel
0227.desc: This is a small parrot with a pointed crest on the head and orange cheek patches. It lives in the Australian interior and is a very popular pet.
facts: It raises or lowers the crest on its head according to its mood.
It is the smallest member of the cockatoo family.
Flocks travel across the interior in search of waterholes.

0228.name: Snowy Owl

info.서식지: Polar regions · Grassland · Arctic Ocean · North America · Europe · Asia

0228.desc: This is a large owl with feathers as white as snow. It lives on the cold Arctic tundra and hunts even in daylight.
facts: Unlike most owls, it hunts actively during the day.
It can catch more than ten lemmings in a single day.
Feathers cover even its toes, so it withstands the cold well.

0229.name: Great Blue Heron
0229.desc: This is a large blue-grey bird with a long neck and legs. It stands motionless at the water's edge and spears fish with its pointed bill.
facts: It is the largest heron in North America.
It folds its neck into an S and shoots it out like lightning to seize prey.
In flight it tucks its neck into an S and trails its legs straight behind.

0230.name: African Sacred Ibis
0230.desc: This is a bird with a white body, black head and a downward-curving bill. It was considered sacred in ancient Egypt.
facts: In ancient Egypt it symbolised Thoth, the god of wisdom.
It probes for food in the mud with its long, curved bill.
It was held so sacred that mummified ibises were offered to the gods.

0231.name: Scarlet Ibis
0231.nickname: The scarlet jewel of the wetlands
0231.desc: This is a beautiful bird whose whole body is a dazzling red. It lives in flocks in the wetlands of South America.
facts: Its red feathers come from pigments in the crabs and shrimp it eats.
It is one of the national birds of Trinidad and Tobago.
When hundreds fly together the sky looks stained red.

0233.name: Marabou Stork

info.서식지: Grassland · Rivers & lakes · Near cities · Africa

0233.desc: This is a large stork with a bald head and a big pouch at the throat. It eats dead animals and keeps the grassland clean.
facts: Being bald keeps its feathers clean while it feeds on carrion.
Its wingspan exceeds three metres, among the largest of any bird.
It uses the drooping pink throat pouch to make sounds and to display.

0235.name: California Quail
0235.nickname: The bird with a teardrop plume
0235.desc: This is an endearing small bird with a teardrop-shaped plume curling forward over its head. It scurries through the scrub of the western United States.
facts: The forward-curling teardrop plume on its forehead is its trademark.
It is the state bird of California.
In danger it prefers to run into the brush rather than fly.

0236.name: Golden Pheasant
0236.desc: This is a spectacular pheasant with a golden crest and a blend of red, yellow and blue. It lives in the mountain forests of China.
facts: The male is dazzling, with a golden mane and rainbow-coloured body.
Its long tail makes up more than half its total length.
Despite its showy looks it is timid and stays hidden in the forest.

0237.name: Tawny Frogmouth
0237.nickname: The night bird that plays at being a tree
0237.desc: This is a night-active bird with a wide mouth and bark-like plumage. By day it stays perfectly still, pretending to be a branch.
facts: Its bark-like feathers disguise it so well that it looks like a broken branch.
It is often mistaken for an owl, but it is actually related to nightjars.
At night it opens its wide mouth to catch flying insects.

0238.name: Oilbird
0238.desc: The oilbird is a night bird that lives in flocks deep inside the dark caves of South America. Like a bat, it makes sounds to find its way in the darkness, and at night it flies out to pick ripe fruit.
facts: It is one of the very few birds that uses echolocation to fly inside dark caves.
It is one of the few nocturnal birds that eats only fruit.
The chicks grow plump on very oily fruit, which is how the 'oilbird' got its name.

0239.name: European Bee-eater

info.서식지: Grassland · Rivers & lakes · Europe · Africa · Asia

0239.desc: The European bee-eater is a lovely bird with rainbow-coloured plumage. It catches bees and dragonflies in flight, and digs a burrow in a riverbank to nest.
facts: To avoid being stung, it rubs a captured bee against a branch to remove the sting before eating it.
It is a migratory bird, travelling all the way to Africa for the winter.
It digs a tunnel one to two metres long in a riverbank for its nest.

0240.name: Blue Jay
0240.desc: The blue jay is a clever blue bird found in the woods and parks of North America. It has a talent for mimicking other birds and animals, and it hides acorns in the ground to eat through winter.
facts: It imitates the call of a hawk to startle other birds.
It buries acorns, and the ones it forgets grow into new trees.
It has a crest that it raises or lowers depending on its mood.

0242.name: Sulphur-crested Cockatoo
0242.desc: The sulphur-crested cockatoo is a large parrot with a snow-white body and a yellow crest. It calls very loudly and is clever enough to copy human speech, and it lives in big flocks across Australia.
facts: It fans its yellow crest wide when excited or startled.
It is very intelligent, mimicking speech and even using tools.
One bird in the flock acts as a sentry, watching for danger.

0243.name: Crimson Rosella
0243.desc: The crimson rosella is a beautiful parrot with a scarlet body and blue cheeks and wings. It lives in Australian forests eating seeds and fruit, and it starts life green before turning red as it grows.
facts: Young birds are greenish and gradually turn red as they mature.
The blend of red and blue has earned it the nickname 'living paint'.
It visits gardens and parks readily, so people know it well.

0245.name: Victoria Crowned Pigeon
0245.desc: The Victoria crowned pigeon is a very large pigeon wearing a spectacular blue crest like lace. It is one of the largest pigeons in the world, walking the forest floor of New Guinea and picking up fallen fruit.
facts: It is one of the largest pigeons in the world, about the size of a turkey.
It wears a fan-shaped blue crest that looks like lace.
It walks slowly across the forest floor gathering fallen fruit.

0246.name: Nicobar Pigeon

info.서식지: Rainforest · Asia · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

0246.desc: The Nicobar pigeon is a spectacular pigeon with long, glossy hackles trailing from its neck. It shines green, blue and copper, and it is the closest living relative of the extinct dodo.
facts: It is known as the closest living relative of the extinct dodo.
The hackles on its neck shimmer like a rainbow as the light shifts.
It travels in flocks between small islands.

0248.name: Humboldt Penguin
0248.desc: The Humboldt penguin lives in the warm coastal regions of western South America. It catches fish in the cold Humboldt Current and nests among rocks or in burrows to raise its chicks.
facts: It is named after the cold Humboldt Current where it feeds.
It is said to recognise other individuals by the black band across the chest.
Living somewhere warm, it releases heat through the bare skin on its face to cool down.

0250.name: Yellow-eyed Penguin
0250.desc: The yellow-eyed penguin has yellow eyes and a yellow band around its head. It is a very rare penguin found only in New Zealand, and unlike other penguins it does not form colonies, nesting quietly in the forest instead.
facts: It is one of the rarest penguins in the world, found only in New Zealand.
Unlike other penguins it prefers quiet places where nests are out of sight of one another.
The yellow eyes and the yellow band around the head give it its name.

0252.name: Andean Condor
0252.desc: The Andean condor has a wingspan of over three metres, making it one of the largest flying birds in the world. It glides through the high skies of the South American Andes with barely a wingbeat, searching for dead animals to eat.
facts: With a wingspan over three metres, it is among the largest flying birds.
It rides warm updraughts and can stay aloft for hours with almost no wingbeats.
By clearing away dead animals it acts as nature's cleaner of the skies.

0254.name: African Fish Eagle
0254.desc: The African fish eagle is famous for its white head and ringing call. It flies over rivers and lakes and snatches fish with its talons, and its call carries so far that it is known as the voice of Africa.
facts: Its ringing call carries so far it is called 'the voice of Africa'.
It flies low over the water and seizes fish with sharp talons.
It is a very close relative of America's bald eagle.

0255.name: Harpy Eagle
0255.desc: The harpy eagle lives in the rainforests of the Americas and is one of the most powerful eagles in the world. It has a crest of feathers on its head, and with talons as strong as a bear's claws it hunts monkeys and sloths in the trees.
facts: Its talons are as large as a big bear's claws, letting it snatch a monkey in one strike.
It is one of the most powerful birds of prey in the world.
When startled or focused it raises the feathers on its head like a crown.

0256.name: Red-tailed Hawk
0256.desc: The red-tailed hawk is a very common raptor across North America. Its tail is red, just as the name says, and it circles high in the sky looking for small animals like mice and rabbits on the ground.
facts: The ringing hawk cry heard in films and cartoons is usually this bird's call.
It adapts to many environments and is very common across North America.
Its superb eyesight lets it spot prey from an enormous distance while circling.

0257.name: American Kestrel

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · North America · South America

0257.desc: The American kestrel is the smallest falcon in the Americas, about the size of a pigeon. Its plumage is prettily patterned, and it hovers in mid-air on beating wings before dropping on insects and mice.
facts: It is the smallest falcon in the Americas, about pigeon-sized.
It beats its wings rapidly to hover in one spot while watching for prey.
It is said to spot the ultraviolet traces of mouse urine to find the trails prey use.

0258.name: Eurasian Sparrowhawk

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa

0258.desc: The sparrowhawk is a small, quick hawk that lives in woodland and near villages. It flies swiftly between the trees hunting small birds, and the male is far smaller than the female.
facts: It weaves rapidly between trees in pursuit of small birds.
The male is considerably smaller than the female.
In Korea it is protected as a natural monument, seen as both a winter visitor and a resident.

0259.name: Burrowing Owl

info.서식지: Grassland · Desert · North America · South America

0259.desc: The burrowing owl is an unusual owl that lives in a hole in the ground rather than a tree. It has long legs and is active by day, and it borrows burrows dug by other animals — or digs its own — to nest inside.
facts: Unlike most owls it lives in a burrow in the ground rather than a tree.
Its long legs let it dash about on the ground catching insects.
When danger approaches it can imitate a rattlesnake to drive enemies away.

0260.name: Barn Owl

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa · North America · South America · Oceania

0260.desc: The barn owl is an owl with a white, heart-shaped face. It flies silently at night and can pinpoint a mouse from the faintest sound, and it lives all over the world.
facts: Its heart-shaped face acts as a dish, funnelling sound to its ears.
Its soft feathers make its flight almost completely silent.
Living on every continent except Antarctica, it is one of the most widespread birds in the world.

0261.name: Great Grey Owl

info.서식지: Polar regions · North America · Europe · Asia

0261.nickname: Great ghost of the northern forest
0261.desc: The great grey owl is a very large owl with grey plumage and an enormous face. It looks bulky, but most of that is thick feathering, so it is light. It can hear a mouse hidden under the snow and punch straight through to catch it.
facts: It is the longest of all owls, though most of that is feathers, so it is lighter than it looks.
It can hear a mouse beneath more than 50 cm of snow and plunge through to catch it.
Its huge facial disc funnels even the faintest sounds to its ears.

0262.name: Northern Cardinal
0262.desc: The northern cardinal is a beautiful bird, entirely scarlet with a pointed crest. Males are a striking red while females are a soft brown, and it is famous for its clear, lovely song.
facts: The male is bright scarlet while the female is an inconspicuous soft brown.
Both males and females sing beautifully, answering one another.
It is so loved in the United States that several states have made it their state bird.

0263.name: Eastern Bluebird
0263.desc: This is a small, endearing bird with a blue back and an orange breast. It nests in tree holes or nest boxes and eats insects. Americans are very fond of it.
facts: It readily moves into nest boxes, so people love to watch it.
It sometimes raises two or three broods in a single year.
Several US states have made it their state bird.

0264.name: American Robin
0264.desc: This is a common bird with an orange belly and a grey-brown back. You often see it hopping across lawns hunting earthworms. When spring comes it sings beautifully in the morning.
facts: It walks across lawns, cocking its head as it searches for earthworms.
It is one of the first birds to sing in spring.
Its eggs are a lovely sky blue.

0265.name: Red Warbler
0265.desc: This is a tiny bird, entirely scarlet with a white spot on each cheek. It lives only in the high pine forests of Mexico. It works busily through the branches catching insects.
facts: It is found only in the highlands of Mexico.
Its all-red plumage makes it stand out sharply in the forest.
The white cheek spot is its trademark.

0267.name: Sword-billed Hummingbird
0267.nickname: The bill longer than its body
0267.desc: This is a remarkable hummingbird whose bill is longer than its own body. It is the only bird in the world with a bill longer than its body. That long bill lets it reach nectar deep inside flowers.
facts: It is the only bird whose bill is longer than its body.
Its bill is so long it has to use its feet to scratch and preen.
It lives in partnership with flowers that have long tubular blooms.

0268.name: American Goldfinch
0268.desc: This is a small bird that turns bright yellow all over in summer. It loves seeds and cracks them expertly with its bill. It flies in a bouncing, up-and-down pattern.
facts: It nests late, in high summer, when seeds are plentiful.
It is one of the rare birds that eats almost nothing but seeds.
Its plumage changes colour dramatically with the seasons.

0269.name: House Finch
0269.nickname: The red singer of the city
0269.desc: This is a small bird whose male has a head and breast that look painted red. It is very common around towns and cities where people live. It chirps cheerfully all day long.
facts: The male's red colour comes from its diet, so it deepens with what he eats.
It originally lived in the west but has spread widely across the east.
It will nest under building eaves and even in flowerpots.

0270.name: Eurasian Nuthatch
0270.nickname: The bird that walks down head-first
0270.desc: This is a remarkable bird that can walk head-first down a tree trunk. With its sturdy bill it picks insects out from under the bark. It wedges acorns into crevices and hammers them open.
facts: It is one of the few birds that can climb down a tree head-first.
If the nest hole is too big it plasters the entrance with mud to shrink it.
It hides food in crevices to prepare for winter.

0271.name: Eurasian Treecreeper
0271.desc: This is a small brown bird that spirals its way up a tree trunk. Its colour matches the bark so well that it is hard to spot. With a fine curved bill it pulls insects out of the bark.
facts: It spirals around the trunk, always climbing upward.
Its colouring matches tree bark exactly, so it hides very well.
It braces itself against the trunk with its stiff tail.

0272.name: Red-rumped Swallow

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Africa · Europe

0272.desc: This is a swallow that looks as if it is wearing a red belt around its waist. It flies quickly through the sky, catching flying insects with its mouth open. It builds a nest of mud shaped like a jar.
facts: It builds a jar-shaped mud nest with a long entrance tunnel.
It is a migratory bird that travels great distances.
The red band across the rump distinguishes it from the common swallow.

0273.name: Common Swift
0273.nickname: The bird that never leaves the sky
0273.desc: This is an astonishing bird that spends almost its entire life in the air. It eats, drinks and even sleeps on the wing. Its legs are so short it can barely land on the ground, but in the air it is supreme.
facts: It can fly for months without ever touching the ground.
It takes brief naps while still flying.
It is among the fastest flying birds of all.

0274.name: Grey Nightjar
0274.nickname: Champion of hide-and-seek in the leaves
0274.desc: This is a night bird that presses itself against a branch or the ground by day, hidden perfectly among the fallen leaves. After sunset it flies with its wide mouth open, catching moths.
facts: Its plumage matches dead leaves so exactly that it is nearly invisible by day.
At night it opens its wide mouth to catch insects.
It builds no nest, laying its eggs directly on the ground.

0275.name: Great Bustard
0275.desc: This is a large bird, among the heaviest that can still fly. It strides across the wide grassland. The male is spectacular in the breeding season, puffing up his feathers until he looks like a great white flower.
facts: It is among the heaviest birds still capable of flight.
In the breeding season the male puffs his whole body up until he turns white.
As its habitat shrinks it has become a globally protected bird.

0276.name: White-naped Crane
0276.nickname: The crane in a grey coat
0276.desc: This is an elegant crane with a white nape and a grey body. It has red patches around the eyes. In winter it is a precious visitor to the riverside wetlands of Korea.
facts: It is a rare migratory bird that visits Korea in winter.
Bare red skin shows around its eyes.
It travels together in family groups.

0277.name: Common Crane

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Grassland · Europe · Asia · Africa

0277.desc: This is a crane with a black neck and head and a red patch on the crown. It flies in V-shaped flocks and travels very long distances. It calls with a ringing, trumpet-like sound.
facts: Thousands sometimes travel together in a single flock.
Paired birds leap and sing together as though dancing.
It has a red patch on the crown of its head.

0278.name: Grey Crowned Crane
0278.desc: This is a beautiful crane with stiff golden feathers rising from its head like a crown. It lives on the African grasslands. White and red markings on its cheeks make it very striking.
facts: The golden feather crown on its head is its most distinctive feature.
It stamps the ground with its feet to startle insects into moving, then catches them.
It is the national bird of Uganda and appears on the country's flag.

0280.name: Black-winged Stilt

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Ocean · Asia · Europe · Africa

0280.desc: This is an elegant bird with extremely long pink legs for its size. It strides through shallow water on those long legs looking for food. Its white body contrasts crisply with black wings.
facts: It has among the longest legs of any bird relative to its body size.
It strides through shallow water catching food.
It calls loudly to drive away anything that comes near its nest.

0281.name: Pied Avocet

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Europe · Asia · Africa

0281.desc: This is a distinctive bird whose bill curves gently upward. It is a neat-looking bird in white and black. It sweeps its bill from side to side through shallow water to filter out food.
facts: Its upturned bill is ideal for sweeping through water.
It filters food by swinging its bill from side to side.
It nests in noisy colonies that defend the site together.

0282.name: Northern Lapwing
0282.nickname: The bird with a horn crest
0282.desc: This is a bird with a fine, long feather rising from the back of its head like a horn. Its back shines green. In flight it flaps its broad wings in a tumbling, acrobatic style.
facts: The long crest rising from the back of the head is its trademark.
When an enemy approaches it feigns injury to lure them away from the nest.
It flaps its rounded wings in an acrobatic, tumbling flight.

0284.name: Black-headed Gull

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa

0284.desc: This is a small gull with a red bill and legs whose head turns chocolate-coloured in summer. It lives in flocks along rivers and coasts. In winter its head turns white again.
facts: In summer its head turns chocolate brown.
It lives in noisy flocks, calling together.
It is good at finding food close to people.

0285.name: Common Tern

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Europe · Asia · North America · Africa

0285.desc: This is a slim seabird with a forked tail like a swallow's. When it spots a fish it dives from the sky into the water like an arrow to catch it. Its pointed wings and quick movements are magnificent.
facts: It plunges from the sky into the water to catch fish.
Its tail is deeply forked like a swallow's.
It is a migratory bird that travels very long distances.

0287.name: Great Cormorant

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Europe · Asia · Africa · Oceania · North America

0287.desc: This is a black water bird that dives underwater and chases fish. When it finishes swimming it spreads its wings wide to dry its wet feathers. It swims across the surface with its neck stretched long.
facts: It dives underwater and pursues fish to catch them.
Its feathers get wet, so it spreads its wings to dry them in the sun.
In old China and Japan people used this bird to catch fish.

0288.name: Great Crested Grebe

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · Africa · Oceania

0288.desc: This is a water bird with horn-like feather tufts on its head. It slips underwater to catch fish. In the breeding season it is famous for dancing face to face with its mate.
facts: It carries its chicks on its back while swimming.
In courtship a pair face each other holding waterweed and dance.
In danger it dives underwater to escape rather than flying away.

0289.name: Common Loon

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Ocean · North America · Europe

0289.desc: This is a large water bird with a sharply pointed bill that is excellent at catching fish underwater. Its call is haunting and carries a long way. Its legs sit far back on its body, so it swims well but walks poorly on land.
facts: Its heavy bones make diving easy.
It sometimes dives more than 60 metres deep.
With its legs set far back it walks awkwardly on land.

0290.name: Common Shelduck

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · Africa

0290.desc: This is a duck with a white body, a dark greenish-black head and a chestnut band across the breast. The male has a red knob above the bill. It filters small creatures from tidal mud.
facts: The male's red knob above the bill swells in the breeding season.
It sometimes nests in rabbit burrows or tree holes.
Several families may pool their ducklings and raise them together.

0291.name: Mallard

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · North America · Africa

0291.desc: This is the duck we see most often of all. The male has a glossy green head and the female is brown. It is the ancestor of the domestic duck, and it eats waterweed, seeds and insects alike.
facts: It is the ancestor of most domestic ducks.
It feeds by tipping head-down into the water with its tail in the air.
The male's glossy green head shifts colour depending on the light.

0292.name: Northern Pintail

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · North America · Africa

0292.desc: This is a duck with a long neck and a tail as long and fine as a needle. The male is handsome, with a chocolate head and a white neck. It is a migratory bird that travels very long distances.
facts: The male's needle-like tail is what gives the pintail its name.
It makes very long migrations that span much of the globe.
Its long neck lets it reach food deeper below the surface.

0293.name: Northern Shoveler

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · North America · Africa

0293.desc: This duck is easy to recognise by its broad, spoon-shaped bill. It uses that wide bill to strain small creatures from the water. The male has a green head and a white breast.
facts: It strains the water for food with its broad, spoon-like bill.
Comb-like ridges inside the bill filter out tiny food items.
It swims in circles to stir the water and bring food to the surface.

0294.name: Common Goldeneye

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Ocean · Asia · Europe · North America

0294.desc: It is called the goldeneye because its eyes are a golden yellow. The male has a black head with a round white spot on the cheek. It dives underwater to catch shellfish and insects.
facts: Its golden eyes are what give the goldeneye its name.
It nests in tree holes, and the ducklings leap out as soon as they hatch.
It dives to catch shellfish and insects on the bottom.

0295.name: Mute Swan

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · North America

0295.desc: This is a large bird, pure white all over with a long, elegant neck. It has a black knob above its orange bill. It looks beautiful gliding across the water.
facts: It is one of the heaviest flying birds.
The black knob above the bill distinguishes it from other swans.
Its long neck lets it graze on plants deep below the surface.

0298.name: Black-capped Kingfisher
0298.desc: This is a beautiful kingfisher with a black head, glossy blue back and wings, and a bright red bill. It waits by the water and snatches up fish and frogs in an instant.
facts: The red bill, black head and blue wings make it very striking.
It watches from the waterside and seizes prey in an instant.
It digs a burrow into an earth bank to nest.

0299.name: Grey-headed Woodpecker
0299.desc: This is a woodpecker with a green back and a grey head. It taps at trees with its bill to catch the insects living inside. It is especially fond of ants.
facts: It extends its long tongue to hook ants out from inside the wood.
It drums on trees with its bill to announce its territory.
The male has a red spot on the forehead.

0300.name: Goldcrest
0300.desc: This is one of the smallest birds in Europe. It has a yellow and orange stripe on the crown of its head. It is so tiny that it darts quickly between branches catching small insects.
facts: It is one of the smallest birds in Europe.
It raises the yellow stripe on its crown to show how it feels.
Being so tiny, it huddles together in groups to survive the cold.

0301.name: Green Sea Turtle

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0301.desc: This is a large turtle that lives in warm seas. It swims through the water with long flippers and mainly eats seaweed. It returns to the very beach where it was born to lay its eggs. It also has the special ability to live for a very long time.
facts: By grazing on seaweed, it helps keep the seaweed beds healthy.
Female turtles come ashore at night to lay their eggs.
It returns to the beach where it hatched to lay its own eggs.

0302.name: Japanese Gecko
0302.desc: The Japanese gecko looks like a small lizard. It is mostly active at night, hunting insects and spiders. Its tail can easily fall off, but it soon grows back.
facts: Its tail detaches easily but regrows quickly.
Its body is covered in small bump-like scales.
It is mostly nocturnal and can make small chirping sounds.

0303.name: Crocodile

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Africa · Asia · North America · South America · Oceania

0303.desc: The crocodile is a large reptile that lives in water. With powerful jaws and a long tail, it can move quickly through the water. It lays eggs and buries them in the ground to protect its young.
facts: Its powerful jaws can clamp shut and hold with tremendous force.
Its thick, scaly skin acts like a suit of armour.
Its muscular tail lets it move swiftly through the water.

0305.name: Iguana
0305.desc: The iguana is a lizard friend that is usually green in colour. It lives up in trees in the forest and can defend itself with its long tail. It is a clever animal that basks in the sun to control its body temperature.
facts: Its green colour helps it hide well among the leaves of the forest.
It uses its long tail to defend itself.
It mostly lives in trees and is also a strong swimmer.

0308.name: Green Sea Turtle

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0308.desc: The green sea turtle is a large turtle that lives in the ocean. It mainly eats seaweed and can swim incredibly long distances. Females come ashore to dig nests and lay their eggs. Green sea turtles can live for a very long time!
facts: It mainly eats seaweed, nibbling off the tips to keep the seaweed beds healthy.
It travels long distances between its feeding grounds and the beaches where it hatched.
Females come ashore at night to dig a nest and lay their eggs.

0310.name: Cobra
0310.desc: The cobra is a snake with a long body and venom. When it feels threatened, it spreads its neck into a wide hood. Cobras mainly eat rats, frogs, birds and other snakes.
facts: When threatened, it spreads its neck to form a hood.
It has a powerful neurotoxic venom.
It is preyed on by mongooses and snake eagles.

0312.name: Green Anaconda
0312.desc: The anaconda is a huge snake that mainly lives in the rivers and forests of South America. It preys on animals such as fish and birds. Anacondas look frightening, but they rarely attack people.
facts: The green anaconda is known as the heaviest snake in the world.
Female anacondas are much larger than males.
It is well adapted to water and swims very well.

0313.name: Boa Constrictor
0313.desc: The boa constrictor is a large snake that mainly lives in South America. It has a wide variety of patterns and colours. It is also a strong swimmer.
facts: It comes in a huge variety of colours and patterns.
It is an excellent swimmer.
It is mostly active at night and likes to bask in the sun during the day.

0314.name: King Cobra
0314.desc: The king cobra is a truly massive snake that can grow up to 4 metres long. Its venom makes it frightening to other animals, but it doesn't bother people. It mostly lives in forests and hunts other snakes.
facts: It is the longest venomous snake in the world.
It is the only snake known to build a nest to guard its eggs.
It mainly hunts other snakes.

0315.name: Common Basilisk
0315.nickname: The lizard that runs on water
0315.desc: The basilisk is a remarkable lizard that can run across the surface of water! It has a green body and lives near forests in Costa Rica. It eats fruit and small animals.
facts: It can run across water, earning it the nickname 'Jesus lizard'.
It can also stay underwater for a long time.
It runs across the water so fast that the motion can only be seen clearly with a high-speed camera.

0316.name: Boa Constrictor
0316.desc: The boa is a very large snake. It mostly lives alone and is active mainly at night. Its colours and patterns are so varied that many people love keeping it.
facts: It comes in an enormous range of colours and patterns.
It is mostly active at night, though it sometimes basks in the sun during the day.
It is an excellent swimmer and is often found near rivers.

0317.name: Alligator Snapping Turtle
0317.desc: The alligator snapping turtle is a large turtle that lives in rivers and lakes. It uses an unusual tongue to lure fish. With its heavy shell and huge head, it looks like a small dinosaur.
facts: It is the largest freshwater turtle species in North America.
Its long, thick shell and large scales make it look like an armoured dinosaur.
It has a uniquely shaped tongue that it uses to lure fish.

0318.name: Desert Tortoise
0318.desc: The desert tortoise is a special tortoise that lives in the hot desert. It digs its own burrow and rests in the shade. It can live for a very long time!
facts: It can survive in the desert, where there is almost no water.
It is an excellent digger, building burrows to escape the sun.
It can live for up to 80 years.

0319.name: Leopard Gecko
0319.desc: This adorable gecko is covered in black spots like a leopard's. Unlike most geckos, it has eyelids that let it blink. When in danger, it can drop its plump tail to escape. It wakes at night to hunt insects.
facts: Its tail stores fat, which is why it looks plump.
It has eyelids, so it can blink its eyes.
Its dropped tail grows back over time.

0320.name: Bearded Dragon
0320.desc: When it feels angry or startled, it puffs out the skin under its chin and turns it black, forming what looks like a beard. It has a gentle temperament, which is why many people keep it as a pet. It loves basking on warm rocks in the sun. It even waves its front leg in a circle as a kind of greeting.
facts: It puffs out its throat into a black, beard-like pouch.
It waves a front leg in a circular motion as a greeting.
It changes its body colour to help regulate its temperature.

0321.name: Gecko
0321.desc: Tiny hairs cover the soles of its feet, letting it climb straight up glass walls. It is active at night and its large eyes see well in the dark. Its name comes from the 'gecko-gecko' sound it makes. It is a keen hunter that loves catching insects.
facts: The tiny hairs on its feet let it climb up glass walls.
It has no eyelids, so it licks its eyes clean with its tongue.
Its name comes from the 'gecko-gecko' sound it makes.

0322.name: Green Anole
0322.desc: It can change its body colour between green and brown, which is why it is nicknamed the 'American chameleon'. Males show off by unfurling a pink fan of skin under their throat. It moves quickly through trees and bushes. It survives by catching small insects.
facts: It can change its body colour between green and brown.
Males show off with a pink throat fan.
It is nicknamed the 'American chameleon'.

0324.name: Gila Monster
0324.desc: Its body is covered in colourful, bead-like scales. It is one of the few lizards in the world that carries venom. It moves very slowly and never seems to be in a hurry. It stores fat in its tail, letting it go without food for months.
facts: It is one of the very few venomous lizards.
Bead-like scales cover its entire body.
It stores fat in its tail, letting it survive long periods without eating.

0325.name: Nile Monitor
0325.desc: This is a very large lizard that lives along Africa's Nile River. It is a strong swimmer and hunts even in the water. It uses its long, forked tongue to smell out prey. It especially likes eggs, fish and crabs.
facts: It is a strong swimmer and hunts in the water.
It uses its forked tongue to smell out prey.
It is the longest lizard species in Africa.

0330.name: Lizard
0330.desc: This is a common sight in grassy fields and stone walls near us. When in danger, it drops its own tail and escapes. The lost tail grows back over time. It basks in the warm sun and hunts insects.
facts: It drops its own tail to escape danger.
Its dropped tail grows back over time.
It basks in the sun to warm its body.

0332.name: Tiger Keelback
0332.desc: Its body is covered in colourful red, green and black patterns, which is why it's called a 'flower snake'. It has a special pouch at the back of its neck for storing venom. It loves living near water and hunting frogs. When startled, it flattens its neck.
facts: Its colourful pattern earns it the nickname 'flower snake'.
It has a venom-storing pouch at the back of its neck.
It collects toxins from toads and uses them as its own venom.

0333.name: Short-tailed Pit Viper
0333.desc: This is a venomous snake found in Korea. It has a special heat-sensing pit between its eyes and nose. This pit lets it find warm prey even in total darkness. It has a short, plump body and a triangular head.
facts: A pit between its eyes and nose senses heat.
Its head is triangular in shape.
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.

0334.name: Python

info.서식지: Rainforest · Rivers & lakes · Asia · Africa · Oceania

0334.desc: The python is one of the largest snakes in the world. Instead of venom, it squeezes its prey tightly with its body. It lays eggs, and the mother coils around them to keep them warm and protected.
facts: It squeezes and constricts its prey with its body.
It has no venom.
It guards and incubates its eggs.

0335.name: King Cobra
0335.desc: The king cobra is the longest venomous snake in the world. When angered, it spreads its neck wide like an umbrella to look bigger. It is a fearsome snake that hunts other snakes.
facts: It is the longest venomous snake in the world.
It hunts other snakes.
It is the only snake known to build a nest.

0336.name: Sea Snake

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean · Asia

0336.nickname: The venomous snake of the sea
0336.desc: The sea snake is a snake that lives and swims in the ocean. Its flat, oar-like tail makes it an excellent swimmer. It has strong venom, but it rarely bites people.
facts: Its tail is flat like an oar.
It has strong venom.
It can hold its breath and dive for long periods.

0337.name: Green Tree Python
0337.nickname: The green snake of the treetops
0337.desc: The green tree python is a snake with a beautiful green body. It rests by coiling its body around tree branches. As babies, they are yellow or red, and turn green as they grow.
facts: It rests coiled around tree branches.
Its colour is different when it's a baby.
It has no venom.

0338.name: Corn Snake
0338.desc: The corn snake is a gentle snake with a beautiful pattern on its body. It has no venom and a calm temperament, which people love. It helps farmers by hunting rats.
facts: It has no venom and a gentle temperament.
It helps farmers by hunting rats.
Its pattern resembles corn kernels.

0342.name: Leatherback Sea Turtle

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

0342.desc: The leatherback is the largest sea turtle in the world. Its shell isn't hard but soft like leather. It can dive to very deep depths and loves eating jellyfish.
facts: It is the biggest sea turtle in the world.
Its shell is soft, like leather.
It can dive to very great depths.

0344.name: Reeves's Turtle
0344.desc: The Reeves's turtle is a native turtle of Korea. Its shell has three raised ridges running along it. It lives in ponds and rivers and has long been regarded as an animal that brings good fortune.
facts: It is a turtle native to Korea.
It has three raised ridges on its shell.
It is a natural monument.

0345.name: Snapping Turtle
0345.desc: The snapping turtle has an extremely powerful bite. When angered, it stretches out its long neck and bites hard. It waits quietly for prey at the bottom of the water.
facts: Its bite is extremely powerful.
It can stretch its neck out a long way.
It waits at the bottom of the water for prey.

0346.name: Box Turtle
0346.nickname: The turtle that shuts its door
0346.desc: The box turtle shuts its shell tight like a door when in danger. It pulls in its head and legs and closes up like a box to hide. It lives on land and can live for a very long time.
facts: It closes its shell like a box.
It lives on land.
It can live for a very long time.

0347.name: American Alligator
0347.nickname: The great alligator of the swamp
0347.desc: The American alligator is a large alligator that lives in swamps. It catches prey with its broad snout and powerful teeth. It's a caring alligator — the mother guards her eggs and even carries her babies on her back.
facts: Its snout is broad and flat.
The mother guards her eggs.
She carries her babies on her back.

0348.name: Caiman
0348.desc: The caiman is one of the smaller members of the crocodile family. It lives in warm rivers and swamps and hunts fish. It's also called the spectacled caiman because of the bony ridge above its eyes that looks like glasses.
facts: It is one of the smaller crocodilians.
It has a bony ridge above its eyes that looks like spectacles.
The mother guards her eggs.

0349.name: Gharial
0349.desc: The gharial is a special crocodilian with an extremely thin, long snout. It uses this narrow snout to snatch fish quickly underwater. It lives in India's great rivers, and its numbers are now so low that it is under protection.
facts: Males have a pot-like bump at the tip of their snout.
Its legs are weak, so it can barely walk on land and mostly crawls.
It has over 100 teeth, so slippery fish rarely escape.

0351.name: Galapagos Giant Tortoise
0351.nickname: The giant tortoise of the islands
0351.desc: It is one of the largest tortoises on Earth, and some have a saddle-shaped shell. It lives only on the Galapagos Islands. It moves very slowly but can live for more than 100 years.
facts: It can live for more than 100 years.
It can survive for months without eating or drinking.
Its shell shape varies slightly from island to island.

0352.name: Aldabra Giant Tortoise
0352.nickname: The giant of the Indian Ocean
0352.desc: This is a very large tortoise living on Aldabra Atoll in the Indian Ocean. Along with the Galapagos tortoise, it's among the largest tortoises in the world. Its long neck lets it reach leaves on high branches.
facts: It is one of the largest tortoises in the world.
Its long neck lets it stand and reach leaves on high branches.
It plays an important role in spreading plant seeds across the island.

0353.name: Radiated Tortoise
0353.desc: Its shell has yellow lines radiating outward like a star, making it a truly beautiful tortoise. It lives only in Madagascar. Sadly, it is under severe threat from poaching.
facts: The yellow markings on its shell radiate outward like starlight.
It is seriously endangered because of poaching.
It is one of the longest-living tortoises.

0354.name: Gopher Tortoise
0354.desc: Its sturdy, shovel-like front feet let it dig long burrows underground. Many other animals share these burrows too, earning it the nickname 'landlord tortoise'. It lives in the southeastern United States.
facts: It can dig burrows over 10 metres long with its front feet.
More than 300 other species share its burrows.
This makes it a vitally important 'landlord' species for its ecosystem.

0355.name: Leopard Tortoise
0355.desc: Its shell has black spots and patterns like a leopard's, which is how it got its name. It is a large tortoise that lives on the grasslands of Africa. Its shell rises up high, giving it a rounded shape.
facts: The leopard-like spots on its shell give it its name.
It can go a long time without water.
Unlike most other tortoises, it can swim.

0356.name: African Spurred Tortoise
0356.desc: It lives at the edge of Africa's deserts and is the third-largest tortoise species on land. It has pointed spurs on its hind legs. It digs burrows in the ground to escape the heat.
facts: It is the third-largest tortoise species on land.
It digs deep burrows to escape the heat.
It has spur-like points on its hind legs.

0357.name: Greek Tortoise
0357.nickname: The small tortoise with engraved patterns
0357.desc: Its shell has a colourful pattern that looks almost carved, which is how it got its name. It is a small tortoise that lives around the Mediterranean. It has a pair of horn-like spurs on its hind legs.
facts: Its shell pattern resembles ancient Greek decoration, hence its name.
It has a pair of horn-like spurs on its hind legs.
Though small, it can live for nearly 100 years.

0358.name: Hermann's Tortoise
0358.desc: This is a small, adorable tortoise living in southern Europe. Its shell is a colourful pattern of yellow and black. It has a small horn-like spur at the tip of its tail. It's also popular as a pet.
facts: It has a small horn-like spur at the tip of its tail.
Though small, it can live for decades.
It wakes from hibernation when spring arrives.

0359.name: Indian Star Tortoise
0359.desc: Each section of its shell bursts with a beautiful yellow star pattern. It lives in India and Sri Lanka. Its shell rises up in bumpy, dome-like sections.
facts: Yellow star-shaped patterns spread across each section of its shell.
Its shell rises in bumpy, dome-like sections.
Its beautiful pattern makes it a target for poachers.

0360.name: Ploughshare Tortoise
0360.nickname: The rarest tortoise in the world
0360.desc: The underside of its shell juts forward like a plough blade, which is how it got its name. It lives only in Madagascar and is one of the rarest tortoises in the world, critically endangered.
facts: The underside of its shell juts forward like a plough blade.
It is one of the rarest tortoises on Earth.
Only a few hundred remain in the wild because of poaching.

0361.name: Pancake Tortoise
0361.desc: Its shell is flat and soft like a pancake, making it a truly unusual tortoise. This thin body lets it squeeze into crevices between rocks. Unlike other tortoises, it can climb rocks quickly.
facts: Its shell is flat and slightly soft, like a pancake.
When in danger, it wedges itself into a rock crevice and puffs up so it can't be pulled out.
It climbs rocks far faster than other tortoises.

0362.name: Red-footed Tortoise
0362.desc: Its legs have red and orange scales, which is how it got its name. It lives in the forests and grasslands of South America. It's an omnivore that eats fruit and small insects as well as plants.
facts: Its red and orange leg scales give it the name 'red-footed'.
Unlike most tortoises, it also eats fruit and insects.
Males make a clucking sound to call for a mate.

0363.name: Yellow-spotted River Turtle
0363.desc: This is a large freshwater turtle living in the rivers of South America. As a baby, it has yellow spots on its head, which is how it got its name. It folds its neck sideways to tuck it into its shell.
facts: It's a 'side-necked' turtle that folds its neck sideways into its shell.
Females are much larger than males.
It lays its eggs on sandy riverbanks.

0364.name: Mata Mata
0364.nickname: The fallen leaf of the water
0364.desc: This is a very strange-looking turtle from South America. Its body looks like a leaf or bark, letting it hide underwater. When a fish comes close, it opens its mouth wide and sucks it in along with the water.
facts: It disguises itself as a fallen leaf while waiting for prey.
It swallows its food whole without chewing.
It pokes its long snout above the water to breathe.

0365.name: Pig-nosed Turtle
0365.desc: This unusual freshwater turtle has a nose shaped like a pig's snout. Unlike other freshwater turtles, it has paddle-like flippers just like a sea turtle. It lives in New Guinea and northern Australia.
facts: It uses its pig-like nose to breathe while underwater.
Though a freshwater turtle, it swims with flippers.
It is the only surviving species in its family.

0366.name: Green Sea Turtle

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0366.nickname: The plant-eating traveller of the sea
0366.desc: This large sea turtle swims through warm oceans. As an adult, it eats sea grass and algae, which turns its body fat green. When it comes to lay eggs, it returns to the beach where it was born.
facts: It's one of the few sea turtles that eats mainly plants as an adult.
It swims thousands of kilometres back to the beach where it hatched to lay its eggs.
Its name comes from the green colour of its fat.

0367.name: Hawksbill Sea Turtle

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0367.desc: This sea turtle has a beak as sharp and pointed as a hawk's. It uses that beak to pick sponges out from crevices in coral reefs. Its beautiful shell has led people to hunt it heavily, leaving it critically endangered.
facts: It uses its pointed beak to pick prey from crevices in coral reefs.
Its beautiful shell pattern has made it critically endangered.
It can even eat venomous jellyfish.

0368.name: Loggerhead Sea Turtle

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0368.desc: This sea turtle has a big head and an extremely powerful bite. It can crack open hard clams and crab shells with its powerful jaws. It swims across wide oceans for a long lifetime.
facts: Its powerful jaws can crack open hard clams.
Its large head is why it's called the 'loggerhead'.
It travels thousands of kilometres across the open ocean.

0369.name: Olive Ridley Sea Turtle

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0369.nickname: The turtle that nests in crowds
0369.desc: This is one of the smaller sea turtle species. Its shell is olive-coloured, which is how it got its name. When it's time to lay eggs, huge numbers of females gather on the same beach all at once.
facts: Thousands gather on the same beach on the same day to lay eggs, an event called an 'arribada'.
It is one of the smaller sea turtle species.
Its shell is olive-coloured.

0370.name: Painted Turtle
0370.desc: This is a beautiful freshwater turtle common in North America. Its shell edges and legs have painting-like red and yellow stripes. When the sun is out, it loves climbing onto logs to bask.
facts: It has painting-like red and yellow stripes on its body.
It loves basking on logs.
In winter, it hibernates in the mud at the bottom of ponds.

0371.name: Common Musk Turtle
0371.desc: This is a very small freshwater turtle found in North America. When it feels threatened, it releases a foul musky smell to drive enemies away. This is why it's nicknamed the 'stinkpot'.
facts: When threatened, it releases a foul musky smell.
It likes to walk along the bottom of the water.
It is a very small turtle.

0372.name: Diamondback Terrapin
0372.desc: This turtle has diamond-shaped patterns on its shell. It lives in tidal marshes and wetlands where seawater and freshwater mix. It's a special freshwater turtle that can survive in salt water.
facts: Its shell has diamond-shaped patterns.
It lives in tidal marshes where salt water and fresh water mix.
It loves eating crabs.

0373.name: Chinese Softshell Turtle
0373.desc: This is a softshell turtle whose shell isn't hard, but soft like leather. Its nose is long like a tube, letting it breathe by poking just the tip above the water. It usually hides its body in sand or mud.
facts: Its shell is soft, like leather.
It uses its long nose to breathe underwater.
It hides in the mud, waiting for prey.

0375.name: Nile Crocodile
0375.desc: This is a very large and powerful crocodile living in Africa. It's so fearsome it can even hunt big animals that come to the riverbank to drink. The mother carries her babies to the water in her mouth, caring for them tenderly.
facts: It is one of the largest crocodiles in Africa.
The mother carries her babies in her mouth to the water.
It lies hidden in the water before ambushing prey.

0376.name: Saltwater Crocodile

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Asia · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

0376.nickname: The largest crocodile in the world
0376.desc: This is the largest living reptile of any kind. It can survive in salt water, letting it swim across the sea to reach distant islands. Its bite is the strongest of any animal.
facts: It is the largest living reptile.
It can survive in salt water.
Its bite is the strongest of any animal.

0377.name: American Alligator
0377.desc: The American alligator lives in the swamps and wetlands of the southeastern United States. Its snout is wider and rounder than a crocodile's. It once nearly disappeared, but thanks to conservation efforts, its numbers have grown again.
facts: Its snout is broad and rounded, shaped like a U.
It is a conservation success story, having recovered from near extinction.
In cold winters, it digs a burrow to rest in.

0378.name: Chinese Alligator
0378.desc: The Chinese alligator is a small alligator living in China's Yangtze River. It is related to the American alligator but is much smaller. So few remain that it's one of the most endangered alligators in the world.
facts: It is one of the smallest alligators in the world.
Very few remain in the wild.
It hibernates in a burrow during cold winters.

0379.name: Spectacled Caiman
0379.desc: It has a bony ridge between its eyes that looks like eyeglasses, which is how it got its name. It's very common in the rivers and swamps of Central and South America. It's a relatively small crocodilian.
facts: It has a bony ridge between its eyes that looks like spectacles.
It's a very common crocodilian in Central and South America.
When water dries up, it rests in the mud to survive.

0380.name: Black Caiman
0380.nickname: The black hunter of the Amazon
0380.desc: This is the largest caiman living in the Amazon River. Its black body helps it hunt well at night. It's the biggest of all the alligator family members.
facts: It is the largest caiman in the Amazon.
Its black body helps it hunt well at night.
It even preys on fish such as piranhas.

0381.name: Gharial
0381.desc: This unusual crocodilian has an extremely long, thin snout. It uses that narrow mouth to snatch fish quickly. It lives in India's rivers, and very few remain.
facts: Its snout is as thin and long as chopsticks.
It uses its narrow mouth to snatch fish quickly.
Males have a pot-shaped bump on the tip of their snout.

0382.name: American Crocodile

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Ocean · North America · South America

0382.nickname: The great crocodile of the Americas
0382.desc: This is a large crocodile living in Central America and northern South America. Its snout is pointed and narrow. It thrives in river mouths and coastlines where salt water mixes in.
facts: Its snout is more pointed than the American alligator's.
It also thrives where salt water mixes in near coastlines.
In Florida, it lives alongside the American alligator.

0383.name: Siamese Crocodile
0383.desc: This is a medium-sized crocodile living in the fresh waters of Southeast Asia. So few remain in the wild that it's critically endangered. It tends to be gentle and rarely harms people.
facts: Very few remain in the wild.
It tends to be gentle and rarely harms people.
Conservation centres are breeding it and releasing it back into the wild.

0384.name: Dwarf Crocodile
0384.nickname: The smallest crocodile in the world
0384.desc: This is the smallest crocodile species in the world, living in Africa. Its body is covered in thick, bony armour. It's mostly active at night and lives in burrows near water.
facts: It is the smallest crocodile in the world.
Its body is covered in thick, bony armour.
It is nocturnal.

0385.name: False Gharial
0385.desc: It has a long snout like the gharial's, but it's actually a different species — hence the name 'false gharial'. It lives in the swamps and rivers of Southeast Asia.
facts: Its snout is long like the gharial's, but it's a different species.
It lives in the swamps of Malaysia and Indonesia.
It builds a nest of leaves to lay its eggs.

0386.name: Leopard Gecko
0386.desc: This small lizard has leopard-like spots on its body. Unlike most other geckos, it has eyelids that let it blink. It's gentle-tempered, making it a popular pet reptile.
facts: It's one of the few geckos with eyelids.
Its tail stores fat, which is why it looks thick.
It is nocturnal.

0387.name: Crested Gecko
0387.desc: This adorable gecko has eyelash-like crests above its eyes. Its sticky feet let it cling to glass with ease. It lives in the forests of New Caledonia and is also popular as a pet.
facts: It has eyelash-like crests above its eyes.
Its sticky feet let it cling to glass.
It was once thought to be extinct before being rediscovered.

0388.name: Leachie Gecko
0388.desc: This is the largest gecko species in the world, living in New Caledonia. It lives in trees and is active at night, with a thick, short tail and bumpy skin.
facts: It's the largest of all gecko species.
At night, it makes a barking sound like a dog.
Special toe pads let it climb even smooth tree trunks with ease.

0389.name: Gargoyle Gecko
0389.desc: This gecko from New Caledonia has small horn-like bumps on its head. It's active at night, eating insects and fruit up in the trees.
facts: The horn-like bumps on its head give it the name 'gargoyle'.
It can regrow a lost tail.
It can change its body colour slightly.

0390.name: Tokay Gecko
0390.desc: This large gecko from Southeast Asia is famous for its loud 'tokay! tokay!' call. Its blue body is covered in colourful orange spots.
facts: Its name comes from the sound of its call.
Its bite is so strong it rarely lets go once it bites.
It sometimes clings to house walls, hunting pests.

0391.name: Gold Dust Day Gecko
0391.desc: This is a bright green gecko from Madagascar. It has yellow spots on its back that look like sprinkled gold dust, and unlike most geckos, it is active during the day.
facts: Unlike most geckos, it is active during the day.
The golden yellow spots on its back give it its name.
It loves drinking flower nectar.

0392.name: Madagascar Giant Day Gecko
0392.desc: This is the largest of all the day geckos, found in Madagascar. It has a vivid green body with red stripes, and it climbs up and down trees during the day.
facts: It is the largest species of day gecko.
Its bright green colour helps it hide among leaves.
It enjoys sweet nectar and fruit.

0393.name: Satanic Leaf-tailed Gecko
0393.desc: This tiny gecko from Madagascar has a body and tail shaped just like a dried leaf. Its perfect disguise lets it hide among real leaves and avoid predators.
facts: Its body looks exactly like a dry, dead leaf.
The tip of its tail even looks like a leaf nibbled by insects.
It hunts insects at night.

0394.name: African Fat-tailed Gecko
0394.nickname: The gecko with a pantry tail
0394.desc: This gecko from West Africa stores nutrients in its plump tail. It lives on the ground and hunts insects at night.
facts: It stores nutrients in its fat tail so it can survive when food is scarce.
It can drop its tail and flee when in danger.
It is a ground-dwelling gecko.

0395.name: Web-footed Gecko
0395.nickname: The desert gecko with webbed feet
0395.desc: This gecko lives in the Namib Desert in Africa. Webbed skin on its feet lets it walk easily across sand and dig burrows. It is active at night and gets its water from fog.
facts: Its webbed feet help it walk on sand and dig burrows.
It licks droplets of night fog off its eyes and body to drink.
Its skin is semi-translucent, so you can faintly see through it.

0396.name: Panther Chameleon
0396.desc: This chameleon from Madagascar shows brilliantly different colours depending on where it lives. It moves each eye independently and catches insects with its long tongue.
facts: Its colours differ completely depending on its home region.
It can move each eye separately to look in two directions at once.
It shoots out a tongue as long as its body to catch insects in an instant.

0397.name: Veiled Chameleon
0397.nickname: The chameleon with a built-in helmet
0397.desc: This chameleon from the Arabian Peninsula has a tall, helmet-like crest on its head. It even uses that crest to collect dew droplets to drink.
facts: It uses the helmet-shaped crest on its head to collect dew for drinking.
Unlike most chameleons, it also eats a little plant matter.
It moves its eyes independently for a wide field of view.

0398.name: Jackson's Chameleon
0398.desc: This chameleon lives in the mountains of East Africa. Males have three horns on their head, just like a dinosaur. It is unusual in giving birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
facts: Males have three horns like a Triceratops.
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
It lives in cool mountains, so it does not tolerate heat well.

0399.name: Parson's Chameleon
0399.desc: This is the largest and heaviest chameleon in the world, found in Madagascar. It moves slowly, hunts insects in the trees, and lives a long life.
facts: It is the heaviest chameleon species in the world.
It moves very slowly, so it is not easily noticed.
It lives longer than most other chameleons.

0400.name: Spectral Pygmy Chameleon
0400.nickname: Tiny chameleon of the leaf litter
0400.desc: This very small chameleon lives on the forest floor in Central Africa. It looks like a dry, fallen leaf, so it stays hidden on the ground and hunts tiny insects.
facts: It lives among the fallen leaves on the forest floor.
Its leaf-like shape helps it hide well.
It is one of the smallest chameleons.

0401.name: Green Iguana

info.서식지: Rainforest · Rivers & lakes · South America · North America

0401.nickname: The green vegetarian of the treetops
0401.desc: This large lizard from Central and South America has a green body and a long tail. It lives in the trees eating leaves, and it is also a strong swimmer.
facts: It is an almost entirely plant-eating lizard.
When in danger, it leaps into water and swims away.
The flap of skin under its throat makes it look bigger.

0402.name: Marine Iguana
0402.desc: Found only on the Galapagos Islands, this is the only lizard in the world that swims in the sea. It eats seaweed underwater and sneezes salt out through its nostrils.
facts: It is the only lizard in the world that dives into the sea to feed.
It sneezes excess salt out through its nostrils.
After coming out of the cold ocean, it basks on rocks to warm back up.

0403.name: Desert Iguana
0403.desc: This lizard lives in North American deserts and can stay active even in the hottest part of the day. It especially enjoys eating the leaves and flowers of the creosote bush.
facts: It stays active during the scorching midday heat when other animals hide.
It especially loves the yellow flowers of the creosote bush.
When in danger, it quickly dashes into a burrow in the sand.

0404.name: Rhinoceros Iguana
0404.desc: This large lizard lives on the Caribbean island of Hispaniola. It has horn-like bumps on its snout, just like a rhinoceros. It lives on the ground and eats plants.
facts: It has rhino-like horn bumps on its snout.
It is a ground-dwelling iguana that spends most of its time on the ground.
Its shrinking habitat has put it at risk of extinction.

0406.name: Brown Basilisk
0406.desc: This brown lizard from Central America can also run across water on its hind legs, just like the green basilisk. It hunts insects and small animals along riverbanks.
facts: When in danger, it runs across the water on two legs to escape.
Its long tail helps it balance while running.
It lives along rivers, moving between the trees and the ground.

0407.name: Egyptian Spiny-tailed Lizard
0407.desc: This lizard lives in the deserts of North Africa and the Middle East and has sharp spines on its tail. It hides in a burrow and swings its spiny tail as a weapon when threatened. It mostly eats plants.
facts: It swings its spiny tail like a weapon.
It is one of the largest uromastyx species.
It comes out of its burrow in the daytime to bask and eat plants.

0408.name: Thorny Devil
0408.nickname: Covered head to tail in spikes
0408.desc: This small lizard from the Australian desert is covered from head to tail in sharp spikes. It collects water through its skin and eats nothing but ants.
facts: Being covered in spikes makes it hard for predators to swallow.
Grooves in its skin channel dew all the way to its mouth to drink.
It can eat thousands of ants in a single day.

0409.name: Texas Horned Lizard
0409.desc: This flat-bodied lizard lives in the deserts of the southern United States and has horns on its head. When threatened, it can squirt blood from its eyes to startle predators. It mostly eats ants.
facts: When in danger, it squirts blood from around its eyes to scare off predators.
Its flat body presses close to the ground, helping it hide well.
It is a dedicated ant hunter that eats huge numbers of ants.

0411.name: Chinese Water Dragon
0411.nickname: The green lizard by the water
0411.desc: This green lizard lives along rivers in Southeast Asia. It climbs trees well and swims well too, diving into the water whenever it senses danger.
facts: It lives in trees near water and dives in when threatened.
It is a strong swimmer and can stay submerged for a long time.
Its long tail helps it balance while swimming.

0412.name: Asian Water Monitor
0412.nickname: The big hunter by the water
0412.desc: This very large lizard from Southeast Asia is the second-largest lizard in the world, after the Komodo dragon. It lives near water, swims well, and hunts a wide range of animals.
facts: It is the second-largest lizard species after the Komodo dragon.
It swims so well that it can cross rivers and even the sea.
It uses its long tongue to smell out its prey.

0413.name: Savannah Monitor
0413.nickname: The stout hunter of the savanna
0413.desc: This sturdy monitor lizard lives on the African savanna. It loves insects and snails so much that it digs into the ground to find them. It has a thick, powerful body.
facts: It uses its strong jaws to crack open even hard snail shells.
It digs a burrow and rests inside during the hot part of the day.
When threatened, it puffs up its body and lashes out with its tail.

0414.name: Crocodile Monitor
0414.nickname: The longest lizard in the world
0414.desc: This extremely long monitor lizard lives in the rainforests of New Guinea. Thanks to its long tail, it is the longest lizard species of all. It is a skilled tree-climbing hunter.
facts: Its tail is much longer than its body, making it the longest lizard overall.
It spends most of its time up in the trees.
It has long, sharp teeth.

0415.name: Lace Monitor
0415.desc: This large monitor lizard lives in Australian forests. It gets its name from the lace-like yellow patterns on its body. When it senses danger, it quickly climbs up a tree.
facts: It lays its eggs in other animals' burrows or termite mounds.
When threatened, it spirals around a tree trunk to hide on the far side.
It also scavenges the meat of dead animals, acting as a cleaner.

0416.name: Perentie
0416.desc: This is the largest lizard living in the Australian desert. Its body is covered in beautiful cream-coloured spots. It can run very fast, and it sometimes stands up on its hind legs.
facts: It is the largest lizard in Australia.
When it senses a threat, it stands on its hind legs to look around.
It hides in rock crevices and burrows.

0417.name: Emerald Tree Monitor
0417.nickname: The emerald fairy of the treetops
0417.desc: This shimmering green monitor lizard lives in the rainforests of New Guinea. It grips branches tightly with its long tail as it moves through the trees. It loves eating insects.
facts: It uses its tail to grip branches like an extra hand.
Its green body is excellent camouflage among the leaves.
It is small and slender for a monitor lizard.

0418.name: Red Tegu
0418.desc: This is a large, sturdy tegu from South America. Males turn a reddish colour as they grow older. It is an omnivore that eats just about anything.
facts: Adult males develop a reddish face and body.
It hibernates in a burrow during the cold season.
It is intelligent and can grow fairly comfortable around people.

0419.name: Argentine Black and White Tegu
0419.desc: This is a large, intelligent tegu from South America with a beautiful black-and-white mottled pattern. Remarkably, this lizard can raise its own body temperature.
facts: During the breeding season, it can raise its body temperature slightly on its own.
It digs a burrow and hibernates for several months in winter.
It is very intelligent, which makes it a popular pet.

0420.name: European Green Lizard
0420.nickname: The green jewel of the meadow
0420.desc: This shimmering green lizard lives in European meadows. Males develop a blue throat in spring. It loves sunlight and basks happily in warm spots.
facts: Males develop a bright blue throat during the breeding season.
It can drop its tail and escape when in danger.
More than half of its body length is its long tail.

0421.name: Sand Lizard
0421.desc: This small lizard lives in sandy ground and grassland across Europe and Asia. Males turn green along their sides during the mating season. It lives on small insects.
facts: During the mating season, males' sides turn bright green.
It lays its eggs in warm sand where sunlight hatches them.
It hibernates underground through the cold winter.

0422.name: Black Spiny-tailed Iguana
0422.nickname: The fastest lizard in the world
0422.desc: This is an iguana from Central America with rows of sharp spines running down its tail. It is an astonishing animal, the fastest-running lizard of all.
facts: It holds the record as the fastest lizard in the world.
The spines on its tail help protect it.
Young ones eat insects, while adults mostly eat plants.

0424.name: Prehensile-tailed Skink
0424.nickname: The vegetarian giant of the treetops
0424.desc: This is the largest skink in the world, found in the Solomon Islands. It can wrap its tail around branches to grip them. Remarkably, it is a vegetarian lizard that eats only plants.
facts: It is the largest species of skink in the world.
It can grip and hang from branches with its tail.
It is a rare lizard that lives together in family groups.

0425.name: Sandfish Skink
0425.nickname: The lizard that swims through sand
0425.desc: This is a small skink that lives in the desert. With its smooth body, it swims through the sand just like a fish through water. In the heat of the day, it slips beneath the sand to cool off.
facts: It glides through sand almost like a fish swimming through water.
Its smooth scales keep sand from sticking to its body.
It senses the footsteps of insects on the sand to hunt them.

0426.name: Many-lined Sun Skink
0426.desc: This is a common skink found in Southeast Asia. It has many stripes running along its body and reddish or orange sides. It moves quickly in the sunshine, hunting for insects.
facts: Its reddish-orange sides give it the nickname 'fire-tailed skink.'
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
It is active in the warm sun during the day.

0427.name: Egyptian Cobra
0427.nickname: The desert snake with a hood
0427.desc: This is a large cobra from North Africa. When it feels threatened, it spreads its hood wide to look bigger. It is a dangerous snake with powerful venom.
facts: When threatened, it spreads its hood into a wide shape.
It was regarded as a symbol of royalty in ancient Egypt.
It can be active both by day and by night.

0428.name: Indian Cobra
0428.desc: This is a famous cobra from India. When it spreads its hood, a spectacle-shaped marking appears on the back. It has powerful venom, so it must be treated with caution.
facts: It has a spectacle-shaped marking on the back of its hood.
It eats many rats, which can help farmers.
It is the snake famously handled by Indian snake charmers.

0429.name: Monocled Cobra
0429.nickname: The cobra with one ring mark
0429.desc: This cobra lives in Southeast Asia. When it spreads its hood, a single round ring marking appears, giving it the name 'monocled' cobra, after the single-lens eyeglass. It likes to live near water.
facts: It has a single circular ring marking on the back of its hood.
It loves water and often lives near rice paddies and wetlands.
It hunts more actively at night.

0430.name: Cape Cobra
0430.desc: This cobra lives in South Africa. Its shimmering colours, ranging from yellow to brown, make it beautiful, but it has extremely powerful venom. It is active during the day.
facts: Its colour varies between individuals: yellow, brown or black.
It climbs trees well to raid bird nests.
It is one of the most venomous cobras in Africa.

0431.name: Black-necked Spitting Cobra
0431.desc: This is an unusual cobra from Africa that can accurately spray venom straight at an enemy's eyes. It gets its name from the black colouring on its neck.
facts: It can spray venom several metres straight at an enemy's eyes.
It can defend itself with venom alone, without ever biting.
When threatened, it spreads its hood and rears up.

0432.name: Black Mamba
0432.desc: This is an extremely fast and dangerous snake from Africa. It is called the black mamba because the inside of its mouth is black. It is one of the fastest-moving snakes in the world.
facts: It is called the black mamba because of the black colour inside its mouth.
It can move at speeds of around 20 km/h.
It is one of the most venomous snakes in Africa.

0433.name: Eastern Green Mamba
0433.nickname: The green arrow of the treetops
0433.desc: This bright green snake lives in the forests of East Africa. It spends most of its time up in the trees, hiding easily among the green leaves. It is a slender, fast-moving snake.
facts: Its bright green body is excellent camouflage among leaves.
It lives almost entirely up in the trees.
It is gentler than the black mamba, but its venom is still powerful.

0437.name: Gaboon Viper
0437.desc: This is a large, heavy-bodied viper from the rainforests of Africa. Its leaf-like pattern hides it perfectly on the forest floor. It has the longest fangs of any snake in the world.
facts: It has the longest fangs of any snake.
Its leaf-patterned skin hides it perfectly on the forest floor.
It has a very heavy, thick body.

0438.name: Puff Adder
0438.nickname: The snake that huffs and puffs
0438.desc: This is a thick, stout venomous snake from the African savanna. When threatened, it puffs up its body and makes a loud hissing sound, which is how it got its name. It moves slowly, but its bite is very dangerous.
facts: When threatened, it puffs up its body and hisses loudly.
It waits in ambush, then strikes its prey extremely fast.
It is one of the snakes responsible for the most bites on people in Africa.

0439.name: Eastern Diamondback Rattlesnake
