0439.desc: This is a very large rattlesnake from the southeastern United States. Rows of diamond-shaped markings run down its back. It shakes the rattle at the tip of its tail to warn others to stay away.
facts: It shakes the rattle on its tail to warn of danger.
It is the heaviest venomous snake in North America.
It smells with its tongue and senses the heat of warm prey through pits on its face.

0440.name: Sidewinder
0440.desc: This small rattlesnake lives in the hot deserts of the United States and Mexico. It flicks its body sideways in an S-shape to move quickly across the hot sand. It has horn-like scales sticking up above its eyes.
facts: Moving sideways means less of its body touches the hot sand at once.
The horn-like scales above its eyes help shield them from sunlight.
During the day, it buries itself in the sand to hide.

0441.name: Bushmaster
0441.nickname: The silent hunter of the jungle
0441.desc: This is the longest venomous snake in the Americas, living in the rainforests of South America. It is also one of the rare vipers that lay eggs. It is a very quiet, nocturnal snake that is rarely seen.
facts: It is the longest venomous snake in the Americas.
It is one of the few vipers that lay eggs.
It uses heat-sensing pits to find warm prey even in darkness.

0442.name: Copperhead
0442.desc: This venomous snake from the eastern United States has a copper-coloured head. Its body matches fallen leaves so closely that it is hard to spot on the forest floor. It has venom, but it is a mild-mannered snake that does not attack people first.
facts: Its body colour matches fallen leaves so perfectly that it disappears when hiding.
It senses the heat of warm prey through pits on its face.
It bites more people in the United States than any other snake, but its venom is relatively mild.

0443.name: Ball Python
0443.nickname: The snake that curls into a ball
0443.desc: This is a gentle python from Africa. When frightened, it curls its body into a tight ball and tucks its head inside, which is how it got the name 'ball python.' It has no venom and a calm temperament, making it a popular pet.
facts: When frightened, it curls its whole body into a ball.
It has no venom and kills prey by constriction.
Its calm nature makes it one of the most popular pet snakes in the world.

0444.name: Burmese Python
0444.nickname: The giant constrictor of the forest
0444.desc: This is one of the largest snakes in the world, found in Southeast Asia. It has no venom, but it kills prey by squeezing tightly with its body. Because of its beautiful pattern it has been sold as a pet, and released animals have spread through the wild in Florida, causing serious problems.
facts: It is one of the largest snakes in the world.
It has no venom and kills prey by constriction.
It is a strong swimmer and can stay underwater for more than 30 minutes.

0445.name: Reticulated Python
0445.nickname: The longest snake in the world
0445.desc: This is the longest snake species in the world, found in Southeast Asia. It gets its name from the net-like pattern on its body. It climbs trees and swims well, and it has no venom, killing prey by constriction instead.
facts: It is the longest snake in the world, sometimes exceeding 6 metres.
It has a beautiful net-like, reticulated pattern.
It has no venom and kills prey by constriction.

0446.name: African Rock Python
0446.desc: This is the largest snake in Africa. It has no venom, but it is so powerful that it can constrict even large animals. It likes living near water, swims well, and tends to have a fierce temperament.
facts: It is the largest snake in Africa.
It has no venom and uses its powerful body to constrict prey.
It loves water and is a very strong swimmer.

0447.name: Red-tailed Boa

info.서식지: Rainforest · Grassland · South America · North America

0447.nickname: The constrictor with a red tail
0447.desc: This large snake from Central and South America gets its name from the reddish pattern near its tail. It has no venom and constricts prey with its body. It is unusual in giving birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
facts: It has a reddish pattern near its tail.
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
It has no venom and kills prey by constriction.

0448.name: Emerald Tree Boa
0448.nickname: The emerald jewel of the trees
0448.desc: This beautiful green snake lives up in the trees of the South American rainforest. During the day, it rests draped in loops over a branch. Its colour matches the leaves so closely that it is hard to spot in the trees.
facts: Its green colour matches the leaves, so it is hard to spot in the trees.
It rests draped in coils over a branch.
Its young are born red or orange and turn green as they grow.

0451.name: Lionfish
0451.nickname: The venomous beauty of the reef
0451.desc: The lionfish is a striking fish that lives in the ocean. It has long, showy fins and carries venom. It eats small fish and crustaceans, and it is often kept as an ornamental fish in aquariums.
facts: Its dorsal fin spines carry venom that it uses to defend itself.
It mainly lives on coastal reefs in the Indian and Pacific Oceans.
Its striking stripes make it a popular fish for aquariums.

0452.name: Whale Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean · Atlantic Ocean

0452.nickname: The gentle giant of the sea
0452.desc: The whale shark is the largest fish in the world. But it is nothing to be afraid of. It lives on tiny plankton and small fish. It may be as huge as a whale, but it is very gentle with people.
facts: It is the largest living vertebrate on Earth.
It filters plankton from the water to feed.
It is gentle and does not attack people.

0453.name: Ray

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0453.desc: The ray is a large fish that lives in the ocean. It swims using wing-like fins. Unlike most fish, its eggs are fertilised inside the female's body, and she gives birth to live young. Many different kinds of rays live in the sea.
facts: There are around 350 species of ray, and most live on the seafloor.
Its skeleton is made of tough, flexible cartilage.
Its flat, strong teeth let it crush molluscs and crustaceans.

0454.name: Seahorse

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0454.desc: The seahorse is a small, curious fish that lives in the ocean. In a special role, it is the father seahorse that carries and cares for the young. It moves slowly through the water and clings to seaweed.
facts: The male seahorse carries and cares for the young, a unique role among animals.
It clings to seaweed or coral with its tail.
It moves very slowly through the water.

0455.name: Yellowtail Clownfish
0455.desc: The clownfish is a small fish that lives in the ocean. It becomes friends with a sea anemone and lives alongside it. It is famous as the fish called Nemo, so many people already know it well.
facts: It lives together with a sea anemone in a partnership that helps them both.
If the female dies, one of the males changes sex to become female.
It lays around 700 to 800 eggs at a time.

0456.name: Fugu

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0456.desc: The pufferfish is a fish with a very special skill. When in danger, it puffs up its body to startle its enemies. But pufferfish carry venom, so great care must be taken when preparing them as food!
facts: It is only safe to eat once the venom has been carefully removed.
It puffs up its body when it senses danger.
Farmed pufferfish are raised without venom.

0457.name: Salmon
0457.nickname: The traveller of sea and river
0457.desc: The salmon is a fish that travels between the sea and rivers. It is born in a river, grows up in the sea, and returns to the river to lay its eggs. Salmon is also a delicious fish often used in cooking.
facts: It has the habit of returning to the exact river where it was born to spawn.
Most Pacific salmon die after spawning, but Atlantic salmon can spawn again.
It lives in the sea for a long time before swimming upstream to spawn.

0458.name: Pacific Bluefin Tuna
0458.desc: The bluefin tuna is a large fish that lives in the ocean. Its body has a bluish sheen, and it has a long tail. Tuna can regulate their own body temperature, which lets them swim well even in cold seas.
facts: It is one of the few fish that can regulate its own body temperature.
It has been found at depths of up to 550 metres.
It is caught most often in summer, when it migrates north.

0459.name: Amur Carp
0459.desc: The carp is a fish that lives in rivers and lakes. It lays eggs and feeds on small animals. There are many varieties, distinguished by their colour and scales.
facts: It is a carnivore that feeds on small aquatic animals.
Its appetite grows strongest once the water warms above 20 degrees Celsius.
It lays its eggs among the weeds along the water's edge.

0460.name: Amur Catfish
0460.nickname: The whiskered cat of the water
0460.desc: The catfish is a fish that lives in the water and has long whiskers, just like a cat. It is active mostly at night, hunting small fish and insects. Its body is a dark brown colour.
facts: It is active mainly at night.
Its whiskers help it search for food.
It hunts small animals living in the water.

0461.name: Eel
0461.desc: The eel is a long, slippery fish. It is born in the ocean, grows up in a river, and then returns to the sea to lay its eggs before dying. Eels can live for more than 10 years.
facts: It is born in the sea and migrates to rivers to grow up.
It returns to the sea to spawn, and dies afterward.
Its blood contains a nerve toxin, so care is needed when handling it raw.

0462.name: Striped Marlin
0462.nickname: The striped speedster of the sea
0462.desc: The striped marlin is a large fish that lives in the ocean. It has blue stripes on its body, and its colours shift when it gets excited. During the day, it swims near the surface with others of its kind, and at night it dives down into deep water.
facts: It is a large fish that can grow up to 6 metres long.
It stays near the surface during the day and moves into deep water at night.
Special pigment cells let its colours change when it becomes excited.

0464.name: Electric Eel
0464.nickname: The living battery of the river
0464.desc: The electric eel is a remarkable fish that generates electricity to stun its prey. Its long, tapering tail makes up most of its body, and it also uses electricity underwater to detect other objects. It mainly lives in South American rivers such as the Amazon.
facts: It generates electricity to stun its prey.
More than four-fifths of its body length is its tail.
It cannot survive out of water for more than 15 minutes.

0465.name: Piranha
0465.desc: The piranha is a small fish that lives in South American rivers. It travels in groups and feeds on a variety of animals. Its teeth are extremely sharp and strong.
facts: It is a carnivore that eats a wide variety of animals.
It is used as food by Indigenous peoples in South America.
Its name means 'toothed fish.'

0467.name: Devil Fish
0467.desc: The devil ray is a large fish that lives in the ocean. It swims using wing-like pectoral fins and feeds on small creatures. It gives birth to just one pup at a time.
facts: Its wingspan can reach up to 5.2 metres.
It is the only mobulid ray species found in the Mediterranean Sea.
It mainly feeds on plankton and small fish.

0468.name: Flying Fish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0468.desc: The flying fish is a fish, but it uses its large, wing-like fins to launch itself above the water. It mainly lives in warm seas, and many come near Jeju Island too. It is drawn to light at night, so shining a lamp makes it easy to catch.
facts: It uses its large pectoral fins to glide through the air as if flying.
There are around 50 species of flying fish around the world.
Because it is drawn to light, it can be caught at night using bright lamps.

0469.name: Blackmouth Angler

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0469.nickname: The flattened hunter of the seabed
0469.desc: The angler is a remarkable fish that lives in the ocean. Its flat, broad body lets it hide easily on the seafloor. It mainly feeds on small fish and other seafood.
facts: Its flat body is an adaptation to life on the seafloor.
It mostly preys on creatures that live on the seabed.
It is caught in the largest numbers during winter.

0470.name: Pacific Mackerel
0470.desc: The mackerel is a fish that travels in schools through the ocean. Wavy patterns on its body help it stay hidden. It is small and swims very fast!
facts: It lays between 100,000 and 300,000 eggs at a time.
The chub mackerel has a swim bladder.
Its wavy pattern helps camouflage it.

0471.name: Sardine
0471.desc: The sardine is a small fish. Huge numbers live in the ocean, and people often can them as tinned food. In Portugal, sardines are grilled and eaten as a delicious treat.
facts: It is divided into 21 different species.
It is widely processed into tinned fish.
In Portugal, sardines are grilled over a fire and eaten.

0472.name: Pacific Herring
0472.nickname: The silver fish of the sea
0472.desc: The herring is a small fish that lives in the ocean. Its body shimmers silver, and it travels in large schools. Herring is an important food source for many animals, and people enjoy eating it too.
facts: It had a major impact on the economy of medieval Europe.
In Korea, it is famously dried in winter to make gwamegi.
Sweden's surstromming, a famously smelly dish, is made from fermented herring.

0473.name: Goldfish
0473.desc: The goldfish is a small fish that is orange or golden in colour. It is kept in tanks, and it eats eagerly and swims actively when fed. With good care, it can live for a very long time.
facts: It can grow up to 30cm long.
It has been bred into many different colours and shapes.
It has been kept as an ornamental fish for thousands of years.

0475.name: Halibut
0475.desc: The halibut is a fish that lives in the ocean, with a flat body and both eyes on the left side of its head. It lives in shallow water in summer and moves to deeper water in winter. People mostly eat halibut raw, as sashimi.
facts: It has an unusual look, with both eyes on the left side of its head.
It lives in shallow water in summer and moves to deeper water in winter.
Farmed halibut, unlike wild ones, often have blotchy patterns on their underside.

0478.name: Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0478.desc: The shark is a large fish that lives in the ocean. Its sharp teeth make it an effective hunter. But sharks attacking people is actually very rare. There are many kinds of sharks: some are tiny, and others are truly enormous.
facts: It replaces its teeth throughout its entire life.
Some shark species can even live in fresh water.
It mainly uses its sense of smell to find prey.

0479.name: Korean Mandarin Fish
0479.nickname: The hunter of the mountain stream
0479.desc: The mandarin fish lives in clear, clean rivers. Its colourful, mottled pattern makes it hard to spot when it hides among rocks. It is a fearsome hunter that stalks small fish at night.
facts: Its mottled pattern helps it hide among the rocks.
It stays hidden during the day and hunts at night.
It only lives in clean water, so its presence signals good water quality.

0480.name: Northern Snakehead
0480.desc: The snakehead is a fish with a long, snake-like body. Remarkably, it can breathe air out of water, which lets it survive well even in shallow water. It is a very powerful hunter that preys on other fish.
facts: It can breathe air in addition to using its gills.
Its body is long and slippery, like a snake.
Both parents guard and protect their young.

0481.name: Crucian Carp
0481.desc: This chubby fish lives in ponds and rivers all over the country. It can put up with murky water that would trouble other fish. Since it eats almost anything, it thrives just about everywhere.
facts: It is a tough fish that can survive in dirty water.
It eats almost anything without being picky.
It is one of the most common fish found in Korea.

0482.name: Chinese Muddy Loach
0482.desc: This slippery fish lives on muddy river and pond bottoms. It has whiskers around its mouth that help it find food hidden in the mud. Amazingly, it can even breathe through its intestine when oxygen runs low.
facts: It has long whiskers around its mouth.
It can breathe air through its intestine.
It burrows deep into the mud to hide.

0483.name: Ayu Sweetfish
0483.desc: This silvery fish lives in clear rivers and gives off a fresh scent like watermelon or cucumber, which is how it earned its nickname. It scrapes moss off rocks to eat. Most live for only one year, dying soon after laying their eggs.
facts: Its body gives off a fresh, watermelon-like scent.
It feeds by scraping moss off rocks.
It usually lives just one year, dying after it lays its eggs.

0484.name: Japanese Smelt
0484.desc: This small, see-through fish loves cold water. In winter it swims in schools beneath the frozen surface of lakes. It is a cute little fish that even carries a fresh, cucumber-like scent.
facts: It swims in schools under frozen lake ice in winter.
Its body is transparent and shimmers silver.
It has a fresh scent similar to cucumber.

0485.name: Japanese Rice Fish
0485.desc: This is one of the smallest fish found in Korea. It lives in shallow, calm water such as rice paddies and ditches, swimming together in schools. With its big eyes and tiny body, it is an especially cute little fish.
facts: It is one of the smallest fish found in Korea.
It eats mosquito larvae, helping keep mosquito numbers down.
It swims in schools through shallow water.

0486.name: Pacific Cod
0486.desc: This is a large fish that lives in cold seas. Its mouth is so enormous that its name literally means 'big mouth.' Every winter, it gathers in huge schools and moves into shallow water to lay its eggs.
facts: Its mouth is so large that its name literally means big mouth.
It gathers in shallow seas in winter to lay its eggs.
It has a single short whisker under its chin.

0487.name: Alaska Pollock
0487.desc: This cold-water fish is a close cousin of the cod. Depending on how it is prepared, it goes by many different names — frozen, it is called dongtae, and dried, it is called bugeo. Korean people have enjoyed eating it for a very long time.
facts: It has many names such as dongtae, bugeo and hwangtae depending on how it is prepared.
It is a close cousin of the Pacific cod.
It lives in schools in cold, deep seas.

0488.name: Japanese Amberjack
0488.desc: This powerful fish swims swiftly through the sea. Its long, sleek body shoots forward like an arrow. In winter it grows plump and especially tasty.
facts: It swims as fast as a shooting arrow.
Its name changes as it grows bigger, a trait it shares with a few other fish.
It becomes plump and fatty in winter.

0489.name: Japanese Spanish Mackerel
0489.desc: This silver fish swims extremely fast through the sea. Its long, slender body lets it dart after small fish and catch them in a flash. Rows of spots run along its sides, marking it as a fine hunter.
facts: It is a close cousin of the mackerel.
It chases down small fish at high speed.
Rows of spots run along its body.

0490.name: Pacific Saury
0490.desc: This long, silvery fish shimmers in the sea. It becomes especially tasty in autumn, when people love to grill it. It travels the open ocean in large schools.
facts: It is at its fattiest and tastiest in autumn.
Its long body makes it look a bit like a cutlassfish.
It gathers at night, drawn toward light.

0491.name: Japanese Anchovy
0491.desc: This tiny silver fish swims together in enormous schools. It becomes food for many larger fish, which makes it a very important part of the ocean's food web.
facts: It swims in huge schools.
It is often dried and used to make soup stock.
Though small, it is an essential part of the marine food chain.

0492.name: Japanese Horse Mackerel
0492.nickname: The fish with the spiky sideline
0492.desc: This silvery sea fish has a row of hard, shield-like scales running along its side. It swims quickly through the water in large schools. It is delicious whether grilled or eaten raw.
facts: It has hard, shield-like scales running along its lateral line.
It swims very fast in schools.
It looks quite similar to the mackerel.

0494.name: Red Seabream
0494.nickname: The red king of the sea
0494.desc: This handsome sea fish has a reddish body dotted with shining blue spots. It is a prized fish often served at celebrations and special occasions. It lives a very long time and grows quite large.
facts: Blue spots shimmer across its body.
It is a prized fish served at celebration feasts.
Its strong teeth can even crush shellfish.

0496.name: Bartail Flathead

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

0496.desc: This sea fish has an extremely flat, wide head. It buries its body in the sand and waits patiently for prey to pass by. Its sandy colouring makes it very hard to spot.
facts: Its head is extremely flat from top to bottom.
It hides in the sand while waiting for prey.
Its body colour blends in with the sand, making it hard to see.

0498.name: Great White Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0498.nickname: The mightiest hunter of the sea
0498.desc: This is a huge, powerful shark with rows of razor-sharp teeth that make it an excellent hunter. It is considered one of the most fearsome predators in the ocean.
facts: It has teeth arranged in several rows, so new teeth grow in when old ones fall out.
Its sense of smell is so sharp it can find prey from far away.
Its skeleton is made not of hard bone but of soft cartilage.

0500.name: Guppy
0500.desc: This small, pretty freshwater fish is a popular choice for home aquariums. Males have tails as colourful as a rainbow. Instead of laying eggs, guppies give birth to live young that can swim right away.
facts: It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
The male's tail is as colourful as a rainbow.
It breeds so well that its numbers can grow very quickly.

0501.name: Longspined Porcupinefish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0501.desc: When this fish senses danger, it gulps down water to puff its body up like a ball. Once inflated, sharp spines all over its body stand on end, making it impossible for predators to swallow.
facts: When puffed up, it can grow to more than twice its original size.
Its hard teeth can crush open shellfish.
It carries a poison similar to that of a pufferfish.

0503.name: Striped Eel Catfish
0503.desc: This sea catfish has bold white stripes running along its body. When young, hundreds of them cluster together into a tight ball, so that they look like one single large creature.
facts: Its dorsal and pectoral fins carry venomous spines that cause a lot of pain if you are stung.
Young fish swim together in tight, ball-shaped schools.
It has four pairs of whiskers around its mouth to help it find food.

0505.name: Pacific Rudderfish
0505.desc: This fish has a flat, round, silvery body. It lives a little deeper in the ocean, swimming in schools and feeding on small prey.
facts: Its body is slippery and its scales fall off easily.
It has a habit of swimming together in schools.
It is a tasty fish, often grilled or braised.

0506.name: Silver Pomfret
0506.desc: This fish has a flat, diamond-shaped body that shines silver all over. Its soft flesh is delicious, which makes it a favourite on dinner tables.
facts: It has no pelvic fins, which makes its body look extra smooth.
It travels in large schools.
Its soft meat makes it a popular fish to eat.

0508.name: Flathead Grey Mullet

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0508.desc: This sturdy fish moves back and forth between rivers and the sea. It is famous for leaping powerfully out of the water again and again.
facts: It can move freely between saltwater and freshwater.
It has a habit of leaping above the surface several times in a row.
It sifts through mud on the bottom for food, swallowing even grains of sand.

0511.name: Okhotsk Atka Mackerel
0511.desc: This fish lives in the cold seas of the far north. Its skin is especially tasty, and people enjoy eating it wrapped around other food.
facts: It swims together in large schools.
It is famous for its delicious skin.
Males guard the eggs that are attached to rocks.

0514.name: Brown-backed Toadfish
0514.desc: This pufferfish has a smooth, brownish back. Like other pufferfish, it puffs up its body to protect itself whenever it senses danger.
facts: Like other pufferfish, it puffs up its belly when in danger.
As a pufferfish, certain parts of its body may contain poison.
It uses its hard teeth to crack open shellfish.

0518.name: Coelacanth
0518.desc: This ancient fish has been swimming the oceans since long before the dinosaurs. It was believed to be extinct until a living one was discovered in 1938, astonishing the whole world.
facts: It is a 'living fossil' that has changed hardly at all in about 400 million years.
Its fins contain sturdy, limb-like bones.
It gives birth to live young rather than laying eggs.

0520.name: Arapaima
0520.nickname: The giant fish of the Amazon
0520.desc: This is one of the largest freshwater fish in the world, living in the Amazon River. It can grow to nearly three metres long and regularly rises to the surface to gulp air.
facts: It is one of the largest freshwater fish in the world.
It must gulp air using its swim bladder, almost like a lung, to survive.
Hard scales protect its body like a suit of armour.

0523.name: Neon Tetra
0523.nickname: The little neon light of the water
0523.desc: This tiny fish has glowing blue and red stripes running along its body. When dozens of them swim together in a school, the whole tank seems to sparkle.
facts: Its blue and red stripes glow like little neon lights.
It swims together in large schools.
It is one of the most popular aquarium fish in the world.

0524.name: Discus
0524.nickname: The round disc of the water
0524.desc: This fish has a flat, round body shaped like a disc, covered in bright, colourful patterns. Thanks to its beautiful markings, it is often called the 'king of tropical fish.'
facts: Parents produce a special mucus on their skin to feed their young.
Its round, disc-like shape is how it got its name.
It is a popular aquarium fish known as the 'king of tropical fish.'

0528.name: Nile Tilapia
0528.nickname: The friendly fish on the dinner table
0528.desc: This fish originally comes from the Nile River in Africa but is now farmed and eaten all over the world. It eats almost anything and grows quickly, which makes it easy to raise. It also protects its eggs and young by carrying them inside its mouth.
facts: The female protects her eggs and young by carrying them in her mouth, a habit called mouthbrooding.
It survives well in many different environments, making it one of the most widely farmed fish in the world.
It grows quickly and is widely used as an important food fish.

0529.name: Largemouth Bass
0529.desc: This freshwater fish is a fierce predator with an enormous mouth. It quickly snatches up small fish or frogs. While popular among anglers, in Korea it is considered an invasive species that disrupts local ecosystems.
facts: Its mouth opens so wide it reaches past its eyes, letting it swallow even large prey.
It ambushes its prey and strikes in an instant.
It is officially designated as an ecosystem-disturbing species in Korea.

0530.name: Bluegill
0530.desc: This flat freshwater fish has a dark blue spot just behind its gills. It travels in schools, feeding on small insects. Like the largemouth bass, it arrived in Korea as an invasive species.
facts: It is called 'bluegill' because of the dark blue spot behind its gill cover.
The male digs a nest on the bottom to guard the eggs.
It is officially designated as an ecosystem-disturbing species in Korea.

0531.name: Rainbow Trout
0531.desc: This beautiful trout has a colourful, rainbow-like stripe running along its side. It loves clear, cold water and is farmed and eaten in many places around the world.
facts: A pink, rainbow-coloured stripe along its side is how it got its name.
It can only survive in clear, cold water, so its presence signals clean water.
Some individuals migrate out to sea and later return, and these are called 'steelhead.'

0532.name: Whitespotted Char
0532.desc: This salmon-family fish lives in cold mountain streams. Pale white spots are scattered across its body. It loves clear water flowing through mountain valleys.
facts: The pale white spots scattered across its body give it its name.
It can only live in extremely cold, clean water.
Some individuals travel down to the sea and later return back up the river.

0533.name: Lenok
0533.desc: This salmon-family fish lives only in extremely cold, clear mountain water. It is a rare, protected fish in Korea. Even in summer, it seeks out the coolest water it can find.
facts: It can only survive in water colder than 20 degrees Celsius.
In parts of Korea it is protected as a natural monument.
Its numbers are declining as clean water becomes harder to find.

0539.name: Shortfin Mako Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0539.desc: This is the fastest shark in the world, capable of swimming faster than 70 kilometres per hour, which is how it earned the nickname 'bullet of the sea.' Its dark blue back and white belly make it hard to spot in the water.
facts: It is the fastest shark in the world.
It can leap more than six metres out of the water.
It can keep its body warm, letting it move quickly even in cold water.

0540.name: Tiger Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0540.desc: This large shark has tiger-like stripes across its body. It is famous for eating almost anything, which earned it the nickname 'cleaner of the sea.' It lives in warm waters and hunts mostly at night.
facts: It has tiger-like stripes across its body.
It eats almost anything, earning it the nickname 'cleaner of the sea.'
It hunts mainly at night.

0541.name: Bull Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0541.nickname: The shark that swims up rivers
0541.desc: This is a remarkable shark that can live in freshwater rivers as well as the sea. It sometimes swims far upstream, occasionally appearing close to where people live. Its thick, powerful body is how it earned the name 'bull shark.'
facts: Despite being a shark, it can live in freshwater.
It can swim hundreds of kilometres up a river.
Its thick, powerful body is why it is called the 'bull shark.'

0543.name: Blacktip Reef Shark
0543.desc: This small shark lives on coral reefs, easily recognised by the black tips on its fins. It swims through shallow, warm reef waters. It is a fairly gentle shark and not very dangerous to people.
facts: Its fins are tipped with black.
In shallow reef waters, its dorsal fin sometimes pokes above the surface.
It is not very dangerous to people, a fairly gentle shark.

0544.name: Whitetip Reef Shark
0544.desc: This shark lives on coral reefs, easily recognised by the white tips on its fins. During the day it rests in caves or rocky crevices, then hunts in groups at night. Its slender body lets it slip into narrow gaps.
facts: Its fins are tipped with white.
It rests in caves during the day and hunts at night.
Its slender body lets it slip into narrow crevices between rocks.

0545.name: Thresher Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0545.desc: This shark has an extraordinarily long tail, nearly as long as its whole body. It swings this tail like a whip to stun schools of fish before eating them. The tail alone can make up half its total length.
facts: Its tail can be as long as half its total body length.
It swings its long tail like a whip to stun fish.
It sometimes leaps powerfully out of the water.

0546.name: Basking Shark
0546.desc: This is the second largest fish in the world, after the whale shark. Despite its enormous size, it eats only tiny plankton. It swims slowly with its huge mouth wide open, filtering food from the seawater.
facts: It is the second largest fish in the world.
Despite its huge size, it eats only tiny plankton.
It swims with its mouth wide open, filtering food out of the seawater.

0549.name: Greenland Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar regions · Arctic Ocean · Atlantic Ocean

0549.desc: This shark lives in the cold Arctic seas and is famous as one of the longest-living animals on Earth — some individuals live for more than 400 years. It swims very slowly and lives deep in the dark water.
facts: It is the longest-living vertebrate animal known.
It swims so slowly that it is nicknamed the 'sleeping shark.'
It grows so slowly that it doesn't reach adulthood until it is over 150 years old.

0550.name: Dwarf Lanternshark
0550.desc: This is the smallest shark in the world — even fully grown, it is only about the size of a hand. It lives deep in the dark ocean, where it glows softly from its belly, either to hide itself or to find others of its kind.
facts: It is the smallest shark in the world, about the size of a palm.
It glows light from its belly to stay hidden in the dark ocean.
It lives so deep that it is rarely seen by people.

0554.name: Cownose Ray

info.서식지: Ocean · North America · South America · Atlantic Ocean

0554.desc: This ray has the front of its head split into two rounded lobes, like a cow's nose. It flaps its wing-like fins and swims together in groups. It loves eating shellfish.
facts: Thousands of them can travel together in an enormous group.
It crushes hard shellfish with its flat teeth.
Flapping its fins, it looks just like it is flying through the water.

0555.name: Bluespotted Ribbontail Ray

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0555.nickname: The blue-spotted fairy of the reef
0555.desc: This small ray has a yellow body dotted with vivid blue spots. It moves through coral reefs searching for shellfish and shrimp. Its tail carries a venomous spine, so it's best to keep a careful distance.
facts: The blue spots on its body are a warning colour meaning 'I am venomous.'
A venomous spine at the tip of its tail is swung out when it feels threatened.
It comes up onto the reef at high tide to search for food.

0558.name: Blue Shark

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0558.nickname: The blue traveller of the open sea
0558.desc: This slender shark has a beautiful blue sheen along its back. It travels vast distances across the open ocean and can be found in seas all around the world.
facts: It is a great traveller, crossing thousands of kilometres of open ocean.
Its thin, long fins let it swim for a long time without tiring.
It gives birth to dozens of pups at once.

0559.name: Humphead Wrasse

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0559.nickname: The coral reef giant with a bump on its head
0559.desc: This is a very large fish with a big bump jutting out on its forehead. It is the largest wrasse living on coral reefs, and it can crush even hard shellfish with ease.
facts: It readily eats even venomous starfish and sea urchins.
It can change from female to male as it grows.
The bump on its forehead grows larger as it ages.

0560.name: Bullethead Parrotfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0560.nickname: The parrot-mouth that gnaws on coral
0560.desc: This fish uses its hard, parrot-like beak to gnaw on coral. It eats the algae growing on the coral and spits out the leftover coral as fine sand, which helps build white sandy beaches.
facts: The sand it spits out after feeding on coral can be enough to help form a white sandy beach.
At night, it wraps itself in a clear mucus cocoon to sleep in.
Its colour, and even its sex, can change as it grows.

0566.name: Blue Tang

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0566.nickname: The blue fish with a yellow tail
0566.desc: This cute fish has a vivid blue body and a bright yellow tail. It swims in groups among coral reefs. A sharp, hidden spine sits near its tail.
facts: It has a blade-like spine near its tail, which is why it is also called a 'surgeonfish.'
When in danger, it plays dead, lying still on its side.
Its body is yellow as a young fish and turns blue as it grows.

0567.name: Yellow Tang
0567.desc: This pretty fish is bright yellow from head to tail. It grazes on algae growing on the rocks of coral reefs. At night, its bright colour fades a little.
facts: It has a white spine near its tail to protect itself.
It sometimes cleans algae off the bodies of other fish.
At night its yellow colour fades and a pale stripe appears along its side.

0570.name: Pearl-spot Chromis
0570.nickname: The dark school beside the rocks
0570.desc: This small fish is common in the seas around Korea's south coast and Jeju Island. It lives in groups near rocks, feeding on plankton in the water. It has a white spot near its tail.
facts: On Jeju Island it is known as 'jari' and is enjoyed as food.
The male fans the eggs stuck to the rock with his fins to protect them.
It travels in schools, watching out for one another.

0578.name: Opah

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0578.desc: This large sea fish has a round, flattened, silvery body with beautiful red fins. Remarkably, it is the only fish known to keep its entire body warm. This lets it swim fast even in the deep ocean.
facts: It was the first fish ever discovered to be fully warm-blooded.
It beats its fins like a bird's wings, seeming to fly through the water.
Its large, round body can look a bit like a car tyre.

0579.name: John Dory

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

0579.nickname: The fish with an eye on its side
0579.desc: This fish has a very flat body with one large black spot on each side. The spot looks like an eye, which confuses predators. It is a skilled hunter, shooting its mouth forward to suck in prey in an instant.
facts: The black spot on its side is a fake eye that fools enemies.
It shoots its mouth forward to suck in prey.
Its body is so flat that it is almost invisible when seen head-on.

0580.name: False Kelpfish
0580.desc: This fish has a mottled pattern that makes it nearly invisible when hiding among rocks or seaweed. Its fins carry sharp spines, so it needs to be handled with care. It is also unusual in giving birth to live young.
facts: Its mottled pattern lets it hide perfectly among the rocks.
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
A jab from its fin spines can be quite painful.

0581.name: Dark-banded Rockfish
0581.desc: This fish lives in shallow seas rich in rocks and seaweed. It has dark stripes on its body and travels in groups. It is a popular catch among anglers in Korea.
facts: It gives birth to live young rather than laying eggs.
It becomes active and searches for food at night.
It lives in groups near the rocks.

0583.name: Blackhead Seabream
0583.nickname: The silver bream that changes sex
0583.desc: This bream has a silvery body with a dark tinge around the head. Most start life as males and change into females as they grow. It is a clever, cautious fish that is hard to catch by fishing.
facts: It starts life as a male and becomes female as it grows.
It can live well even in river mouths where fresh water mixes in.
It is extremely cautious, making it a difficult fish to hook.

0584.name: Japanese Seabass
0584.nickname: The silver hunter that visits rivers
0584.desc: This large fish has a long, silvery, shimmering body. Though it lives in the sea, it sometimes swims up into river mouths to find food. It is a skilled hunter, chasing down small fish with great speed.
facts: It is a sea fish but swims up into river mouths.
Young seabass travel together in groups.
It hunts by chasing small fish at speed.

0585.name: Convict Grouper
0585.desc: This large grouper has seven dark bands across its body. It stays hidden among rocks and dens, swallowing prey whole. It is big and powerful enough to be called the king of the rocky reef.
facts: It has seven thick stripes across its body.
It swallows its prey whole instead of chewing.
It claims one rocky den as its home and guards it.

0587.name: Giant Grouper

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

0587.desc: This is one of the largest fish found on coral reefs. It can grow to more than twice the height of a person, and its huge mouth can swallow large prey whole. It is gentle by nature but truly enormous in size.
facts: It ranks among the largest of all coral reef fish.
Its mouth is big enough to swallow a small shark whole.
Its weight can exceed that of several people combined.

0590.name: Greater Amberjack

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0590.nickname: The mighty big brother of the amberjacks
0590.desc: This is the largest fish among the amberjacks. It has a dark stripe running across its eye and is extremely strong. It swims quickly in groups through warm seas, feeding on small fish.
facts: It is the largest of all the amberjack fish.
A dark stripe runs across each eye.
It is so strong that reeling one in can tire out your arm.

0591.name: Mahi-mahi

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0591.desc: This beautiful fish has a bulging forehead and a body that shimmers blue, green and yellow. It swims extremely fast and leaps powerfully out of the water. It also grows at an astonishing rate.
facts: It is one of the fastest-growing fish in the world.
Its colours flash brilliant blue and yellow when it is caught.
Males have a forehead that bulges out like a wall.

0592.name: Cobia

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0592.desc: This fish has a long body and a flat head that make it look like a small shark. It usually swims alone, sometimes following large fish or boats. It grows quickly and is strong, making it a popular fish for farming.
facts: Its flat head gives it the look of a small shark.
It prefers to travel alone as a loner.
Like a remora, it sometimes tags along behind large fish.

0598.name: Indo-Pacific Sailfish
0598.nickname: The fastest sailboat in the sea
0598.desc: This fish has a huge sail-like fin on its back and a snout as sharp as a spear. It is considered one of the fastest fish in the ocean. It raises its large dorsal fin to herd schools of prey while hunting.
facts: It is considered one of the fastest fish in the sea.
It can raise its dorsal fin into a huge sail shape.
It uses its sharp snout to slash at schools of prey.

0601.name: American Bullfrog
0601.desc: The bullfrog is a big frog that mostly lives near water. Its long, strong back legs let it jump great distances. At night it can make a loud sound like a bull bellowing. It should be treated with caution, since bullfrogs prey on small animals.
facts: A bullfrog can lay an enormous number of eggs.
Its powerful back legs let it leap great distances.
At night it can make a loud sound like a bull bellowing.

0602.name: Frog

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · North America · South America · Africa · Oceania

0602.desc: Frogs are animals that can live both in water and on land. They leap great distances on their long legs and catch insects with their long tongues. In winter they sleep underground.
facts: Frogs catch their prey using their long tongues.
While hibernating, some frogs can survive even when their body temperature drops below freezing.
A frog can jump up to 20 times its own body length.

0603.name: Poison Dart Frog
0603.desc: The poison dart frog is a small frog that lives in the rainforests of Latin America. Its skin carries a powerful poison, which Central and South American tribes once used to coat their arrows. Its bright, colourful skin makes it easy to recognise.
facts: Poison dart frogs carry a powerful poison in their skin.
Central and South American tribes coated their arrows with the poison from these frogs.
Their bright colours act as a warning sign to predators.

0605.name: Axolotl
0605.desc: The axolotl is a remarkable salamander that lives its whole life in water. It can reach adulthood without ever transforming its body. It comes in a wonderful range of colours, which makes it truly fascinating.
facts: An axolotl can regrow lost body parts.
It can reproduce without ever completing metamorphosis into a land-dwelling adult.
It comes in a wide variety of colour forms.

0606.name: Sakhalin Toad
0606.desc: Toads are larger than frogs and have poisonous skin that keeps most predators away. They lay their eggs in long, string-like strands. In Korean legends, toads often appear as creatures that help humans.
facts: Toads lay their eggs in long, string-like strands.
Toads release toxic substances from their skin to defend themselves against predators.
In Korean folktales, toads often appear as helpers to humans.

0607.name: Japanese Tree Frog
0607.desc: The Japanese tree frog is a small frog that lives up in the trees. It can change colour, which helps it hide well against trees or grass. It is known for its loud calling at night.
facts: It can change colour to blend in well with its surroundings.
Males use a vocal sac to call loudly and attract females.
It hibernates in winter and breeds around May to June.

0609.name: Boreal Digging Frog
0609.desc: This is a frog with a small, round body. It mostly emerges from underground during the rainy season to mate and lay eggs. It is famous for its distinctive 'maeng-kkong' call.
facts: When threatened by a predator, it puffs up its body to defend itself.
It lays its eggs during the rainy season and goes through metamorphosis quickly.
Its call is used by males to attract females.

0610.name: Korean Clawed Salamander
0610.nickname: The clawed guardian of the valley
0610.desc: The Korean clawed salamander is a small salamander that lives in clear, cold mountain streams. Tiny claws on its toe tips help it grip slippery stones. It only lives where the water is pristine, making it a rare creature to encounter.
facts: It has true claws on the tips of its toes.
It can only survive in extremely clean water.
It is mostly active at night.

0611.name: Dwarf Salamander
0611.desc: True to its name, the dwarf salamander is a very small and adorable salamander. It lives beneath damp fallen leaves or near mountain streams. Its tiny body makes it almost impossible to spot when it hides among the leaves.
facts: It is one of the smallest salamanders around.
It likes to live beneath damp fallen leaves.
It lays jelly-like eggs in the water.

0612.name: Gori Salamander
0612.desc: The Gori salamander is a special salamander found only in a very small area near Busan, Korea. Because its range is so limited, it is a truly precious creature. In spring, it lays its eggs in small pools of water.
facts: In the whole world, it lives only in this small part of Korea.
It was identified as a new species in 2003.
Because its habitat is so limited, it needs protection.

0613.name: Jeju Salamander
0613.desc: The Jeju salamander lives on Jeju Island and in Korea's southern regions. It loves damp forests and mountain streams. When spring arrives, it attaches pretty egg sacs underwater.
facts: It lives on Jeju Island and in Korea's southern regions.
In spring it attaches egg sacs underwater.
Its skin is moist and smooth.

0614.name: Four-toed Salamander
0614.nickname: The friend with four back toes
0614.desc: The four-toed salamander is a small salamander with only four toes on each back foot. It favours damp, mossy places. When in danger, it can drop its own tail to escape.
facts: It has only four toes on each back foot.
When threatened, it can drop its tail and flee.
It lays its eggs in moss and watches over them.

0615.name: Red-spotted Newt
0615.nickname: The water salamander with red spots
0615.desc: The red-spotted newt has pretty red spots dotting its back. It lives on land when young and returns to the water once it becomes an adult. The red spots are a warning sign that it carries poison.
facts: It has a row of red spots along its back.
It lives on land when young and in water as an adult.
It carries a mild poison in its body for protection.

0616.name: Texas Blind Salamander
0616.desc: The Texas blind salamander is a remarkable creature that lives only in the pitch-dark water of caves. Because it lives in darkness, it has almost no eyes and a pale white body. Red gills stick out from the sides of its head.
facts: It has almost no eyes because it lives in caves.
Its body is pale, almost translucent white.
Red gills stick out from the sides of its head.

0618.name: Red-eyed Tree Frog
0618.desc: The red-eyed tree frog is a striking frog with large, brilliant red eyes. Its body is green while its legs flash blue and orange, making it wonderfully colourful. It sleeps behind leaves during the day and comes out at night.
facts: Its eyes are large and vividly red.
When startled, it flashes its red eyes to surprise predators.
It lives up among the leaves of trees.

0619.name: Amazon Milk Frog
0619.desc: The Amazon milk frog gets its name from the milky-white fluid it releases when threatened. Its grey and brown markings make it look like tree bark. It lives high up in the trees of the Amazon rainforest.
facts: When threatened, it releases a milky-white fluid.
It lays its eggs in small pools of water inside tree hollows.
Its markings resemble tree bark.

0620.name: Waxy Monkey Tree Frog
0620.desc: The waxy monkey tree frog spreads a wax-like oil over its body to stop moisture from escaping. Like a monkey, it climbs slowly along branches, gripping with its hands and feet. It is a clever frog that thrives even in dry places.
facts: It coats its body in wax to hold in moisture.
It climbs slowly along branches like a monkey.
It survives well even in dry environments.

0621.name: Strawberry Poison Dart Frog
0621.desc: The strawberry poison dart frog is small and bright red like a strawberry. Its lovely colour is a warning that says, 'I'm poisonous, don't eat me.' The mother frog carries her tadpoles on her back to pools of water collected in leaves.
facts: It is a bright strawberry-red poison frog.
Its vivid colours are a signal of danger.
The mother carries her tadpoles on her back to move them.

0622.name: Green and Black Poison Dart Frog
0622.nickname: The green and black patterned poison frog
0622.desc: The green and black poison dart frog has striking green and black markings. Its beautiful pattern is a warning that says, 'don't touch me.' It hops about among the fallen leaves on the rainforest floor.
facts: Its green and black markings are strikingly bold.
Its bright colours are a warning of danger.
The father carries the tadpoles on his back to move them.

0623.name: Golden Poison Dart Frog
0623.nickname: The world's most poisonous golden frog
0623.desc: The golden poison dart frog is a tiny frog with a gleaming golden body. But it is the most poisonous frog in the world, so it must never be touched. Its beautiful colour is itself a major warning sign of danger.
facts: It is the most poisonous frog in the world.
Its golden body is a warning sign of danger.
It lives only in one part of Colombia.

0624.name: Panamanian Golden Frog
0624.desc: This frog is truly beautiful, with a shining golden body. Its skin carries a very strong poison, so it should never be handled carelessly. In Panama, it is much loved as a frog that brings good luck.
facts: It waves its front feet to greet other frogs.
Its skin carries a very strong poison.
It has nearly vanished from the wild and is now protected in zoos.

0625.name: Green Climbing Toad
0625.desc: Unlike other toads, this one is a skilled tree climber. Its green and yellow skin lets it hide well among leaves. It becomes active at night, moving about in search of food.
facts: It is a toad that is unusually good at climbing trees.
It is active at night.
Its skin colour helps it hide among the leaves.

0626.name: African Bullfrog
0626.nickname: The big eater that sleeps underground
0626.desc: This is a very large, powerful frog with a big mouth that lets it eat almost anything. During the dry season, it burrows underground and sleeps for months. The father frog is a devoted parent who watches over the tadpoles.
facts: It is one of the largest frogs in Africa.
During dry periods, it sleeps underground for months at a time.
The father frog carefully guards the tadpoles.

0630.name: Paradoxical Frog
0630.nickname: The strange frog that grows backwards
0630.desc: This astonishing frog has tadpoles that are much bigger than the adult frog. As the tadpole grows up, its body actually gets smaller. It loves living and swimming in the water.
facts: Its tadpoles are much larger than the adult frog.
Its body actually shrinks as it matures.
It loves living and swimming in water.

0634.name: Colorado River Toad
0634.desc: This very large toad lives in the deserts of the United States. It releases poison from its skin to protect itself. On rainy nights, it gathers at pools of water to lay its eggs.
facts: It is one of the largest toads in North America.
It releases poison from its skin.
It gathers by water on rainy nights.

0635.name: Cane Toad
0635.nickname: The biggest toad in the world
0635.desc: This is one of the largest toads in the world, with a truly huge body. It has poison glands behind its eyes that release toxin if a predator bites down. It eats almost anything and thrives almost anywhere, making it very common.
facts: It is one of the largest toads in the world.
Poison glands behind its eyes release toxin when it is bitten.
It thrives almost anywhere, which makes it very common.

0639.name: Tree Frog
0639.desc: This green tree frog has wide webbing between its toes. It hops between trees and can glide smoothly through the air. Round pads on its toe tips let it stick firmly to leaves.
facts: It has wide webbing between its toes.
Sticky pads on its toe tips let it cling to trees.
It lives up in the trees.

0640.name: Dybowski's Brown Frog
0640.desc: This brown frog lives in mountain streams and pools. It is an early riser, among the very first frogs to lay eggs in early spring. Its long back legs let it jump great distances.
facts: It is one of the first frogs to lay eggs each spring.
It lives in mountain streams.
Its long back legs let it jump far.

0644.name: Suwon Tree Frog
0644.nickname: The tree frog that calls in the rice paddies
0644.desc: This precious tree frog was first discovered near Suwon, Korea. When the rice paddies are flooded, it clings to rice stalks and sings. Its numbers have dropped a great deal, so it truly needs protecting.
facts: It was first discovered near Suwon.
It clings to rice stalks to call.
Its numbers have declined, so it needs protection.

0645.name: Glass Frog
0645.desc: This remarkable frog has a transparent belly through which you can see its heart and internal organs. It lives in the forests of Central and South America. It attaches its eggs beneath leaves and watches over them.
facts: Its transparent belly reveals its beating heart.
It lays its eggs beneath leaves.
It is active at night.

0647.name: Leopard Frog
0647.desc: This green frog has round, dark spots covering its back like a leopard's coat. It lives in the ponds and meadows of North America. It is an excellent jumper, able to escape danger in a flash.
facts: It has round, leopard-like spots on its back.
It lives in North America.
It is an excellent jumper and escapes danger quickly.

0648.name: Water Toad
0648.desc: Unlike most toads, this one lives near clear mountain streams. It favours cool, watery spots in the mountains. A distinctive trait is that it lays its eggs in fast-flowing streams.
facts: It lives near clear mountain streams.
It lays its eggs in fast-flowing water.
Its skin is bumpy.

0653.name: Japanese Giant Salamander
0653.nickname: The great elder of the riverbed
0653.desc: This is one of the largest amphibians in the world. It lives in clear, cold mountain streams and can live for a very long time. It absorbs oxygen from the water through its wrinkled skin.
facts: It is one of the largest amphibians in the world, growing over 1 metre long.
Its wrinkled skin absorbs oxygen directly from the water.
As clean water becomes scarcer, its numbers have declined and it is now protected.

0654.name: Chinese Giant Salamander
0654.nickname: The largest amphibian in the world
0654.desc: This is the largest amphibian on Earth. It lives in cold mountain streams and can live for a very long time. Sadly, its numbers have dropped sharply and it is now critically endangered.
facts: It is the largest amphibian on Earth, with records of individuals reaching 1.8 metres.
It breathes by absorbing oxygen through its wrinkled skin.
It is critically endangered due to habitat destruction and overharvesting.

0661.name: Emerald Glass Frog
0661.nickname: The frog you can see through
0661.desc: This is an amazing frog whose belly is so transparent you can see its heart and organs inside. Its back is emerald green. It lives on leaves in the rainforest.
facts: Its belly is so transparent that its heart and organs are visible from below.
Its green back helps it hide well on leaves.
The male guards and cares for the eggs on the leaf.

0670.name: Red-backed Salamander
0670.nickname: The little one with a red stripe
0670.desc: This small salamander lives under fallen leaves on forest floors in North America. It has no lungs, so it breathes through its skin.
facts: With no lungs, it breathes through its moist skin.
Some have a red stripe down the back, while others do not.
It never enters water, laying its eggs on land instead.

0671.name: Spotted Salamander
0671.nickname: The salamander dotted with yellow spots
0671.desc: Its jet-black body is lined with round yellow spots. On rainy spring nights, they all migrate together to ponds to lay eggs.
facts: It has two rows of yellow spots along its body.
On rainy spring nights, it migrates to ponds in large groups.
It hides underground and is rarely seen.

0672.name: Marbled Salamander
0672.nickname: The chubby salamander with silver bands
0672.desc: Its black body has silver-and-white marbled bands. Unlike other salamanders, it lays its eggs in a dry pond bed in autumn and guards them.
facts: Its body has a silvery, marbled pattern.
It lays its eggs in autumn on the bottom of a dried-up pond.
The mother stays with the eggs until the rain comes.

0676.name: Red Salamander
0676.nickname: The forest salamander that's bright red all over
0676.desc: This pretty salamander has a bright red body dotted with black spots. It loves living near clear, cold springs.
facts: Its bright red body is scattered with black spots.
It has no lungs and breathes through its skin.
Its red colour mimics a poisonous salamander to keep predators away.

0677.name: Vietnamese Mossy Frog
0677.desc: Its bumpy, green skin looks just like moss, so it's hard to spot once it presses against a rock. It lives in damp forests and caves in Vietnam.
facts: Its moss-like skin gives it perfect camouflage.
When in danger, it curls into a ball and plays dead.
It lives in water-filled rock crevices or tree hollows.

0678.name: Malayan Horned Frog
0678.nickname: The horned frog disguised as a fallen leaf
0678.desc: It has horn-like points of flesh above its eyes, and its body is the colour of dry leaves, so it looks just like a fallen leaf on the forest floor.
facts: The horn-like points above its eyes make it look like a dead leaf.
It hides on the forest floor and waits for prey to pass by.
Its mouth is so large it can swallow big prey whole.

0680.name: American Green Treefrog
0680.nickname: The green frog with a white side stripe
0680.desc: Its bright green body has a white stripe along each side. It is common in the southeastern United States and calls loudly around ponds on summer nights.
facts: It has a white or yellow stripe along each side of its body.
It has been chosen as the official state frog in several U.S. states.
It feeds on insects that gather around bright lights.

0685.name: Common Coquí
0685.desc: This small frog is famous for its "co-quí" call. It's a symbol of Puerto Rico, and its young hatch directly as tiny frogs, skipping the tadpole stage.
facts: It was named after its "co-quí" call.
It skips the tadpole stage entirely, hatching straight from the egg as a tiny frog.
It is a symbol of Puerto Rico.

0686.name: Grey Foam-nest Treefrog
0686.nickname: The grey frog that builds a foam nest
0686.desc: It builds a white foam nest on tree branches and lays its eggs inside. When it's hot, it turns its body white to reflect sunlight.
facts: It builds a foam nest on branches to protect its eggs.
In hot weather, it turns its body white to reflect heat.
Several frogs sometimes build a foam nest together.

0688.name: Japanese Tree Frog
0688.nickname: The green singer of the rice paddies
0688.desc: This small green treefrog lives in the rice paddies and wetlands of East Asia. Round pads on its toes help it climb leaves and branches easily. Males call loudly when rain is on the way.
facts: The pads on its toes let it climb even slippery surfaces like glass.
It can change colour from green to greyish brown to match its surroundings.
During the breeding season, the male's vocal sac swells up large.

0689.name: European Tree Frog
0689.desc: This bright green treefrog lives in forests and wetlands across Europe. Sticky toe pads let it climb trees and grass easily, and it calls loudly at night. It has a dark stripe running along its side.
facts: It can change colour from green to brown depending on the situation.
The male's call can be heard from hundreds of metres away.
In winter, it hibernates underground or beneath fallen leaves.

0693.name: Pool Frog
0693.desc: This green frog lives in clear ponds and marshes across Europe. It often has a light stripe down the middle of its back and rarely strays far from water. It is one of the parent species of the edible frog.
facts: On warm days, it basks by the water to warm itself.
In winter, it hibernates in mud or underground.
It is one of the parent species of the hybrid known as the edible frog.

0694.name: Marsh Frog
0694.desc: It is one of the largest water frogs in Europe, living in marshes and riverbanks. Its colour ranges from green to brown, and it calls loudly. It rarely leaves the water and is an excellent swimmer.
facts: It is one of the largest water frogs in Europe.
When hungry, it will even eat smaller frogs.
Its scientific name means "laughing," because of the male's laughter-like call.

0695.name: Common Frog
0695.nickname: The regular visitor to European gardens
0695.desc: This is a very common brown frog in Europe, easily found in gardens, forests, and ponds. It has a dark patch behind each eye and lays its eggs in a large clump in spring. It does fine in damp places even without water.
facts: In spring, it lays jelly-like eggs in a large clump.
In cold regions, it sometimes hibernates in mud underwater.
It is a helpful neighbour that eats garden pests.

0696.name: Agile Frog
0696.desc: This slender brown frog lives in European forests and is famous for its incredible jumps. Its long hind legs let it leap several times its own body length in a single bound. It likes damp, leaf-covered ground.
facts: It can jump several times its own body length in a single leap.
Its long hind legs earned it the name "agile frog."
It only gathers near water to breed, spending the rest of its time in the forest.

0697.name: Moor Frog
0697.nickname: The frog that turns blue in spring
0697.desc: This brown frog lives in wetlands across Europe and northern Asia. Strangely, in the breeding season, males turn blue for a few days. It likes damp meadows and marshes.
facts: In the breeding season, males turn blue for a few days.
It thrives even in cold regions, ranging as far as Siberia.
In winter, it hibernates underground or beneath fallen leaves.

0698.name: Northern Leopard Frog
0698.desc: This green frog from North America has round, leopard-like spots on its body. It moves between wetlands and grassy fields and is a strong jumper. It used to be very common, though numbers have declined in some areas.
facts: It gets its name from its round, leopard-like spots.
In summer, it wanders through grassy fields looking for food.
It is well known as a species commonly used for dissection in biology classes.

0699.name: Wood Frog
0699.nickname: The frog that freezes and thaws
0699.desc: This brown frog lives in North American forests and has an amazing ability: even when its body freezes in winter, it comes back to life in spring. It has a dark mask-like patch around each eye. It even lives close to the Arctic Circle.
facts: Even when parts of its body freeze in winter, it wakes up again in spring.
Its body produces a special substance that helps it survive being frozen.
It is one of the northernmost-living frog species in the world.

0700.name: Green Frog
0700.nickname: The frog with a banjo call
0700.desc: This green frog is common in ponds and streams across eastern North America. The male's call sounds like a plucked banjo string. It rarely strays from the water's edge and dives in readily.
facts: Its call sounds like a single "plunk" of a banjo string.
When startled, it lets out a squeak and leaps into the water.
It defends its territory along the water's edge and chases off other males.

0701.name: Pickerel Frog
0701.nickname: The bitter-tasting frog with square spots
0701.desc: This North American frog has two neat rows of square spots down its back. Its skin releases a bitter substance, so even snakes tend to avoid eating it. It prefers clear, cool water.
facts: It has two neat rows of square spots down its back.
Its skin releases a bitter substance that keeps predators from eating it.
It especially favours cold, clear water.

0702.name: Dark-spotted Frog
0702.nickname: The black-spotted frog of the rice paddies
0702.desc: This frog is common in the rice paddies and wetlands of East Asia and has black spots on its back. It can easily be found near water in Korea, China, and Japan. It's one of the classic frogs heard calling in rice paddies on summer nights.
facts: It has large black spots scattered across its back.
On summer nights, it calls together with others like a chorus in the rice paddies.
It is a helpful neighbour that eats pests harmful to rice crops.

0703.name: Wrinkled Frog
0703.desc: This frog lives near streams and waterways in East Asia and has a wrinkled, bumpy back. It releases a bitter substance from its skin to protect itself. It can be found near valleys in Korea and Japan.
facts: Its wrinkled, bumpy back gives it the Korean nickname meaning "scabby frog."
Its skin releases a bitter substance that wards off predators.
It hides well in rock crevices near the water.

0704.name: Huanren Brown Frog
0704.nickname: The hidden frog of cold mountain streams
0704.desc: This brown mountain frog lives in clear, cold streams in Korea and northeastern China. It lays its eggs in cold stream water in early spring. It looks so similar to other mountain frogs that it can be hard to tell apart.
facts: It lays its eggs very early in spring, just as the ice begins to melt.
It can only survive where there are clear, cold mountain streams.
Its scientific name comes from Huanren, a region in China.

0705.name: Dybowski's Brown Frog
0705.nickname: The mountain frog of early spring
0705.desc: This brown frog lives in the mountains and forests of Korea and northeast Asia. As soon as it wakes from hibernation, it lays its eggs in pools in early spring. It's commonly found around mountains and valleys.
facts: It lays its eggs very early in spring, right after waking from hibernation.
Once breeding is over, it returns to live in the mountain forest.
It is one of the most common mountain frogs found in Korea.

0706.name: Natterjack Toad
0706.nickname: The runner with a yellow racing stripe
0706.desc: This toad lives in sandy areas and wetlands across Europe. It has a yellow stripe down the middle of its back, and instead of hopping, it scurries along quickly like it's running. It's famous for its extremely loud nighttime call.
facts: It has a bright yellow stripe running down the middle of its back.
Instead of hopping, it scurries along like a running mouse.
The male's call can be heard from several kilometres away.

0710.name: Green Paddy Frog
0710.nickname: The green frog with golden stripes
0710.desc: This green frog lives in the rice paddies and wetlands of Southeast Asia. Golden stripes run along its sides, giving it a beautiful look. It often perches on water plants and is active at night.
facts: Its green body has clear golden stripes running along both sides.
It sits on water plants or leaves, waiting for prey.
It is most active at night in rice paddies and wetlands.

0717.name: Asian Common Toad
0717.desc: This toad is a common sight across many Asian countries. It thrives near people's homes and comes out at night to eat insects.
facts: It has large poison glands behind its eyes that release toxins.
It often shows up at night to eat the insects gathered under streetlights.
It is so common that it can easily be found even in Asian cities.

0721.name: Green-and-black Poison Frog
0721.desc: This poison frog looks like a jewel with its green-and-black pattern. Its bold colours warn, "I'm poisonous, don't eat me!"
facts: It builds up toxins in its body by eating ants, so captive-bred frogs have almost no poison at all.
Its bright colours are a warning that signals danger to predators.
The male carries the tadpoles on his back to water-filled pools up in the trees.

0727.name: Southern Toad
0727.nickname: The toad with a crown on its head
0727.desc: This toad has raised, bump-like ridges on top of its head. It comes out at night to eat insects and burrows well underground.
facts: Raised bony ridges on its head set it apart from other toads.
It is often seen at night eating insects under streetlights.
When threatened, it digs into the ground to hide.

0728.name: Western Toad
0728.nickname: The mountain toad with a back stripe
0728.desc: This large toad has a light stripe running down the middle of its back. It lives in the mountains, forests, and waterways of western North America.
facts: A light stripe down the middle of its back is its most distinctive feature.
Unlike other toads, it rarely hops, instead walking along like it's crawling.
It even breeds and lays eggs in lakes high up in the mountains.

0729.name: Woodhouse's Toad
0729.desc: This common toad has many wart-like bumps on its body. At night, it comes out near water or towns to eat plenty of insects.
facts: A light stripe down the middle of its back is a distinctive feature.
On spring and summer nights, it makes a bleating call like a sheep.
It often comes near human settlements to hunt insects gathered under streetlights.

0732.name: Hairy Frog
0732.nickname: The hairy one with terrifying claws
0732.desc: This unusual frog grows hair-like skin along its sides during the breeding season. Remarkably, when threatened, it can push its own toe bones through its skin to form claws.
facts: When threatened, it pushes its toe bones through its skin to form claws.
During the breeding season, hair-like skin grows along the male's sides to help it absorb oxygen.
It is also nicknamed the "horror frog."

0736.name: Common Reed Frog
0736.desc: This small, colourful frog lives clinging to reeds and grass. It's a fascinating frog whose colour and pattern change dramatically as it grows.
facts: Its colour and pattern change dramatically between youth and adulthood.
Sticky pads on its toes let it cling tightly to reed stems.
In the dry season, it turns its body white to reflect heat and survive.

0737.name: Amani Forest Treefrog
0737.nickname: The treefrog with big green eyes
0737.desc: This treefrog has large eyes and a green body with wavy patterns. It lives happily up in the trees of the forests of Tanzania.
facts: As a youngster it's green with wavy patterns, though it sometimes turns brown as it grows.
Its large eyes help it find food well at night.
Sticky pads on its toes let it cling well to branches.

0750.name: Sardinian Tree Frog
0750.nickname: The treefrog of the Mediterranean islands
0750.desc: This small treefrog lives on the Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica. Its green body helps it hide well among leaves and grass. Round pads on its toes let it stick even to slippery leaves.
facts: It lives only on the islands of Sardinia and Corsica.
The pads on its toes let it cling even to slippery leaves.
Its green body helps it hide among leaves.

0751.name: Butterfly

info.서식지: Asia · Europe · North America · South America · Africa · Oceania

0751.desc: A butterfly is an insect with beautiful wings. It hatches from an egg, grows into a caterpillar, and passes through a pupa stage before becoming an adult. Once it's an adult, it sips nectar from flowers.
facts: Its wings are covered in tiny scales, which give them their many colours and patterns.
It goes through complete metamorphosis, eating plant leaves during its caterpillar stage.
It uses a long tube-like mouth to sip nectar and sap.

0752.name: Honeybee

info.서식지: Near cities · Grassland · Asia · Europe · Africa · North America

0752.desc: The honeybee is a small flying insect. It makes honey and carries pollen from flower to flower, helping plants grow fruit. Without honeybees, many of the foods we eat could disappear.
facts: It builds hives with six-sided cells to house its colony.
Worker bees and the queen have stingers, but male drones do not.
Its sense of direction can be badly thrown off by electromagnetic waves.

0753.name: Harlequin Ladybird

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America

0753.desc: The ladybird is a small beetle known for the spots on its back. It mostly eats aphids, which helps protect crops. Ladybirds come in many colours, making them fun to spot.
facts: Its colours and patterns vary so much that it can be tricky to identify.
It eats aphids and scale insects, helping to protect crops.
It has been introduced by people to many regions around the world.

0754.name: Dragonfly

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Africa · Europe · North America · South America · Oceania

0754.desc: The dragonfly is an insect that flies through the sky hunting small insects. Its long body and two pairs of wings let it fly incredibly fast. Its young live in water before growing up and coming out onto land.
facts: Its two pairs of wings let it fly at high speed.
Its young live underwater and breathe through gills.
Its huge compound eyes give it excellent all-round vision.

0757.name: Japanese Rhinoceros Beetle
0757.desc: The Japanese rhinoceros beetle is an insect with a big horn. Males have a long horn shaped like deer antlers, and they come out at night to feed on tree sap. It's easy to spot in Korea too!
facts: It can lift objects more than 50 times its own body weight.
A male's long horn branches out like a deer's antlers.
It is mostly active at night.

0758.name: Ant

info.서식지: Rainforest · Asia · Africa · Europe · North America · South America · Oceania

0758.desc: The ant is a very small insect. It lives in groups, working together and searching for food. There are many kinds of ants, and they're easy to spot all around us.
facts: Scientists estimate there are quadrillions of ants living on Earth.
It lives in a highly organised society, forming large colonies.
It can be found on every continent except Antarctica.

0759.name: Cricket

info.서식지: Grassland · Asia · Africa · Europe · North America

0759.desc: The cricket is an insect known for its song. These little creatures hide in grass or under stones and mostly sing at night. A cricket's song is its way of sending a love message to other crickets.
facts: It communicates with other crickets through its song.
Some crickets make a special sound to claim their territory.
It can live in many different habitats and eats almost anything.

0760.name: Dung Beetle
0760.desc: The dung beetle is a funny insect that rolls dung into balls. It gathers animal droppings, shapes them into a ball, and lays its eggs inside to raise its young. Dung beetles help keep nature clean.
facts: It rolls up animal droppings to store and use later.
It uses the sun and moon to find its way.
It plays an important role in nature as a decomposer.

0763.name: Firefly
0763.desc: The firefly is a small insect that glows in the dark. In the early evening, it flies over meadows giving off a soft yellow light. It uses this light to find other fireflies. Thanks to its special glow, it looks like a tiny star in the night sky.
facts: It glows mostly in the early evening as it flies over grassy fields.
Its larvae mainly feed on snails and freshwater mussels.
In Korea, fireflies are endangered because their habitats have been destroyed.

0765.name: Asian Giant Hornet
0765.nickname: The giant of the wasp world
0765.desc: The Asian giant hornet is an enormous wasp. It lives in forests and hunts other insects. Be careful around it, because its sting can be dangerous!
facts: It is known as the largest hornet in the world.
It builds its nest underground.
Its long stinger and powerful venom make it an effective hunter of other insects.

0768.name: Water Strider

info.서식지: Rivers & lakes · Asia · Europe · North America

0768.nickname: The insect that walks on water
0768.desc: The water strider is a small insect that walks on the surface of water. It feeds on dead fish and insects that have fallen into the water. It moves quickly across the surface, and in winter it sleeps under fallen leaves.
facts: It can move quickly across the surface of water.
Fine hairs and natural oils on its legs let it float.
It lives mostly on the water's surface, where it hunts for food.

0769.name: Diving Beetle
0769.desc: The diving beetle is a large insect that lives in water. It swims underwater and can carry a bubble of air with it. When in danger, it can release a special smell to escape.
facts: It grows to about 3.5 to 4.5 centimetres, the largest of the diving beetle family.
As an adult it is carnivorous, eating both live and dead prey.
When threatened, it releases a foul-smelling substance from behind its head to escape.

0770.name: Green Chafer
0770.desc: This beetle has a shiny, metallic green body. It nibbles on leaves and often flies towards lights on summer nights. A hard shell protects its body.
facts: Its body shines like polished metal.
It flies towards lights on summer nights.
Its larvae live underground, feeding on roots.

0771.name: Katydid
0771.desc: This is a green insect with long antennae. In summer, it rubs its wings together to make a chirring song. Its strong back legs let it jump far.
facts: It rubs its wings together to make sound.
Its antennae are longer than its body.
It uses its back legs to jump long distances.

0772.name: Bell Cricket
0772.desc: This small insect sings a ringing, bell-like song at night. Its body is black with broad, flat wings. Its song sounds even prettier once autumn arrives.
facts: It sings with a sound like a ringing bell.
Only males sing.
It sings most often on autumn nights.

0779.name: Hoverfly

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America

0779.desc: This gentle fly looks like a honeybee but has no sting. It hovers in place in front of flowers, feeding on nectar and pollen. Looking like a bee helps scare birds away.
facts: It looks like a bee but has no sting.
It is skilled at hovering in one spot.
It helps flowers by carrying pollen between them.
