0785.name: Lesser Emperor Dragonfly
0785.desc: This is a large, powerful green dragonfly. Its huge eyes let it watch all directions as it hunts flying insects. It can fly fast and freely through the sky.
facts: Its large eyes let it see in every direction.
It catches insects while flying.
Its young live underwater.

0789.name: Flower Longhorn Beetle
0789.desc: This beetle sits on flowers, feeding on nectar and pollen. Its very long antennae are why it's grouped with the longhorn beetles. Its colourful body brightens up summer flower gardens.
facts: Its long antennae are how it got its longhorn name.
It feeds on pollen while perched on flowers.
Its larvae grow inside wood.

0801.name: Monarch Butterfly
0801.desc: This beautiful butterfly has orange and black wings. It's famous for its epic journey of thousands of kilometres from North America to Mexico. Its caterpillar eats poisonous milkweed, which makes its own body toxic too.
facts: It flies thousands of kilometres to reach Mexico.
Toxins from the milkweed it ate as a caterpillar protect its body.
The migration continues across several generations.

0858.name: Green Scarab Beetle
0858.desc: This beetle has a bronze-green, shimmering body. It feeds on tree leaves in summer and is often drawn to lights at night. It is a common sight in Korea during the summer.
facts: Its bronze shimmer gives it the name bronze beetle in Korean.
It is nocturnal and flies toward lights at night.
The larva grows in the soil, feeding on plant roots.

0865.name: Seven-spot Ladybird

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America

0865.desc: This cute ladybird has a red back with seven black spots. It is a helpful insect to farmers because it eats huge numbers of aphids. When threatened, it releases a yellow fluid.
facts: It can eat dozens of aphids in a single day.
When it senses danger, it releases a bitter yellow fluid from its legs.
Its red and black colouring is a warning sign that says I taste bad.

0866.name: 28-spotted Ladybird
0866.desc: This ladybird has twenty-eight black spots on its back. Unlike most ladybirds, it eats plant leaves such as potato and eggplant instead of aphids. Because of this, it can damage crops.
facts: It is a ladybird that eats plant leaves instead of other insects.
It gets its name from having twenty-eight spots.
It leaves a net-like pattern on leaves after feeding.

0876.name: Black Oil Beetle
0876.nickname: Black beetle with a poisonous oil
0876.desc: This beetle has a plump, glossy black body. When threatened, it releases a poisonous yellow oil from its leg joints to defend itself. Its wings are too short to fly, so it walks slowly across the ground.
facts: It releases a poisonous oil called cantharidin from its body.
The larva clings to a bee to sneak into its hive and steal honey.
Its wings are too short for it to fly.

0879.name: Bumblebee
0879.desc: This is a cute, plump bee with a fuzzy coat of hair. It moves between flowers, gathering nectar and pollen and helping to pollinate them. It is widely used to pollinate greenhouse crops.
facts: Its thick fur picks up pollen easily as it moves between flowers.
It shakes pollen loose with a buzzing vibration.
It is used to pollinate greenhouse crops such as tomatoes.

0880.name: Giant Resin Bee

info.서식지: Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America

0880.desc: This bee belongs to a group of large leafcutter bees, about the size of a bumblebee. Instead of cutting leaves, it gathers tree resin to build chambers inside bamboo or hollow dead wood, where it stores eggs and honey. It is a solitary bee that builds its nest alone.
facts: It gathers tree resin to seal and build its nest chambers.
It is a solitary bee that builds its nest alone rather than in a colony.
It originally came from East Asia but has spread to Europe and North America.

0881.name: Carpenter Bee
0881.desc: This bee looks like a bumblebee but drills tunnels into wood with its jaws to build its nest, which is why it is also called a carpenter bee. It bores smooth holes into dead wood or bamboo.
facts: It drills tunnels into wood with its jaws to build its nest.
It resembles a bumblebee but has a shiny, black, hairless abdomen.
Its strong jaws bore smooth, round holes into dry wood.

0882.name: Red-banded Sand Wasp
0882.desc: This wasp has a waist as thin as a thread and a red band on its abdomen. It digs a burrow in sandy ground and stocks it with a paralysed caterpillar as food for its egg. It hunts and builds its nest alone.
facts: Its thread-thin waist gives it the name thread-waisted wasp.
It digs a burrow in sand and stocks it with a paralysed caterpillar.
After stocking the burrow, it seals the entrance with a stone to hide it.

0883.name: Ichneumon Wasp
0883.nickname: The wasp that lays eggs in caterpillars
0883.desc: This is an orange wasp with a slender body and thin legs. It is a parasitic wasp that lays its eggs inside moth caterpillars, and its own larvae grow by feeding on the caterpillar's body. It helps reduce pest numbers.
facts: It is a parasitic wasp that lays its eggs inside moth caterpillars.
It is a helpful insect that reduces the number of pest caterpillars.
Its slender orange body is long and thin.

0884.name: Japanese Carpenter Ant
0884.nickname: Big black ant of the forest
0884.desc: This is one of the largest black ants found in Korea. It builds large nests in wood or underground and lives together in colonies. It feeds on the sweet honeydew produced by aphids as well as dead insects.
facts: It is one of the largest ants found in Korea.
The queen can live for several years, leading the colony.
It tends aphids to collect the sweet honeydew they produce.

0885.name: Red Wood Ant
0885.desc: This forest ant has a reddish front half to its body. It gathers pine needles and twigs to build a large, mound-shaped nest. When threatened, it sprays a sour liquid called formic acid from the tip of its abdomen.
facts: It piles up pine needles to build a large mound-shaped nest.
When it feels threatened, it sprays sour formic acid.
It protects trees by preying on forest pests.

0886.name: Leucospid Wasp
0886.nickname: The parasitic wasp with its tail raised
0886.desc: This small wasp has black and yellow markings and holds its abdomen tilted slightly upward. It is a parasitic wasp that uses its long ovipositor to lay eggs in the nests of other bees. Its thick hind legs are a distinctive feature.
facts: It folds its long ovipositor back over its abdomen when at rest.
It is a parasitic wasp that lays its eggs in the nests of other bees.
Its thick hind legs have small, saw-like bumps.

0896.name: Oriental Mole Cricket
0896.nickname: The insect mole that digs underground
0896.desc: This insect has broad, shovel-like front legs that make it an excellent digger. It spends most of its life underground, tunnelling through the soil. It eats plant roots and small insects.
facts: Its front legs are broad like a mole's paws, perfect for digging.
It lives underground, so it is rarely seen.
It can fly short distances and is even able to swim.

0901.name: Hermit Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0901.desc: The hermit crab is a fun crustacean that uses a shell as its home. It lives in the sea and on beaches, moving into a bigger shell as it grows. It is mostly active at night and eats a wide variety of foods.
facts: As it grows, a hermit crab needs to find a bigger shell.
Most hermit crabs are nocturnal.
Hermit crabs can be kept as pets.

0902.name: Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0902.desc: Crabs live in the sea and have a pair of pincers. They come in many sizes, from tiny crabs to very large ones. A hard shell protects their body.
facts: There are about 5,000 species of crab around the world.
A crab's pincers are used for catching food and for defence.
Crabs grow by moulting their shell.

0903.name: Shrimp

info.서식지: Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0903.desc: Shrimp are small animals that live in the sea and in rivers. They have a hard shell and long whiskers. There are many kinds of shrimp, and people enjoy eating them too.
facts: Shrimp have a hard shell made of chitin.
Shrimp grow by moulting and can regrow lost limbs.
Shrimp act as cleaners, eating parasites off fish gills, mouths and scales.

0904.name: Lobster
0904.desc: The lobster is a sea animal with large pincers. It mostly lives on the seafloor and eats fish and small creatures. It can live an extremely long time, over 100 years!
facts: A lobster's ability to reproduce can actually increase as it ages.
Lobsters sometimes eat their own shell after moulting.
The largest lobster ever recorded weighed up to 20.15 kilograms.

0911.name: Red Snow Crab
0911.nickname: Friend of the red snow crab
0911.desc: The hongge is a type of snow crab with a red shell. It is caught in large numbers in deep water, making it affordable and tasty. Its moist, sweet meat makes it popular.
facts: Its red shell gives it the name hongge, or red crab.
It is the same crab as the red snow crab.
It is caught in large numbers in deep water.

0913.name: Fiddler Crab
0913.nickname: The crab with one big claw
0913.desc: In the fiddler crab, the male has one large, showy pincer bigger than its own body. It waves this big pincer up and down to attract mates and show off. It lives in burrows it digs in tidal mudflats.
facts: Males have one pincer that is bigger than their own body.
They wave their big pincer to attract mates.
They live in burrows dug into tidal mudflats.

0915.name: Red-clawed Crab
0915.desc: The red-clawed crab has bright red pincers. Long ago, it would sneak into houses to steal food, which is how it got its Korean name, meaning thief crab. It lives on land near the coast and only goes to the sea to lay its eggs.
facts: Its pincers are a striking red colour.
It got its name from sneaking into houses to steal food.
It only goes down to the sea when it is time to lay eggs.

0918.name: Grapsid Crab
0918.desc: The grapsid crab is a small crab that digs burrows in tidal mudflats. It has a square-shaped shell and can be found in huge numbers across mudflats. When a person approaches, it quickly darts into its burrow to hide.
facts: It digs burrows to live in on the mudflat.
Its shell is shaped like a square.
It darts quickly into its burrow when people approach.

0920.name: Sand Bubbler Crab
0920.nickname: The crab that makes sand dumplings
0920.desc: The sand bubbler crab is a very small, round crab. It eats sand, picks out the edible bits, and spits out the leftover sand as little round balls. This leaves the mudflat covered in tiny sand pellets.
facts: It eats sand and picks out only the edible parts.
It spits out the leftover sand as tiny round balls.
It leaves mudflats covered with countless tiny sand balls.

0921.name: Japanese Spider Crab
0921.desc: With its legs stretched out, the Japanese spider crab is far taller than a person. It walks slowly across the deep seafloor. It has the longest legs of any crab in the world.
facts: With its legs spread out, it is the crab with the longest legs in the world.
It lives in deep water, at depths of 200 to 300 metres.
It can live an extremely long time, up to 100 years.

0923.name: Japanese Mud Crab
0923.desc: The Japanese mud crab is a small crab that digs burrows across wide tidal mudflats. Its eyes sit atop long stalks, letting it see far into the distance. When in danger, it quickly darts into its burrow to hide.
facts: It digs burrows to live in on mudflats.
Its eyes sit high atop long stalks.
It darts quickly into its burrow when in danger.

0927.name: Japanese Spiny Lobster
0927.desc: The Japanese spiny lobster is a large shrimp covered in hard, spiny armour. It has no pincers, but instead has very long, sturdy antennae. It comes out from crevices in the rocks at night to find food.
facts: It has no pincers, but has long antennae instead.
Its whole body is covered in sharp spines.
It comes out from rock crevices at night to find food.

0928.name: Coonstripe Shrimp
0928.desc: The coonstripe shrimp has a pretty pink body. It lives in extremely cold, deep water. It is one of the precious shrimp species caught near Dokdo.
facts: Its body is pink like peach blossoms.
It lives in cold, deep water.
It is a precious shrimp caught near Dokdo.

0930.name: Paste Shrimp
0930.nickname: Little one of the shrimp paste
0930.desc: The paste shrimp is a very small, transparent shrimp. It is used to make salty fermented shrimp paste. It drifts through the sea in enormous swarms.
facts: It is a very small, transparent shrimp.
It is used to make fermented shrimp paste.
It drifts through the sea in enormous swarms.

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar regions · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Arctic Ocean
Ocean · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean
Ocean · Rivers & lakes · Asia · Pacific Ocean
Ocean · Pacific Ocean · North America · South America
Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Arctic Ocean
Ocean · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean

0101.name: Siberian Musk Deer
0101.desc: Musk deer are deer with a remarkable scent. Instead of antlers they have long, handsome tusks. They live high in the mountains, grazing on grass and moss.
facts: Musk deer have long canine tusks instead of antlers.
Males have a musk gland on the belly that releases their scent.
The musk is used as an animal perfume and is extremely valuable.

0106.name: Proboscis Monkey
0106.desc: Proboscis monkeys have long noses and reddish-brown coats. They live mostly in the trees and are found in mangrove forests and near water. They live in troops, and males can call loudly through their long noses.
facts: A male's nose can hang down as much as 10 cm.
Proboscis monkeys climb up and down trees skilfully.
They love water and are strong swimmers.

0113.name: Coati
0113.desc: Coatis are endearing animals from South America. Their long noses help them sniff out food. They play happily in the trees and move about in groups.
facts: Coatis use their long noses to find food.
They climb well and live mostly in the trees.
They are highly social and live in groups.

0173.name: Crested Ibis
0173.desc: Crested ibises have red faces and white feathers. In spring and summer the undersides of their wings turn pink. They lay eggs and probe the mud for food with their long bills.
facts: Their plumage changes colour around the breeding season.
Highly sensitive bills let them find food buried in mud.
They were once widespread across East Asia.

0182.name: Common Pheasant
0182.desc: Pheasants are birds with magnificent plumage. They live in farmland and grassland, eating grain and small insects. Pheasants can fly quickly, but not very far.
facts: Males have magnificent, colourful plumage.
Females are camouflaged in ochre and dark brown patterning.
They fly at around 60 km/h.

0183.name: Japanese Quail
0183.desc: Quails are small brown birds. They pass through Korea in winter, eating seeds and insects. Quail eggs are a well-loved delicacy.
facts: Quails pass through Korea as winter migrants.
They are yellowish-brown with brown and black mottling.
Their call is a distinctive rolling trill.

0184.name: Cassowary
0184.desc: Cassowaries are very large birds. They cannot fly, but they run fast. They live mainly in tropical rainforest, eating fruit and small animals. Cassowaries play an important role by spreading seeds far and wide.
facts: The cassowary is the third-largest bird on Earth.
It has a horn-like crest known as a casque.
It plays a vital role spreading seeds through the rainforest.

0191.name: Rhinoceros Hornbill
0191.desc: Hornbills are very large birds. They have white bills topped with a casque and live mainly in tropical rainforest. This bird also represents the Malaysian state of Sarawak.
facts: The rhinoceros hornbill is known for its white bill and casque.
It lives on the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Java and Borneo.
It is the state bird of Sarawak.

0195.name: Brown-eared Bulbul
0195.desc: Brown-eared bulbuls are birds you often see in Korea. They are named for the brown patch by the ear. They live in the trees, eating insects and fruit. In spring they lay eggs and care for their chicks.
facts: The brown cheek patch makes them easy to identify.
They live mainly in trees and are common even in cities.
They eat a varied diet, and are especially fond of persimmons and insects.

0214.name: Shoebill
0214.desc: This is a large bird with a bill shaped like an enormous shoe. It stands perfectly still in the swamp and then snatches up a fish in one go.
facts: Its bill looks like a wooden clog, which gives the shoebill its name.
It can stand as still as a statue for hours while waiting for prey.
Shoebills greet one another by clattering their bills together.

0217.name: Mandarin Duck
0217.nickname: The colourful duck on the water
0217.desc: This is a small duck whose male has plumage as bright as a rainbow. It lives happily alongside its mate on ponds and riverbanks.
facts: The male has distinctive orange 'sail' feathers that rise from the wings.
Known for devotion to its mate, it has long symbolised marital harmony.
It nests in tree holes, and the ducklings leap from a great height to reach the water.

0218.name: Wood Duck
0218.desc: This is a duck with a glossy green head and beautifully patterned plumage. It lives in the ponds and swamps of North American woodland.
facts: It is one of the few ducks that nests in tree holes.
Newly hatched ducklings leap from the high nest and head for the water.
It has claws, which let it perch on branches.

0219.name: Muscovy Duck

info.서식지: River & lake · South America · North America

0219.desc: This is a large duck with red, fleshy growths on its face. It lives along the rivers and forests of Central and South America, and people keep it too.
facts: The red facial caruncles make it easy to tell apart from other ducks.
Unlike other ducks it does not quack, making a quiet hiss instead.
Its claws let it grip trees and perch on branches.

0220.name: Atlantic Puffin
0220.desc: This is an endearing seabird with a large, brightly coloured bill. It digs a burrow in the cliffs and swims underwater to catch fish.
facts: In the breeding season its bill turns a vivid orange and yellow.
It can carry several fish in its bill at once.
It 'flies' underwater with its wings and dives over 60 metres deep.

0232.name: Roseate Spoonbill

info.서식지: River & lake · Ocean · North America · South America

0232.desc: This is a bird with a spoon-shaped bill and pink plumage. It sweeps its bill through shallow water to catch food.
facts: It swings its spoon-shaped bill side to side underwater to find food.
Its pink colour comes from pigments in its food.
It is often confused with flamingos, but the bill shape is completely different.

0234.name: Helmeted Guineafowl
0234.desc: This is a bird with a horn-like helmet on its head and a body covered in white spots. It walks the African grasslands in flocks.
facts: The bony helmet (casque) on its head is what gives it the name 'helmeted'.
It is prized on farms for eating ticks and pest insects.
When it senses danger it alerts the flock with a loud, harsh call.

0241.name: King Bird-of-paradise
0241.nickname: The red jewel of the forest
0241.desc: The king bird-of-paradise is a tiny, brilliant bird-of-paradise from the rainforests of New Guinea. It has scarlet feathers with curled green discs at the tail tips, and the male dances beautifully for the female.
facts: It is one of the smallest of all birds-of-paradise.
Two curled green feather discs decorate the tips of its tail.
The male hangs from a branch and spreads his feathers in an elaborate courtship dance.

0244.name: Great Argus
0244.desc: The great argus is a very large pheasant from the rainforests of Southeast Asia. The male's wings are covered in round, eye-like markings, and he spreads them like an enormous fan before the female.
facts: The many eye-spots on its wings earned it the name of Argus, the many-eyed giant of Greek myth.
The male clears a patch of forest floor as a stage and spreads his wings in a courtship dance.
The eye-spots catch the light so they look three-dimensional, as if they stand out from the wing.

0247.name: Tufted Puffin
0247.desc: The tufted puffin is an endearing seabird with a big orange bill and golden tufts trailing back from above its eyes. It swims underwater to catch fish and raises its chick in a burrow on the cliffs.
facts: In the breeding season long golden tufts grow above its eyes.
It swims with its wings and can carry several fish in its bill at once.
It digs a burrow into the cliff soil to lay its egg and raise its chick.

0249.name: Macaroni Penguin

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Southern Ocean · Atlantic Ocean

0249.desc: The macaroni penguin is a penguin with showy yellow plumes sweeping back above its eyes. It catches krill and fish in the seas near Antarctica, and it is one of the most numerous penguins in the world.
facts: It is named after 'macaroni', an old word for a flamboyantly dressed dandy.
Orange-yellow plumes sweeping back above the eyes are its trademark.
It is among the most numerous penguin species, though its numbers have declined recently.

0251.name: Little Penguin
0251.desc: The little penguin is the smallest penguin in the world, standing only about two hand-spans tall. Its blue back makes it hard to see underwater, and at night it comes out of the sea and waddles back to its beach burrow.
facts: It is the smallest penguin in the world, standing no higher than an adult's knee.
Its bluish back has earned it the name 'blue penguin'.
At night it comes ashore in groups and waddles back to its burrow.

0253.name: Bearded Vulture
0253.desc: The bearded vulture is a large vulture with black bristles like a beard beneath its bill. Unusually, it eats mostly bone — it carries large bones high into the air, drops them onto rock to crack them, and eats the marrow inside.
facts: It is one of the very few birds whose diet is mostly bone.
It carries large bones high up and drops them onto rock to crack them open for the marrow.
It enjoys dust baths, which can stain its white feathers a rusty red.

0266.name: Ruby-throated Hummingbird
0266.desc: This is a tiny hummingbird whose male has a throat that glows ruby red. It beats its wings dozens of times a second so it can hold still in the air. It sips nectar from flowers with its long bill.
facts: It crosses the Gulf of Mexico in a single non-stop flight.
It beats its wings more than 50 times a second.
It weighs less than a single coin.

0279.name: Pheasant-tailed Jacana
0279.nickname: The bird that walks on lily pads
0279.desc: This is a bird with such long toes that it can walk across lily pads floating on the water. In summer it grows a long tail like a pheasant's. Remarkably, it is the father who incubates the eggs and cares for the chicks.
facts: Its long toes let it walk across lily pads on the water.
The male incubates the eggs and raises the chicks.
In summer it grows a long tail like a pheasant's.

0283.name: Eurasian Curlew
0283.desc: This is a large wader with a very long, downcurved bill. It pushes that bill deep into tidal mud to pull out crabs and worms. It calls with a plaintive, curling cry.
facts: It pulls food from the mud with its long, curved bill.
It is among the largest waders in Europe.
Its numbers are falling as tidal flats disappear.

0286.name: Arctic Tern

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Arctic Ocean · Southern Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Pacific Ocean

0286.desc: This is the bird that travels the greatest distance on Earth. It goes from the Arctic to the Antarctic and back again. As a result it enjoys more summer and more daylight than any other bird all year round.
facts: Travelling between the Arctic and Antarctic, it migrates farther than any animal on Earth.
Over a lifetime it flies the equivalent of several round trips to the Moon.
It sees more daylight and more summer than any other bird on Earth.

0296.name: Greater White-fronted Goose

info.서식지: River & lake · Grassland · Asia · Europe · North America

0296.desc: This goose is recognised by the white patch on the forehead above the bill. Its belly carries black barring. In winter it arrives in Korea's rice fields and wetlands in great flocks.
facts: The white forehead is what gives this goose its name.
It is one of the best-known winter migrants to Korea.
It travels together in family groups.

0297.name: Brant Goose

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Asia · Europe · North America · Arctic Ocean

0297.nickname: The black goose of the shore
0297.desc: This is a small goose with a black head and neck. It has a small white necklace marking on the throat. It is well known for feeding on seaweed along the shore.
facts: Unlike other geese it feeds mainly on coastal seaweed.
It raises its young on the Arctic tundra and comes south for winter.
It wears a small white necklace marking on the neck.

0304.name: Chameleon
0304.desc: The chameleon is a special lizard that can change the colour of its skin. It usually lives up in trees and catches insects with its long tongue. It can even use colour changes to show its mood.
facts: It can change its skin colour to match its surroundings.
Its tongue can stretch up to 380 millimetres to snatch prey in an instant.
Most chameleons spend their lives up in the trees.

0306.name: Komodo Dragon
0306.desc: The Komodo dragon is a truly huge lizard. It lives on islands in Indonesia and can grow up to 3 metres long. Its saliva contains venom that it uses when hunting. It can live for around 30 years.
facts: It is the largest lizard species in the world.
Its saliva carries venomous bacteria that it uses when hunting.
It can run at speeds of up to 20 kilometres per hour.

0307.name: Snake

info.서식지: Asia · Europe · North America · South America · Africa · Oceania

0307.desc: The snake is a long, slender animal with no legs. It lives in almost every corner of the world. Snakes usually travel alone, and they use their tongue to smell out prey.
facts: It is a reptile with no legs at all.
Its tongue is used to detect smells.
Its venom paralyses prey and helps with digestion.

0309.name: Amur Softshell Turtle
0309.desc: The Amur softshell turtle is a turtle that breathes underwater and swims very well. It has webbing between its toes. It mostly lives near water and in wetlands.
facts: It can breathe while underwater.
Webbing between its toes makes it a strong swimmer.
It mostly lives near water and in wetlands.

0311.name: Rattlesnake
0311.desc: The rattlesnake is a snake that makes a rattling sound with the tip of its tail. It mostly lives in the desert and uses its fangs to catch prey. The rattling sound is meant to scare enemies away.
facts: The horny rings at the tip of its tail rattle to warn enemies away.
It mostly lives in the desert and lifts its body to avoid touching the hot ground.
When it isn't using its fangs, it folds them back into its mouth.

0323.name: Frilled Lizard
0323.desc: When startled, it opens the large frill around its neck like an umbrella. This makes its body look much bigger and scares off enemies. If that doesn't work, it stands on its hind legs, opens its mouth, and runs away. It spends its time in trees waiting for insects.
facts: It spreads its neck frill open like an umbrella.
It runs away on its hind legs.
The spread frill makes its body look much larger.

0326.name: Komodo Dragon
0326.desc: This is the largest lizard on Earth, living on Komodo Island. Its venomous saliva makes its bite extremely dangerous. Its sense of smell is so sharp it can detect scents from 3 kilometres away. It is a powerful hunter that can even take down animals as large as deer.
facts: It is the largest lizard in the world.
Its venomous saliva makes its bite very dangerous.
It can smell prey from up to 3 kilometres away.

0327.name: Frilled-necked Lizard
0327.nickname: The lizard wearing a frilled collar
0327.desc: This lizard wears a wide, frilled collar around its neck. To scare off threats, it spreads the frill open to look bigger. If that doesn't work, it stands on its hind legs and dashes away. It lives among the forests and trees of Australia.
facts: It spreads its frill open like an umbrella to threaten enemies.
It stands on its hind legs to run away.
It once appeared on Australia's two-cent coin.

0328.name: Blue-tongued Skink
0328.desc: When it opens its mouth, out comes a surprisingly bright blue tongue. When it meets an enemy, it sticks out its blue tongue to startle them. It has very short legs and a plump, gentle body. It loves eating snails and fruit.
facts: Its tongue is a vivid blue colour.
It sticks out its blue tongue to startle enemies.
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.

0329.name: Skink

info.서식지: Grassland · Desert · Near cities · Asia · Africa · Oceania

0329.desc: Its scales are so smooth they shine and glisten. Its short legs make it slither along almost like a snake. When in danger, it has the trick of dropping its tail to escape. It hides under fallen leaves or between rocks, hunting insects.
facts: Its smooth scales give it a glossy shine.
Its short legs make it move almost like a snake.
Its dropped tail grows back over time.

0331.name: Amur Rat Snake
0331.desc: This is one of the largest snakes found in Korea. It has no venom, so it poses no danger to people. It's a helpful snake that eats rats, aiding farmers. It also climbs trees well and squeezes prey with its body.
facts: It is one of the largest snakes in Korea.
It has no venom, so it is safe for people.
It helps farmers by hunting rats.

0339.name: Milk Snake

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · North America · South America

0339.desc: The milk snake is a beautiful snake with red, black and yellow bands. It mimics the dangerous coral snake to trick enemies. In truth, it has no venom and is a gentle snake.
facts: It mimics a venomous snake.
It has no venom.
It is mostly active at night.

0340.name: Garter Snake

info.서식지: River & lake · Near cities · North America

0340.desc: The garter snake is a small snake with long stripes running down its body. It's a common sight in gardens and near water. Unusually, it gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
facts: It gives birth to live young.
It has beautiful stripes.
It tolerates cold weather well.

0341.name: Red-backed Rat Snake
0341.desc: This is a snake that lives in Korea. It loves living near water and is a strong swimmer. It has no venom and eats frogs and fish, giving birth to live young.
facts: It lives in Korea.
It is a strong swimmer.
It gives birth to live young.

0343.name: Red-eared Slider

info.서식지: River & lake · Near cities · North America

0343.desc: The red-eared slider is a turtle with a red mark beside its ear. It swims in water and dries its shell in the sun. It is a popular pet, but it should never be released into the wild carelessly.
facts: It has a red mark beside its ear.
It basks in the sun to dry its shell.
It is a popular pet.

0350.name: Tuatara
0350.desc: The tuatara looks like a lizard, but it isn't one — it's a very special reptile. It's the only surviving member of a group that lived alongside the dinosaurs long ago. It lives only on islands in New Zealand and hunts insects at night.
facts: A hidden 'third eye' on top of its head can sense light.
It is one of the few reptiles that can stay active in very cold weather.
It can live for more than 100 years.

0374.name: Spiny Softshell Turtle
0374.desc: This softshell turtle has small spiny bumps at the front of its shell. It lives in the rivers of North America and has a flat, soft shell like a pancake. It's an excellent swimmer.
facts: It has small spines at the front of its shell.
Its shell is flat and soft.
It buries itself in sand to hide.

0405.name: Green Basilisk
0405.desc: This green lizard from Central America can run across the surface of water on its hind legs when in danger. That is why it is nicknamed the 'Jesus lizard.'
facts: It is called the 'Jesus lizard' because it runs across water on its hind legs.
Its wide back feet and fast speed keep it from sinking.
It is also a strong swimmer and can dive underwater.

0410.name: Flying Dragon
0410.nickname: The gliding dragon of the forest
0410.desc: This small lizard from the forests of Southeast Asia spreads flaps of skin attached to its ribs to glide between trees. It mostly eats ants.
facts: It spreads rib-supported skin flaps to glide between trees.
It can glide more than 8 metres in a single leap.
A yellow flag-like pouch under its throat is used to send signals.

0423.name: Eastern Blue-tongued Skink
0423.nickname: The lizard with a blue tongue
0423.desc: This is a plump skink from Australia. When startled or threatened, it sticks out its bright blue tongue to surprise the attacker. It has short legs and a chubby body.
facts: When threatened, it flashes its blue tongue to startle predators.
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.
Its legs are so short it moves almost like a snake.

0434.name: Common Krait
0434.nickname: The striped snake of the night
0434.desc: This snake lives in India and South Asia. It has white stripes on its body and is mostly active at night. It is a quiet snake, but its venom is extremely powerful.
facts: It readily preys on other snakes.
It is a nocturnal snake, active mainly at night.
It has several pale crossbands running along its body.

0435.name: Coastal Taipan
0435.nickname: The mighty hunter of Australia's coast
0435.desc: This large snake lives along the coasts of Australia and New Guinea. It is very fast and carries powerful venom. It mainly hunts small mammals such as rats.
facts: It is one of the largest venomous snakes in Australia.
It strikes its prey repeatedly and quickly to subdue it fast.
It prefers to hunt during the day.

0436.name: Eastern Coral Snake
0436.desc: This small, brightly coloured snake lives in the southeastern United States. Rings of red, yellow and black wrap around its body. It is beautiful, but it carries powerful venom.
facts: There's a saying that if red touches yellow, it's a venomous snake.
It readily preys on other small snakes.
It spends most of its time underground or beneath fallen leaves.

0449.name: Brahminy Blind Snake
0449.desc: This is one of the smallest snakes in the world, and it looks remarkably like an earthworm. Living underground, its eyes have nearly disappeared, so it can barely see. Remarkably, every individual is female, so it can lay eggs entirely on its own.
facts: It is one of the smallest snakes in the world and looks like an earthworm.
Every individual is female, so it can lay eggs without a mate.
It has spread around the world hidden in potted plant soil.

0450.name: Paradise Flying Snake
0450.desc: This remarkable snake lives up in the trees of Southeast Asia. It has no wings, but it flattens its body and moves in an S-shape to glide between trees. It has a beautiful, colourful pattern.
facts: It flattens its body and glides between trees.
It can glide more than 10 metres in a single leap.
It is one of the rare animals that can fly through the air without wings.

0463.name: Ocean Sunfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0463.desc: The ocean sunfish is a large fish that lives in the ocean. Its body is round and flat, and it has no tail. It mainly eats jellyfish, and when it floats at the surface basking in the sun, it looks just like a sunflower.
facts: It can lay more than 300 million eggs at a time.
Its skin is as rough as sandpaper, and other fish use it to scrape off parasites.
It mainly eats jellyfish, which are low in nutrients, so it must eat huge amounts.

0466.name: Chinese Sturgeon
0466.desc: The sturgeon is a large fish that lives in rivers and the sea. It has a long, streamlined body with no scales. Its eggs are used to make caviar, a special delicacy!
facts: It has a streamlined body with no scales.
Its eggs are known as caviar and used as a luxury food ingredient.
It can live in both fresh water and salt water.

0474.name: Smooth Hammerhead

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0474.nickname: The shark with a hammer head
0474.desc: The hammerhead shark is a remarkable shark with a head shaped like a hammer. It travels in large schools through the ocean, feeding on fish. It is highly intelligent and can even detect faint electric currents.
facts: Its head spreads out into a hammer shape, placing its eyes and nostrils at the far ends.
Its electric sense is so keen it can detect currents as faint as one billionth of a volt.
It is often used in high-end cooking, and its fins can sell for more than $100 per kilogram.

0476.name: Oarfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0476.nickname: The ribbon of the deep sea
0476.desc: The oarfish is an extremely long fish that lives deep in the ocean. It has no scales and a silvery body, with a red fin running along the top of its head. It eats fish, and it is occasionally found washed up on beaches.
facts: Its body can grow to an astonishing 8 metres long.
It has no scales and a silvery body, with a distinctive red dorsal fin.
It lives in deep waters and is occasionally washed up on beaches.

0477.name: Swordfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0477.desc: The swordfish is a large fish with a long, blade-like bill. It swims swiftly through the ocean, hunting small fish. It lives in warm seas all around the world.
facts: It uses its long, blade-shaped bill to stun prey.
It is found widely across the warm seas of the world.
It is capable of swimming at very high speed.

0493.name: Wrasse
0493.desc: This sea fish wears bright, colourful patterns. It is active during the day but buries itself in the sand to sleep at night. As some individuals grow, they can even change from female to male.
facts: It buries itself in the sand to sleep at night.
Its body colours are very vivid.
Some individuals change from female to male as they grow.

0495.name: Bluefin Searobin
0495.nickname: The fish that walks the seabed
0495.desc: This curious sea fish can spread its pectoral fins wide open like a big fan. It uses the pointed, leg-like parts of its fins to creep along the seafloor as if walking. Its bright colours make it easy to spot.
facts: It walks along the seabed using leg-like parts of its fins.
It spreads its pectoral fins wide open like a fan.
It can make frog-like sounds using its swim bladder.

0497.name: Righteye Flounder
0497.nickname: The fish with both eyes on one side
0497.desc: This strange sea fish has a very flat body, with both its eyes gathered on one side of its head. It lies flat against the seafloor. It can even change colour to match the bottom and hide.
facts: Both of its eyes sit on the right side of its head.
As a young fish it has an eye on each side, but one eye moves over as it grows.
It changes its body colour to match the seafloor and stay hidden.

0499.name: Mandarinfish
0499.nickname: The colourful jewel of the sea
0499.desc: This tiny fish is dazzlingly colourful, with swirls of blue, orange and green covering its body. It swims slowly among the coral reefs. Many people consider it one of the most beautiful fish in the world.
facts: Swirling, colourful patterns cover its body.
Instead of scales, a slippery layer of mucus covers its skin.
At dusk, paired fish rise up together in the water to find a mate.

0502.name: Yellow Boxfish
0502.desc: This yellow fish has a body so hard and square it looks just like a box. As a youngster, its yellow body is dotted with black spots, making it especially cute.
facts: Its body is covered in hard bony plates that make it as sturdy as a box.
It releases a toxic substance from its skin when threatened.
It is famously said to have inspired a car design.

0504.name: Japanese Sillago
0504.nickname: Silver darter of the sandy shallows
0504.desc: This fish has a slender, silvery body. It lives along shallow, sandy beaches, picking out tiny bits of food hidden in the sand.
facts: When startled, it quickly buries itself in the sand.
It is a popular summer catch for anglers.
It swims in schools near the sandy seabed.

0507.name: Japanese Halfbeak
0507.desc: This funny-looking fish has a long, slender body with only its lower jaw stretched out like a needle. It swims near the surface, snapping up small food.
facts: The tip of its lower jaw has a reddish tint.
It sometimes leaps above the surface when startled.
In spring, it lays its eggs attached to seaweed.

0509.name: Korean Rockfish
0509.desc: This fish is better known by its common name, 'wooreok.' It hides in crevices between rocks and darts out to snatch up any prey that passes by.
facts: It gives birth to live young rather than laying eggs.
Its fins carry sharp, spiny rays.
Known as wooreok, it is popular both as sashimi and in spicy stew.

0510.name: Fat Greenling
0510.nickname: The greenling of the rocky shallows
0510.desc: This fish lives in shallow water rich with rocks and seaweed. Its body colour blends in so well with its surroundings that it nearly disappears when hiding among the rocks.
facts: It has as many as five lateral lines running along its body.
Males guard the eggs after they are laid.
Known as 'noraemi,' it is a popular target for anglers.

0512.name: Sea Raven
0512.desc: This fish has a big head and a lumpy, bumpy body that looks a little homely. But it makes a wonderfully refreshing and tasty broth.
facts: Its skin is covered with whisker-like bumps.
It is a popular ingredient for winter soup dishes.
It sits still on the seafloor, waiting for food to come by.

0513.name: Japanese Pufferfish
0513.desc: Among all pufferfish, this one is considered the most prized. It carries a strong poison inside its body, so only trained experts are allowed to prepare it as food.
facts: It carries a strong poison that is dangerous to humans.
It puffs up its belly greatly when it feels threatened.
It is ranked as the finest ingredient among pufferfish dishes.

0515.name: Clark's Anemonefish
0515.nickname: The little clown of the anemone
0515.desc: This colourful fish lives tucked safely inside sea anemones. It is not harmed by the anemone's stinging tentacles, which makes the anemone a perfectly safe home.
facts: It is a special fish that is not stung by the anemone's venomous tentacles.
The largest fish in a group becomes the female.
It is a close relative of the clownfish made famous as 'Nemo.'

0516.name: Striated Frogfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0516.nickname: The hunter with a fishing rod
0516.desc: This strange fish has a lure shaped like a tiny fishing rod on its head. It wiggles the lure to attract other fish, then swallows them in an instant.
facts: It wiggles the lure on its head to attract prey.
It can change its colour and pattern to blend into its surroundings.
It moves along the seafloor using its fins like little legs.

0517.name: Red Stingray
0517.nickname: The ray with a hidden sting
0517.desc: This ray has a broad, flat body. Its long tail carries a venomous spine, which it uses to defend itself whenever it senses danger.
facts: Its tail carries a sharp, venomous spine.
It buries its body in the sand to hide.
It gives birth to live young instead of laying eggs.

0519.name: West African Lungfish
0519.nickname: The fish that breathes with lungs
0519.desc: This remarkable fish can breathe using lungs as well as gills. When its water dries up, it burrows into the mud and sleeps there for a long time to survive.
facts: It has lungs, so it can breathe even out of water.
During the dry season, it can sleep in the mud for several months.
Its fins are thin and thread-like.

0521.name: Silver Arowana
0521.desc: This fish has a long, shining silver body. It can leap high above the water's surface, even snatching insects off overhanging branches, which is how it earned the nickname 'jumping fish.'
facts: It can leap nearly two metres above the water to catch prey.
The male protects the eggs and young inside its mouth.
Its large, shining silver scales are why it is called a 'dragon.'

0522.name: Asian Arowana
0522.nickname: The lucky dragon of the water
0522.desc: This fish shines brilliantly in red and gold. In Asia, it is believed to bring good luck, so it is treated as an especially precious fish.
facts: Believed to bring good fortune, it is traded at very high prices.
It is an endangered species protected under international law.
The male raises its young inside its mouth.

0525.name: Freshwater Angelfish
0525.desc: This fish swims gracefully, its long fins spread out above and below its body. It is called the 'angelfish' because it looks as beautiful as an angel.
facts: Its long fins, stretching up and down, look very elegant.
The parents take great care of their eggs and young.
It lays its eggs attached to leaves or rocks.

0526.name: Siamese Fighting Fish
0526.desc: This small fish spreads its colourful fins wide open. When two males meet, neither backs down, and they fight fiercely — which is how it got the name 'fighting fish.' It can even breathe air directly from above the water's surface.
facts: A special organ above its gills lets it breathe air directly from above the water.
The male builds a nest of bubbles to guard the eggs.
Its bright colours and fins make it a popular aquarium fish worldwide.

0527.name: Redtail Catfish
0527.desc: This is a very large catfish living in the Amazon River. It gets its name from its orange-and-red tail. Its mouth is so big that it can swallow prey whole.
facts: Its mouth is so large it can swallow prey that is big relative to its own body.
It uses its long whiskers to find food in the dark water.
It starts out small as a young fish but grows into a giant over one metre long as an adult.

0534.name: Big-scaled Redfin
0534.desc: This carp-family fish moves between the sea and rivers. Every spring, it swims upstream to lay its eggs, and at this time a red stripe appears along its body.
facts: It lives in the sea, then swims upstream in spring to lay its eggs.
A clear red stripe appears along its side during the breeding season.
Although it is a carp-family fish, it can tolerate seawater.

0535.name: Red-bellied Piranha
0535.desc: This Amazon fish has a red belly and razor-sharp teeth. It travels in groups, feeding on injured animals or fish. Despite its fierce reputation, it is actually quite timid.
facts: It uses its saw-like, sharp teeth to slice up its food.
It travels in groups, watching out for one another.
Although it looks fearsome, it is actually quite timid and often hides.

0536.name: Humpback Anglerfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0536.nickname: The lantern hunter of the deep sea
0536.desc: This fish lives in the deep, pitch-black ocean. A glowing lure at the tip of a rod above its head attracts prey in the darkness. It has a large mouth filled with sharp teeth.
facts: The glowing lure at the tip of its head-rod attracts prey in the dark sea.
The light is produced by glowing bacteria that live inside the lure.
The male is tiny and attaches himself permanently to the female's body.

0537.name: Blobfish
0537.desc: This soft, squishy fish lives deep in the ocean. Underwater it looks fairly ordinary, but out of the water its body sags into a droopy, melted-looking shape. It drifts along using almost no effort at all.
facts: Its body is slightly lighter than water, letting it drift along the bottom with almost no effort.
It looks ordinary in the deep sea but appears droopy and melted once out of the water.
It waits for food to drift by, then opens its mouth to swallow it.

0538.name: Red-lipped Batfish
0538.desc: This unusual fish has lips as bright red as lipstick. It is not a strong swimmer, so instead it walks along the seafloor using its fins. It lives in the waters near the Galapagos Islands.
facts: The exact reason for its bright red lips is not yet known.
Instead of swimming, it walks along the bottom using its fins.
It is known to use a bump on its head to lure in prey.

0542.name: Lemon Shark
0542.desc: This shark has a yellowish, lemon-coloured body, which is how it got its name. It loves warm, shallow seas and mangrove forests. It travels in groups and is considered fairly gentle for a shark.
facts: Its body is as yellowish as a lemon.
It swims together in groups.
It likes to rest quietly on the shallow seafloor.

0547.name: Japanese Bullhead Shark
0547.desc: This small shark has a big, blunt head. It crawls slowly along the seafloor. It uses its sturdy teeth to crush hard prey such as sea snails and sea urchins.
facts: It has a big, blunt head.
It crushes shellfish and sea urchins with its sturdy teeth.
It lays strange, spiral-shaped eggs.

0548.name: Spotted Wobbegong
0548.desc: This curious shark lies flat against the seafloor. Its patterned skin matches the bottom so well that it looks just like a carpet. Whisker-like flaps of skin around its mouth give it the nickname 'bearded shark.'
facts: Its pattern matches the seafloor so perfectly that it hides like a carpet.
Whisker-like flaps of skin hang around its mouth.
It lies hidden and still, then swallows approaching prey in an instant.

0551.name: Japanese Sawshark
0551.nickname: The sea detective with a saw
0551.desc: This shark has a long snout shaped like a saw. It uses the whiskers and senses along the saw to find prey hidden in the sand. It is not dangerous to people.
facts: A pair of long whiskers halfway along its saw-like snout helps it feel out prey.
It stirs up the sand with its saw to flush out hidden prey.
Although it is a shark, it is a gentle species that does not attack people.

0552.name: Smalltooth Sawfish

info.서식지: Ocean · River & lake · Atlantic Ocean · North America

0552.nickname: The hunter that swings a saw
0552.desc: This ray has a long, saw-like snout lined with tightly packed teeth. It swings the saw sideways to stun fish before catching them. It is now a very rare, endangered animal.
facts: Its long saw has special sensors that can detect faint electrical signals from prey.
It swings the saw side to side to strike and catch schools of fish.
Although it looks like a shark, it actually belongs to the ray family.

0553.name: Common Torpedo
0553.desc: This ray can generate strong electricity inside its body. It fires off an electric shock to catch prey or drive away enemies. Blue, eye-like spots decorate its back.
facts: Special organs inside its body can generate up to 200 volts of electricity.
It stuns prey with electricity before swallowing it.
It hides in the sand during the day and hunts at night.

0556.name: Ocellate Spot Skate
0556.desc: This fish, a member of the ray family, has a broad, flat, diamond-shaped body. It lives close to the seafloor, feeding on small animals. It is a fish that Koreans enjoy eating.
facts: It lays its eggs inside a tough, leathery pouch on the seafloor.
Unlike stingrays, it has no venomous spine on its tail.
Round, eye-like markings decorate its back.

0557.name: Giant Oceanic Manta Ray

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0557.nickname: The giant wing that flies through the sea
0557.desc: This enormous ray can have a wingspan of up to seven metres. It glides gracefully through the ocean as if flying with its huge body. Despite its great size, it filters out only tiny plankton to eat.
facts: It uses fins near its head to funnel water and filter plankton into its mouth.
It sometimes leaps several metres out of the water.
For a fish, it has a remarkably large brain, making it quite an intelligent animal.

0561.name: Bulgyhead Wrasse
0561.nickname: The rock hunter with a bulging forehead
0561.desc: Older males of this fish develop a bulging forehead and jaw. It moves among rock crevices, crushing hard shellfish and sea urchins to eat. It lives in the rocky waters off Korea's southern coast.
facts: It uses its sturdy teeth to crack open even conch and abalone shells.
All individuals are born female, and some change into males as they grow.
It prefers to live alone, guarding its own territory.

0562.name: Oriental Butterflyfish
0562.nickname: The fish in yellow butterfly wings
0562.desc: This pretty butterflyfish has a yellow body marked with thin stripes. It flutters gracefully between coral reefs and rocks, pecking at small invertebrates and algae.
facts: A black stripe across its eye confuses predators.
Its pointed mouth lets it pick out prey hidden in rock crevices.
It is often seen swimming in pairs.

0563.name: Pennant Coralfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0563.nickname: The black-and-white fish with a flag
0563.desc: This fish has one long dorsal fin ray that trails out like a white flag. It has bold black-and-white stripes and swims in groups above coral reefs.
facts: Its long, trailing fin flutters like a flag, earning it the nickname 'flag fish.'
It sometimes picks parasites off other fish.
It sometimes mimics the cleaner wrasse in order to approach other fish.

0564.name: Queen Angelfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Atlantic Ocean · North America · South America

0564.nickname: The crowned queen of the sea
0564.desc: This beautiful fish combines striking yellow and blue colours. A crown-like pattern of blue spots on its head is how it earned the name 'queen.' It grazes on sponges around coral reefs.
facts: The blue spot pattern on its forehead looks just like a crown, giving it the name 'queen.'
Its pattern looks completely different as a juvenile compared to when it is an adult.
It forms pairs and guards its own territory together.

0565.name: Emperor Angelfish

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0565.desc: This striking fish is covered in tight yellow and blue stripes, dazzling like a prince of the coral reef. As a juvenile it has swirling patterns that change into stripes as it grows.
facts: As a juvenile, it looks completely different, with white swirling patterns on a blue background.
It can make a thudding sound to startle enemies.
It stays within its own set territory.

0568.name: Moorish Idol

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0568.nickname: The black-white-yellow beauty with a flagpole
0568.desc: This fish has black, white and yellow stripes and a dorsal fin that trails out like a flagpole. It is one of the fish most associated with coral reefs, using its pointed snout to pick food out of rock crevices.
facts: One dorsal fin ray trails out long like a thread.
It resembles the pennant coralfish but actually belongs to a different family.
Its pointed mouth lets it pick food out of narrow rock crevices very well.

0569.name: Sapphire Devil

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0569.desc: This small blue fish sparkles like a sapphire gem. It swims quickly among coral reefs. Though tiny, it bravely guards its own territory.
facts: Its blue body sparkles differently depending on the angle of the light.
Males turn orange in the face during mating season.
Though small, it defends its patch of reef with great spirit.

0571.name: Bluestreak Cleaner Wrasse

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0571.desc: This small fish picks parasites and dead skin off other fish. Even big fish line up calmly to be cleaned. That's why it is called the cleaner of the sea.
facts: It sets up its own 'cleaning station' and waits for customer fish to arrive.
The blue stripe on its body signals 'I'm a cleaner' to other fish.
It swims right inside the mouths of big fish to clean them, yet is never eaten.

0572.name: Threespot Dascyllus

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0572.nickname: The black baby with three spots
0572.desc: This small fish has a black body with three white spots. When young, it hides among sea anemones or coral. It swims in groups and feeds on plankton.
facts: As a youngster it has three clear white spots on its forehead and sides.
Like the clownfish, it sometimes shelters beside sea anemones for protection.
As it grows, the white spots fade and its body turns greyish.

0573.name: Common Bluestripe Snapper

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0573.desc: This fish has a yellow body marked with neat blue stripes. It swims around coral reefs in large groups. During the day it stays together, then spreads out at night to feed.
facts: It has four blue stripes running side by side along its body.
Hundreds of fish gather together to form a beautiful yellow shoal.
It rests by day and is active at night, scattering to hunt for food.

0574.name: Laced Moray

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Asia

0574.desc: This large moray eel has a white body covered in a tight, honeycomb-like pattern of black blotches. By day it hides in rocky crevices, opening and closing its mouth. At night it comes out to hunt fish and octopus.
facts: Opening and closing its mouth isn't a threat display, it's simply how it breathes.
A second set of jaws deep in its throat pulls prey inward.
It hides in its den by day and hunts at night.

0575.name: Green Moray

info.서식지: Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · North America · South America

0575.nickname: Master of the green rock den
0575.desc: This large moray eel looks green all over. Its skin is actually blue, but a coating of yellow mucus makes it appear green. It hides in a rocky den and hunts at night.
facts: It looks green, but its true skin colour underneath is blue.
A layer of yellow mucus mixes with the blue skin to create the green appearance.
It hides in its den by day and comes out to hunt at night.

0576.name: Ribbon Eel

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

0576.desc: This moray eel has a long, thin body like a ribbon. As it grows, its colour changes from black to blue to yellow, and it often starts life as a male before becoming female. Its flared, fan-shaped nostrils are a distinctive feature.
facts: Its body colour changes from black to blue to yellow as it grows.
Most are born male and later change sex to female.
It hides its body in a sandy burrow, leaving only its head poking out.

0577.name: Spotted Garden Eel
0577.nickname: Blades of grass in the sand
0577.desc: This small eel buries half its body in the sandy seabed, leaving only its head poking out. When many gather together, they look like a garden of swaying grass, which is how it got its name. If danger comes near, it vanishes into the sand in an instant.
facts: Hundreds gather together and look like a garden.
It feeds on plankton carried past by the current.
It is very timid and disappears completely into the sand at the first sign of danger.

0582.name: Barred Knifejaw
0582.desc: This striking fish has seven thick black bands across its body. Its mouth is as hard as a bird's beak, letting it crack open sea urchins and conch shells. It is famous among anglers as a tough fish that is hard to reel in.
facts: It cracks open sea urchins and shellfish with its hard, beak-like teeth.
The stripes are bold when it is young but fade as it grows.
It is so strong that it is nicknamed the wrestler of the sea.

0586.name: Longtooth Grouper
0586.desc: This prized grouper is known in Jeju as 'dageumbari'. Its long teeth and large body give it great strength. It hides in deep rocky dens and takes a long time to reach full size.
facts: It is ranked as one of the finest fish for eating in Jeju.
It grows slowly, taking a long time to reach adulthood.
It makes its home in a deep rocky den and stays hidden.

0588.name: Malabar Grouper

info.서식지: Ocean · River & lake · Rainforest · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

0588.desc: This large grouper is covered in brown blotches and small spots. It moves between coral reefs and river mouths, hiding among the rocks to wait for prey. It lives in shallow water when young and moves to deeper seas as it grows.
facts: Its body is densely covered in brown blotches and spots.
It lives in river mouths when young and moves to deep water as an adult.
It hides among the rocks and waits for prey to pass by.

0589.name: Yellowtail Amberjack

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0589.nickname: Speed king with a yellow tail
0589.desc: This fast sea fish has a sleek body and a bright yellow tail. It swims quickly through open water in groups, hunting small fish. It is a favourite among anglers for its strength and its skill at escaping the line.
facts: Its yellow tail fin makes it easy to spot.
It swims and hunts quickly in groups.
It closely resembles the greater amberjack but is a little slimmer.

0593.name: Live Sharksucker

info.서식지: Ocean · Rainforest · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0593.nickname: The hitchhiker with a sucker on its head
0593.desc: This fish has a flat sucker on top of its head that lets it attach to sharks, turtles, or the bottom of boats. By hitching a ride on larger animals, it travels for free and picks up scraps of leftover food. Despite its name, it is not actually a shark.
facts: It attaches to sharks or turtles using the sucker on its head.
It travels effortlessly by riding along on larger animals.
Despite having 'shark' in its name, it is not a shark at all.

0594.name: African Pompano

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0594.desc: As a juvenile, this fish has fins that trail out long and thin like threads. These threads look like jellyfish tentacles, which confuses predators. As it grows, the threads shorten and its body becomes broad and silvery.
facts: As a juvenile, its fins trail out long like threads.
The long threads resemble jellyfish tentacles and fool predators.
As it matures, the threads shorten and its body becomes broad and silvery.

0595.name: Skipjack Tuna

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0595.nickname: The tuna with stripes on its belly
0595.desc: This small tuna has dark vertical stripes running along its belly. It swims extremely fast, travelling the open ocean in large schools. It is also well known as the key ingredient in the Japanese food katsuobushi.
facts: It has several dark vertical stripes along its belly.
It must keep swimming constantly in order to breathe.
It is the main ingredient used to make katsuobushi (dried bonito flakes).

0596.name: Yellowfin Tuna

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0596.desc: This large tuna is easy to spot thanks to its bright yellow fins. Its streamlined body lets it swim as fast as a bullet. It travels the open ocean in groups, hunting for food.
facts: Its bright yellow fins are how it got its name.
It swims as fast as a speeding bullet.
It must keep swimming constantly in order to breathe.

0597.name: Albacore

info.서식지: Ocean · Pacific Ocean · Atlantic Ocean · Indian Ocean

0597.desc: This fish gets its name from its pectoral fins, which are as long as wings. It copes well with cold water and travels vast distances across the open ocean. It is also widely used as the fish in canned tuna.
facts: Its pectoral fins are extremely long, like a pair of wings.
It tolerates cold water well and migrates over huge distances.
It is one of the fish most commonly used for canned tuna.

0599.name: Thread-sail Filefish
0599.nickname: The small fish with a rat's teeth
0599.desc: This fish has a small mouth with teeth that resemble a rat's, which is how it got its Korean name, meaning 'rat fish'. Its body is flat with a sharp spine on its back. Its skin is so rough that people once used it like sandpaper, and it is well known as the main ingredient in dried filefish snacks.
facts: It has a small mouth with rat-like teeth.
Its rough skin was once used like sandpaper.
It is well known as the main ingredient of dried filefish snacks.

0600.name: Black Scraper
0600.nickname: The tall cousin of the filefish family
0600.desc: This fish resembles the thread-sail filefish but has a larger, longer body. It is dark grey with a sharp spine on its back. It swims in groups and happily eats jellyfish. It is widely used as the main ingredient in dried filefish snacks.
facts: Its body is larger and longer than the thread-sail filefish.
It readily eats jellyfish, stingers and all.
It is the main ingredient used to make dried filefish snacks.

0604.name: Salamander

info.서식지: North America · South America · Europe · Asia

0604.desc: Salamanders are fascinating animals that can live in both water and on land. Some kinds are even kept as pets. They lay eggs, and when young they can only live in the water.
facts: Salamanders can live both in water and on land.
The axolotl is a special kind of salamander that is popular as a pet.
Young salamanders have gills that let them live only in water.

0608.name: Newt

info.서식지: River & lake · Asia · Europe · North America

0608.desc: Newts are animals that look like small salamanders. They move back and forth between water and land. They can be found in many places around the world.
facts: Newts live both in water and on land.
They are found in North America, Europe and Asia.
More than 100 species of newt have been identified.

0617.name: Spectacled Salamander
0617.desc: The spectacled salamander is a small salamander from Italy with a spectacle-like marking on its head. When startled, it flashes its red belly as a warning to stay away. It is also unusual in having only four toes.
facts: It has a marking on its head that looks like a pair of spectacles.
When startled, it flashes its red belly.
It lives only in Italy.

0627.name: Argentine Horned Frog
0627.desc: Its mouth is so huge that it makes up about half its body. It has small horn-like points above its eyes. It stays hidden and still on the ground, then swallows passing prey whole in one gulp.
facts: Its mouth is as big as half its whole body.
It has small horns above its eyes.
It is nicknamed the Pac-Man frog.

0628.name: Amazonian Horned Frog
0628.nickname: The hunter that hides like a leaf
0628.desc: Long, sharp horns above its eyes let it disappear almost completely among fallen leaves. It lies still on the floor of the Amazon forest, waiting for prey. Its mouth is so big it can swallow prey as large as its own body.
facts: The horns above its eyes are long and sharp.
It hides among fallen leaves and waits for prey.
It can swallow prey as large as its own body.

0629.name: African Common Toad
0629.nickname: The common toad of the savanna
0629.desc: This toad is a very common sight across African grasslands and near villages. At night it wanders about hunting for insects. When rain falls, it lays a huge number of eggs in puddles.
facts: It is a very common toad throughout Africa.
It wanders about at night hunting for insects.
It lays a huge number of eggs whenever rain falls.

0631.name: Titicaca Water Frog
0631.nickname: The wrinkly frog of the lake
0631.desc: This frog lives only in Lake Titicaca, high up in the mountains. Its skin has many wrinkles and folds that help it breathe using oxygen from the water. It rarely comes out onto land, spending its whole life in the water.
facts: Its wrinkled skin lets it breathe underwater.
It lives only in a high mountain lake.
It is nicknamed the 'scrotum frog' because of its baggy, wrinkled skin.

0632.name: Darwin's Frog
0632.nickname: The father that raises babies in his mouth
0632.desc: This frog has a pointed snout that makes it look like a leaf. The father frog keeps the eggs inside his mouth, protecting them until they become froglets. It is named after the famous scientist Darwin, who first discovered it.
facts: The father raises the young inside his own mouth.
Its pointed snout makes it look like a leaf.
It was first discovered by the scientist Charles Darwin.

0633.name: Gastric-brooding Frog
0633.nickname: The frog that raised babies in her stomach
0633.desc: This astonishing frog's mother swallowed her eggs and raised the young inside her own stomach. Once fully grown, the babies were brought back up and out through her mouth into the world. Sadly, this frog is now extinct and can no longer be seen.
facts: The mother raised her young inside her own stomach.
The fully grown young were released by being brought back up through her mouth.
It is now extinct and can no longer be found.

0636.name: Common Toad
0636.desc: This toad is a common sight all across Europe. During the day it hides in soil or under stones, then hunts insects at night. When spring comes, it returns to the pond where it was born to lay its eggs.
facts: It is a very common toad in Europe.
It hunts insects at night.
In spring it returns to its birth pond to lay eggs.

0637.name: European Green Toad
0637.desc: This toad has green blotches on its body that let it hide well in grassy areas. At night it sings out with a beautiful call. It tolerates dry conditions well and lives in many different places.
facts: Its green blotches help it hide in the grass.
It sings a beautiful call at night.
It tolerates dry conditions well.

0638.name: Toad
0638.desc: This toad has a long, pointed snout. At night it wanders about slowly, hunting for insects. Its skin is bumpy and rough to the touch.
facts: Its snout juts out to a sharp point.
It is active at night.
Its skin is bumpy and rough.

0641.name: Black-spotted Pond Frog
0641.nickname: The green frog of the rice paddies
0641.desc: This green frog has black spots dotting its back. It is a common sight in rice paddies and ponds, and one of Korea's most familiar frogs. A bright stripe runs down the middle of its back.
facts: It has black spots on its back.
It is common in rice paddies and ponds.
A bright stripe runs down the middle of its back.

0642.name: Seoul Pond Frog
0642.desc: This pretty frog has two shining golden stripes running along its back. It is a precious species found only in Korea, so it needs to be well protected. It likes to spend its time in the water of ponds and rice paddies.
facts: It has two golden stripes along its back.
It is a frog found only in Korea.
It likes spending time in the water.

0643.name: Oriental Fire-bellied Toad
0643.desc: This frog has a green back but a belly covered in vivid red markings. When threatened, it flips onto its back to flash its red belly, warning that it is poisonous. It is a clever creature that uses bright colours to warn off predators.
facts: Its belly is covered in vivid red markings.
When threatened, it flips over to show its red belly.
Its skin carries poison.

0646.name: Tomato Frog
0646.nickname: The frog as red as a tomato
0646.desc: This frog has a plump, bright red body like a ripe tomato. It lives only on the African island of Madagascar. When threatened, it releases a sticky white substance to drive predators away.
facts: Its body is red like a ripe tomato.
It lives only on the island of Madagascar.
When threatened, it releases a sticky substance.

0649.name: Surinam Toad
0649.nickname: The toad whose babies hatch from her back
0649.desc: This remarkable toad has a flat body that looks like a leaf. The mother carries the eggs embedded in her back skin, and tiny young pop out from her back once they are ready. It lives in the rivers of South America.
facts: Its body is as flat as a leaf.
The young emerge directly from the mother's back.
It lives only in water.

0650.name: Goliath Frog
0650.nickname: The biggest frog in the world
0650.desc: This is the largest frog in the world, growing as long as a human baby. It lives near fast-flowing rivers in Africa. It is strong enough to move heavy stones to protect its eggs.
facts: It is the largest frog in the world.
It is strong enough to move heavy stones.
It lives in fast-flowing rivers in Africa.

0651.name: American Toad
0651.desc: This toad lives in North America. Though often lumped in with tree frogs by name, it is actually a toad with a bumpy back. It hunts insects at night and sings out in spring.
facts: The substance released from the bumps on its back drives predators away.
In spring, males sing with a long, trilling call.
It is active at night and eats many pests.

0652.name: Tiger Salamander
0652.desc: This large salamander has yellow and black markings like a tiger. It digs burrows underground and lives there. It is one of the largest land salamanders in North America.
facts: Its yellow and black markings resembling a tiger gave it its name.
It spends most of its time in underground burrows.
As a youngster, it grows underwater, breathing through gills.

0655.name: Hellbender
0655.desc: This is the largest salamander in North America. It hides beneath stones in clear streams. Because it breathes through its wrinkled skin, it can only survive where the water is clean.
facts: It is the largest salamander in North America, growing over 60 centimetres long.
The wrinkled skin along its sides absorbs oxygen from the water.
Since it can only live in clean water, it serves as an indicator of water quality.

0656.name: Japanese Fire-bellied Newt
0656.desc: This small newt has a black back and a bright red belly. The vivid red colour is a warning that says, 'I'm poisonous.' It lives in ponds and rice paddies.
facts: The bright red belly is a warning sign of poison.
Its skin carries a mild poison that keeps predators from eating it.
It can regrow a lost leg or tail.

0657.name: Chinese Fire-bellied Newt
0657.desc: This very small newt has a black back and an orange-red belly. It is also widely kept as an ornamental pet. Its red belly is a warning that it carries poison.
facts: It is smaller than the Japanese fire-bellied newt and has a smoother belly.
Its bright red belly warns predators that it is poisonous.
It moves back and forth between water and land.

0658.name: Cranwell's Horned Frog
0658.desc: This frog's mouth is so enormous that it is also nicknamed the 'Pac-Man frog'. It has small horn-like bumps above its eyes. It buries itself halfway into the ground and waits for prey to come near.
facts: Its mouth is as big as half its whole body.
The horn-like bumps above its eyes give it the name 'horned frog'.
It ambushes from a buried position and swallows prey whole.

0659.name: Ornate Horned Frog
0659.desc: This horned frog has vivid green and red markings. It looks similar to Cranwell's horned frog but is even more colourful. Its huge mouth lets it swallow prey as large as itself.
facts: Its green body with red markings makes it very colourful.
Its huge mouth lets it swallow prey as large as its own body.
Its numbers are declining in the wild as its habitat shrinks.

0660.name: Gliding Leaf Frog
0660.nickname: The tree frog that flies through the sky
0660.desc: This green tree frog lives up in the trees. It spreads the wide webbing between its toes to glide smoothly between trees. It is a nocturnal frog of the rainforest.
facts: It spreads its wide webbed feet like a parachute to glide between trees.
It is active at night and rests beneath leaves during the day.
Many frogs lay their eggs together on leaves above pools of water.

0662.name: Eastern Newt
0662.desc: As a youngster it is bright orange and wanders on land, then as an adult it turns green and returns to the water. It has red spots on its body.
facts: As a young "red eft," it is bright orange and wanders on land.
As an adult, it turns green and goes back to living in the water.
Its bright colour and red spots warn predators that it is poisonous.

0663.name: Northern Crested Newt
0663.nickname: The newt with a spiky crest
0663.desc: In the breeding season, males grow a magnificent, saw-toothed crest along their back. They live in European ponds and pools, moving between water and land.
facts: In the breeding season, males grow a saw-toothed crest on their back.
Its belly is orange with black spots.
It releases a mild toxin from its skin to protect itself.

0664.name: Smooth Newt
0664.desc: This small newt can be found almost anywhere in Europe. Its skin is smooth, and every spring it returns to ponds to lay eggs.
facts: It is the most common and widespread newt in Europe.
In the breeding season, males grow a wavy crest along their back.
Females fold pond-plant leaves to wrap each egg individually.

0665.name: Alpine Newt
0665.nickname: The colourful newt with an orange belly
0665.desc: This newt stands out with its bright orange belly. It loves cold mountain ponds and can live high up in the mountains of Europe.
facts: Its belly is a bright, striking orange.
It can live in cold ponds high in the mountains.
Some individuals keep their gills into adulthood and live their whole lives in water.

0666.name: Marbled Newt
0666.nickname: The newt with green marble patterns
0666.desc: Its body has a marbled mix of green and black. It lives in Western European forests and only enters ponds during the breeding season.
facts: Its green-and-black marbled pattern camouflages it.
In the breeding season, males grow a crest with a red stripe.
It spends most of the year in damp places on land.

0667.name: Fire Salamander
0667.desc: This salamander has jet-black skin with yellow markings that flash like flames. Its bold colours warn "I'm poisonous!"
facts: Its yellow markings are a warning colour that signals it is poisonous.
It sprays toxins from its skin to fend off enemies.
Instead of laying eggs, it gives birth to fully formed larvae in water.

0668.name: Northern Spectacled Salamander
0668.desc: It has a marking between its eyes that looks like a pair of glasses, and its belly is bright red. It is a small, special salamander found only in Italian forests.
facts: It has a bright, glasses-like marking on its head.
When threatened, it curls its tail to show off its red belly.
It has three toes instead of the usual four.

0669.name: Olm
0669.nickname: The white baby dragon of the caves
0669.desc: This pale salamander lives in the dark waters of deep caves with no light. Its eyes have nearly disappeared, and it can live for more than 100 years.
facts: It keeps its gills for life and lives in underwater caves.
With no light, its eyes have become useless and its skin is pale white.
It can survive for years without food.

0673.name: Common Mudpuppy
0673.nickname: The water salamander with red gills
0673.desc: It has feathery red gills on each side of its head. It lives its whole life underwater, crawling along the bottoms of rivers and lakes.
facts: It keeps its red external gills for life and lives entirely in water.
It stays active under the ice even in winter.
It crawls along the bottom of murky water to search for food.

0674.name: Greater Siren
0674.nickname: The eel-like salamander with two legs
0674.desc: Its long body looks like an eel, but it has two small front legs and no back legs. It lives on swamp bottoms and sleeps buried in mud during dry seasons.
facts: It has front legs only, with no back legs at all.
During droughts, it burrows into the mud and estivates.
It breathes through external gills and lives in water its entire life.

0675.name: Two-toed Amphiuma
0675.nickname: The eel-like salamander with two toes
0675.desc: Its very long body has four tiny legs like baby hands. It got its name because each foot has only two toes.
facts: Its legs are tiny compared to its body, and each foot has only two toes.
Its jaws are strong enough to deliver a real bite.
During droughts, it digs a burrow in the mud to survive.

0679.name: Gray Treefrog
0679.nickname: The colour-changing frog in the trees
0679.desc: It changes colour between grey and green to hide against tree bark. Sticky pads on its toes help it climb well, and it calls loudly at night.
facts: It can change its body colour between grey and green.
The sticky pads on its toes let it cling to trees and even glass.
Even if part of its body freezes in winter, it comes back to life in spring.

0681.name: Cuban Treefrog
