0681.desc: It is one of the largest treefrogs of all. Originally from Cuba, it has spread to Florida in the United States, where it even eats other frogs.
facts: It is the largest treefrog in North America.
It preys on other frogs and small animals.
It has spread far beyond its original home into new regions.

0682.name: White's Treefrog
0682.nickname: The chubby frog with a smiling face
0682.desc: Its plump, smooth body always looks like it's smiling. It lives in Australia and New Guinea and often turns up near people's homes.
facts: Its round, easy-going look has made it a popular pet frog.
Its skin produces a special substance that fights off germs.
It often wanders into houses, even bathrooms.

0683.name: Red-eyed Treefrog
0683.nickname: The red-eyed star of the jungle
0683.desc: This flashy frog has bright red eyes, a green body, and blue-and-orange sides. It lives on leaves in the jungles of Central America.
facts: When startled, it flashes open its bright red eyes to startle predators.
Its orange toes and blue side markings make it strikingly colourful.
It lays its eggs on leaves, and the tadpoles drop into the water below when they hatch.

0684.name: Spring Peeper
0684.nickname: The tiny peeper that announces spring
0684.desc: This tiny frog has an X-shaped mark on its back. In early spring, its high peeping calls fill the forest.
facts: It has an X-shaped marking on its back.
It calls loudly when spring arrives, earning it the nickname "herald of spring."
Even if its body freezes, it thaws out and comes back to life in spring.

0687.name: Wallace's Flying Frog
0687.nickname: The jungle frog that flies through the sky
0687.desc: It spreads the large webbing between its toes to glide between trees. It lives high in the trees of Southeast Asian rainforests.
facts: It spreads its large webbed feet like a parachute to glide between trees.
Sticky toe pads let it stick to trees when it lands.
It is named after the explorer Alfred Russel Wallace.

0690.name: African Clawed Frog
0690.desc: This frog spends almost its whole life underwater in southern Africa. It has small claws on its hind feet, and since it has no tongue, it pushes food into its mouth with its front feet. It is also widely kept in laboratories for research.
facts: With no tongue, it pushes food into its mouth using its front feet.
In the past, this frog was used for pregnancy tests.
When the water dries up, it can bury itself in mud and survive for a long time.

0691.name: Dwarf Clawed Frog
0691.nickname: The water frog no bigger than a fingernail
0691.desc: This tiny water frog lives in still water in Central Africa. It swims through the water its entire life, and all four feet are webbed. Because it's so small, it is often kept in aquariums.
facts: With webbing on all four feet, it is an excellent swimmer.
It comes up to the surface now and then to breathe.
It is so small that even fully grown, it's about the size of an adult's thumb.

0692.name: Edible Frog
0692.nickname: The frog born from two species
0692.desc: This is a hybrid frog born from the European pool frog and the marsh frog. It has a green body, loves the water's edge, and is often active during the day. It has long been caught for food in Europe.
facts: As a hybrid of two species, it shows traits of both parent frogs.
When it senses danger, it dives quickly into the water.
It likes basking in the sun by the water on warm days.

0707.name: European Green Toad

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Desert · Europe · Asia

0707.nickname: The toad with green camouflage patches
0707.desc: This toad lives in Europe and western Asia and has green patches on a pale background. It tolerates dry conditions well and can live near cities. At night, it calls with a sound like a bird trilling.
facts: It has mottled green patches on a pale background.
It tolerates dry conditions well and can even live near cities.
In the breeding season, males make a trilling call that sounds like birdsong.

0708.name: Rice Field Frog
0708.nickname: The little frog that calls in the rice paddy
0708.desc: This small frog is common in the rice paddies of Southeast and South Asia. Its earth-toned body with a raised ridge along the back helps it blend into the mud. On rainy nights, it calls loudly from the paddies.
facts: Its earth-coloured body makes it hard to spot against the mud.
When it rains, it calls loudly in groups from the rice paddies.
It is a small helper that eats pests harmful to rice crops.

0709.name: Indian Bullfrog
0709.desc: This large frog from South Asia is usually green, but males turn bright yellow during the breeding season. It likes rice paddies and ponds, and its big mouth lets it swallow large prey.
facts: In the breeding season, the male's body turns bright yellow.
Its large mouth lets it eat animals smaller than itself.
It is commonly found in rice paddies across many regions of India.

0711.name: Common Midwife Toad
0711.nickname: The dad who carries the eggs
0711.desc: This small toad from Europe has fathers who wrap the egg strings around their hind legs and carry them around. When the eggs are ready to hatch, the father takes them to the water and releases the tadpoles. It calls at night with a sound like a small bell.
facts: The father toad wraps the strings of eggs around his hind legs and carries them.
When the eggs are ready, he takes them to the water and releases the tadpoles.
At night, it calls with a soft, bell-like "peep."

0712.name: Common Spadefoot
0712.desc: This toad from Europe and western Asia has a hard, shovel-shaped growth on each hind foot that helps it dig well. It hides underground during the day and comes out at night. When threatened, it releases a substance that smells like garlic.
facts: The shovel-shaped growth on its hind feet lets it dig into the ground quickly.
It hides underground during the day and comes out at night to find food.
When threatened, it releases a substance that smells like garlic.

0713.name: Eastern Spadefoot
0713.desc: A hard, shovel-shaped growth on each hind foot lets it dig straight down into the ground. It only comes out to find a mate on days of heavy rain.
facts: The shovel-shaped growth on its hind feet lets it dig backwards into the ground to hide.
When it rains, it gathers at puddles, calling loudly while laying eggs.
Its tadpoles grow extremely fast, racing to develop before the puddle dries up.

0714.name: Painted Frog
0714.desc: This frog looks as though its back has been painted with colourful stripes or spots. It loves the water's edge and stays active during the day too.
facts: Its markings vary between individuals, with both striped and spotted forms.
Its tongue is disc-shaped and round, so it cannot flick it out far.
It lays its eggs in puddles or ditches.

0715.name: Yellow-bellied Toad
0715.desc: Its belly is a striking pattern of yellow and black. When in danger, it flips over to show its yellow belly, warning "I'm poisonous!"
facts: When threatened, it arches its back to display its yellow belly, a defence called the "unken reflex."
It protects itself by releasing an irritating toxin from its skin.
Its pupils are shaped like little hearts.

0716.name: European Fire-bellied Toad
0716.desc: Its belly is covered with a fiery red pattern. When it meets an enemy, it shows off its belly to warn that it is poisonous.
facts: Its red belly markings warn, "I'm dangerous!"
In spring, males call softly with a low "oo-oo" sound from the water.
It wards off predators with toxins released from its skin.

0718.name: Desert Rain Frog
0718.desc: Its round, plump body looks a bit like an avocado. It's famous for making an adorable squeaking sound when it feels threatened.
facts: When angry or startled, it lets out a squeak like a squeeze toy.
It gets moisture from fog, allowing it to live in a desert with almost no water.
It skips the tadpole stage entirely, hatching straight from the egg as a tiny frog.

0719.name: Asian Painted Frog
0719.desc: It has a plump body with brown stripes on its back. When threatened, it puffs itself up like a balloon to look bigger.
facts: It puffs up its body and secretes sticky mucus to fend off predators.
When it rains, it calls with a loud, cow-like bellow.
It especially loves eating ants and termites.

0720.name: Eastern Narrow-mouthed Toad
0720.desc: This small frog has a pointed head and a very narrow mouth. It picks off ants one by one with its narrow mouth and hides beneath fallen leaves.
facts: Its narrow, pointed mouth is perfectly suited for eating ants.
Slippery mucus on its skin protects it from ant bites.
When hiding, it folds the wrinkled skin behind its eyes to protect them.

0722.name: Dyeing Poison Frog
0722.desc: This vividly coloured poison frog has a yellow back and blue legs. Its colours vary so much between individuals that each one looks like it's wearing different clothes.
facts: It is one of the larger species among poison frogs.
Its colour and pattern vary widely from region to region.
Its bright colours are a warning sign that it is poisonous.

0723.name: Strawberry Poison Frog
0723.desc: This tiny poison frog has a body as red as a ripe strawberry. It's known for how carefully the mother cares for her tadpoles after they hatch.
facts: The mother carries her tadpoles to water-filled pools in the trees and feeds them unfertilised eggs.
Its colour varies widely by region, including red, blue, and green.
Despite its tiny size, it carries strong toxins obtained from the ants it eats.

0724.name: Phantasmal Poison Frog
0724.desc: This small poison frog has a beautiful red body with white stripes. Though tiny, it is a poisonous forest fairy of Ecuador.
facts: Scientists have studied its skin toxin after discovering a powerful pain-relieving compound in it.
Its limited habitat has put it at risk of extinction.
Its red base colour and white stripes vary slightly from region to region.

0725.name: Golden Mantella
0725.desc: This tiny frog is dazzling head to toe in golden orange. It is a rare frog found only in Madagascar.
facts: Its entire body is a bright golden orange, making it easy to spot.
Its very limited habitat has left it critically endangered.
It carries toxins in its skin obtained from its diet.

0726.name: Baron's Mantella
0726.nickname: The patterned one with green legs
0726.desc: This frog has a black body combined with green legs and yellow markings. It moves about actively on the forest floor of Madagascar during the day.
facts: It has a bold pattern mixing black, green, yellow, and orange.
It roams the forest floor by day, hunting ants.
It carries toxins from its diet to keep predators away.

0730.name: Red-spotted Toad
0730.nickname: The desert one with red spots
0730.desc: This flat-bodied toad is dotted with small red spots. It thrives even in dry deserts and rocky crevices.
facts: It gets its name from the red spots dotting its body.
Its flat body lets it hide easily in narrow rock crevices.
When it rains, it gathers at puddles and calls with a clear, bird-like trill.

0731.name: Malagasy Rainbow Frog
0731.desc: This frog looks like a rainbow with red, green, black, and white all mixed together. It climbs rock faces with its front feet and lives on canyon cliffs, a true treasure of Madagascar.
facts: It can climb smooth rock faces using its front feet.
A shovel-shaped growth on its hind feet lets it dig into sand to hide.
Its bright colours have made it a target for poaching, and it is now an endangered species.

0733.name: Purple Frog
0733.desc: This strange frog has a plump purple body and a pointed snout. It lives underground almost all year, coming out only for a few days during the monsoon.
facts: It spends most of the year underground, emerging only briefly during the monsoon season.
It uses its pointed snout to suck up food from inside termite mounds.
It belongs to an ancient lineage of frogs dating back to the age of the dinosaurs.

0734.name: Solomon Island Leaf Frog
0734.desc: This frog has pointed growths above its eyes that make it look like a dried leaf. Once it hides among fallen leaves, it truly seems to vanish.
facts: The growths above its eyes and its body colour closely mimic a dried leaf.
It skips the tadpole stage entirely, hatching straight from the egg as a tiny frog.
Its large mouth lets it eat frogs smaller than itself.

0735.name: Senegal Running Frog
0735.desc: This frog has thick, vertical stripes down its back. Unlike other frogs, it doesn't hop but instead strides along, walking like a small animal.
facts: Because it walks instead of hopping, it's called the "running frog."
At night, it makes a sound like a splashing water droplet.
The thick vertical stripes on its back make it easy to identify.

0738.name: Madagascar Mantella
0738.desc: This tiny frog lives in the forests of Madagascar. Its bold mix of black, green, yellow, and red warns, "I'm poisonous, don't eat me!" It's active during the day, hunting small insects.
facts: Its bright colours are a warning that it is poisonous.
It builds up toxins in its body by eating ants.
Even fully grown, it's only about the size of a fingertip.

0739.name: Long-toed Salamander
0739.desc: This salamander lives in western North America. It gets its name from the unusually long fourth toe on its hind feet. It has a yellow or greenish stripe down its back, and it hides under logs or rocks during the day.
facts: Its long hind toe is the source of its name.
It can survive in cold places and is even found in meltwater pools high in the mountains.
In winter, it hibernates underground or beneath rocks.

0740.name: Coastal Giant Salamander
0740.desc: This very large salamander lives in the Pacific coastal forests of North America. It's one of the largest land salamanders in the world, with sturdy legs and powerful jaws. When threatened, it can even make a small sound.
facts: It is one of the largest land salamanders in the world.
When threatened, it can make a low sound.
It's strong enough to prey on fish and other small animals.

0741.name: Arboreal Salamander
0741.desc: This salamander lives in California and is a skilled climber. It uses its toes and tail to climb up and down oak trees. Its brown body is dotted with yellow spots, and it has no lungs, breathing through its skin instead.
facts: With no lungs, it breathes through its skin.
It climbs trees and even lives inside holes high up in them.
It uses its tail to steady itself as it climbs.

0742.name: Northern Two-lined Salamander
0742.nickname: The salamander with two lines down its back
0742.desc: This small, slender salamander lives along streams in eastern North America. It gets its name from the two dark lines running down its back. It hides under rocks near clear water and eats small insects.
facts: The two stripes down its back are the source of its name.
It has no lungs and breathes through its skin.
It can only survive where there are clear streams.

0743.name: Spring Salamander
0743.desc: This salamander lives in cold, clear springs and streams in eastern North America. Its orange- or pink-tinged body has a light line running from each eye to the nostril. It's a hunter that preys even on other small salamanders.
facts: It can only live in cold, clean spring water.
It preys on other small salamanders too.
It has no lungs and breathes through its skin.

0744.name: Ceylon Caecilian
0744.desc: This limbless amphibian lives in Sri Lanka. It looks like an earthworm or snake, but it's actually related to frogs and salamanders. It burrows through the ground, and the mother has the special habit of guarding her eggs.
facts: With no legs, it looks like a snake or an earthworm.
Its eyes are nearly useless, and it lives buried in the soil.
The mother wraps her body around the eggs to protect them.

0745.name: Ringed Caecilian
0745.desc: This limbless amphibian lives in South America. It gets its name from the many ring-shaped grooves circling its body. Its young grow up in an extraordinary way, by nibbling on their mother's skin.
facts: The ring-shaped pattern on its body is the source of its name.
The young grow by feeding on their mother's specially adapted skin.
It's known to have venom glands in its mouth.

0746.name: Kallar Dancing Frog
0746.nickname: The dancer that waves its feet
0746.desc: This tiny frog lives along streams in India's Western Ghats mountains. Males raise a hind leg and wave their foot in a circular "dance" to call a mate or warn off other males. That's why it's called the "dancing frog."
facts: It sends signals by "dancing," waving one of its hind feet.
It is a rare frog found only in India's Western Ghats mountains.
With the sound of rushing streams too loud for calls, it communicates through gestures instead.

0747.name: Pumpkin Toadlet
0747.desc: This frog, no bigger than a fingernail, lives in Brazil's forests. Its bright orange body warns, "I'm poisonous!" Its bones can even glow, and since it can't hear well, it communicates through gestures instead.
facts: It is one of the smallest frogs in the world.
Its bright orange colour is a warning that it is poisonous.
Its backbone glows under ultraviolet light.

0748.name: Giant Leaf Frog
0748.nickname: The big monkey frog on the leaves
0748.desc: This large treefrog lives in the Amazon rainforest. It has a green back and white belly, and it climbs slowly through the trees like a monkey. It's also known for the powerful secretion that comes from its skin.
facts: It climbs slowly through the trees like a monkey.
It folds a leaf and lays its eggs inside.
Its skin produces a special secretion.

0749.name: Bushveld Rain Frog
0749.desc: This round, plump frog lives in southern Africa. Its body puffs up like a balloon, especially when it's angry. It can't swim, so it lives by burrowing underground.
facts: Since it cannot swim, falling into water is dangerous for it.
When angry, it puffs up its body like a balloon.
It digs into the ground backwards using its short legs.

0755.name: Cicada

info.서식지: Asia · Africa · Europe · North America · South America · Oceania

0755.desc: The cicada is an insect known for its loud buzzing song in summer. It spends a long time underground feeding on tree root sap before climbing up and becoming an adult. Male cicadas sing to call for females.
facts: It can spend three to seventeen years underground as a nymph, feeding on tree root sap.
Males use a special sound organ to sing and attract females.
It becomes an adult by shedding its skin, without going through a pupa stage.

0756.name: Stag Beetle
0756.desc: The stag beetle is famous for its impressive jaws. It mainly feeds on tree sap and is often seen flying towards lights at night. The back of a male's head looks just like an elephant's ear.
facts: The back of a male's head looks just like an elephant's ear.
It is mostly active at night, often flying towards lights.
It takes about two to three years for a larva to become an adult.

0761.name: Stick Insect
0761.desc: The stick insect looks just like a twig. Its body is so twig-like that predators struggle to spot it. When in danger, it can even snap off a leg to escape. It mainly feeds on tree leaves.
facts: It disguises its body as a twig to hide from predators.
It can shed a leg to escape when it's in danger.
It feeds on the leaves of broadleaf trees and is considered a pest in some areas.

0762.name: Praying Mantis

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Africa · Europe · North America

0762.desc: The praying mantis has front legs shaped like big grabbing paws. It mainly hunts and eats other insects, and lays eggs to reproduce. It's also good at hiding, which helps it avoid being caught by other animals.
facts: Its front legs are shaped like sickles, perfect for grabbing prey.
A female may eat the male after mating.
It is able to see in three dimensions, like 3D vision.

0764.name: Longhorn Beetle
0764.desc: The longhorn beetle has very long antennae. Its body is covered in fine yellowish-brown hairs, and its larvae grow inside wood. A male's antennae are even longer than a female's.
facts: A male's antennae are much longer than a female's.
Its larvae burrow into wood as they grow.
Its body is covered in fine yellowish-brown hairs.

0766.name: Oriental Longheaded Locust
0766.desc: This is a large grasshopper with a long, slender body. Its green body and long back legs move like an old grain-pounding mill, which is how it got its Korean name. When it flies, its wings clack together making a rattling sound.
facts: It is the largest grasshopper found in Korea.
Its very long back legs are how it got its name.
It makes a clacking sound with its wings when it flies.

0767.name: Earwig

info.서식지: Grassland · Asia · Europe · Africa · North America

0767.desc: The earwig is a small insect with pincer-like parts at the tip of its abdomen. It likes damp places, so it's often found in gardens and grassy areas. Don't worry, it's harmless to people!
facts: It has pincers at the tip of its abdomen.
It prefers damp, moist places.
It does not harm people.

0773.name: Whirligig Beetle
0773.desc: This small beetle swims in circles on the surface of ponds. Its eyes are split into upper and lower halves, so it can see above and below the water at the same time. When in danger, it quickly dives underwater.
facts: It spins in circles on the water's surface.
Its eyes are divided into upper and lower halves.
It dives underwater when it senses danger.

0774.name: Water Scorpion
0774.desc: This water insect has a long, thin body like a stick. A long breathing tube at its tail lets it breathe while underwater. It grabs small insects with its front legs and eats them.
facts: It breathes air from above the surface through its tail.
Its stick-like shape makes it hard to spot.
It snatches prey with its front legs.

0775.name: Giant Water Bug
0775.desc: This is the largest hunting insect found in Korea's waters. With its powerful front legs, it can even catch frogs and fish. In some related species, the father carries the eggs on his back to protect them.
facts: It is the largest water insect of its kind.
It is powerful enough to catch frogs.
It has become rare and is increasingly hard to find.

0776.name: Dung Beetle
0776.desc: This beetle eats and rolls animal dung, helping to keep the ground clean. It shapes the dung into a round ball and buries it underground. It's one of nature's best cleaners.
facts: It shapes dung into round balls.
It helps keep the ground clean.
Burying dung underground makes the soil richer.

0777.name: Stag Beetle
0777.desc: This impressive beetle has big, sturdy jaws. It uses them to wrestle with other males. It loves oak tree sap and is active at night.
facts: It wrestles other males using its large jaws.
It lives longer than many other stag beetles.
It feeds on tree sap at night.

0778.name: Sawtooth Stag Beetle
0778.desc: This stag beetle has jaws that curve inward like the teeth of a saw. Its body shines a reddish brown. On summer nights, it gathers at oak tree sap.
facts: Its jaws curve like the teeth of a saw.
Its body is a shiny reddish brown.
It gathers at tree sap on summer nights.

0780.name: Cabbage White Butterfly

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America

0780.desc: This is a common butterfly with white wings marked with black spots. It's often seen in cabbage patches and flower gardens. Its caterpillar grows up eating cabbage leaves.
facts: Its white wings are marked with black spots.
Its caterpillar feeds on cabbage leaves.
It is one of the most commonly seen butterflies.

0781.name: Asian Swallowtail
0781.desc: This large butterfly has yellow and black stripes like a tiger. Its hindwings have tail-like extensions. It flies gracefully through flower gardens.
facts: It has tiger-like stripes on its wings.
Its hindwings have tail-like extensions.
Its caterpillar feeds on citrus tree leaves.

0782.name: Chinese Peacock Butterfly
0782.desc: This beautiful butterfly has black wings that shimmer with a green sheen. It's called a swallowtail because it flies as fast as a swallow bird. Its hindwings have long tail-like extensions.
facts: Its black wings shimmer with a green sheen.
It flies as fast as a swallow bird.
Its hindwings have long tail-like extensions.

0783.name: Small Copper
0783.desc: This butterfly is as small as a fingernail, but its wings shine beautifully. Many kinds shimmer in blue or orange. It flutters low over grassy fields.
facts: It is a very small butterfly.
Its wings shine like a jewel.
Its caterpillar sometimes forms a friendly bond with ants.

0784.name: Eastern Pale Clouded Yellow
0784.desc: As its name suggests, this cute butterfly has yellow wings. It can be seen for a long stretch, from early spring to late autumn. It favours legume plants like clover.
facts: Its wings are yellow.
It can be seen from spring all the way through autumn.
Its caterpillar feeds on clover.

0786.name: Asiatic Bluetail
0786.desc: This damselfly has a body as thin and long as a thread. It's smaller than a typical dragonfly and likes to fold its wings when resting. It flutters gently through grass near ponds and streams.
facts: Its thread-thin body is how it got its Korean name.
It folds its wings over its back when resting.
It grows underwater as a nymph before becoming an adult.

0787.name: Wandering Glider
0787.desc: This dragonfly has a yellowish-brown body all over. It's famous for travelling incredibly far, even crossing oceans. In summer, you can see them flying in large swarms across the sky.
facts: It is one of the longest-travelling insects in the world.
It rides the wind to cross even the ocean.
It flies in large swarms during summer.

0788.name: Scorpionfly
0788.desc: The scorpionfly's tail curls upward, making it look like a scorpion. But it can't sting people and is completely harmless. Its face has a long, beak-like snout.
facts: A male's tail curls up like a scorpion's.
Its mouth is long, like a bird's beak.
Unlike a scorpion, it has no venom and is harmless.

0790.name: Rice Grasshopper
0790.desc: This grasshopper is bigger and sturdier than most other grasshoppers. Its powerful back legs let it leap a long way. It's a hardy grasshopper that can even survive winter as an adult.
facts: It is on the larger side among grasshoppers.
It sometimes survives the winter as an adult.
Its sturdy back legs let it jump long distances.

0791.name: Band-winged Grasshopper
0791.desc: This grasshopper is coloured like grass or dirt, so it hides perfectly in the undergrowth. When startled, it spreads its wings and flutters away. Its hindwings have a cute tinge of yellow.
facts: Its body colour matches grass and soil, hiding it well.
When startled, it spreads its wings and flies off.
It prefers dry ground.

0792.name: Pygmy Grasshopper
0792.desc: This grasshopper has a sharply pointed, triangular head. A small male is often seen riding piggyback on a much larger female. Its grass-green colour helps it hide in the undergrowth.
facts: Its head is sharply pointed and triangular.
A small male often rides on a larger female's back.
Its grass-green colour helps it hide in the undergrowth.

0793.name: Luehdorf's Apollo
0793.desc: This is one of the very first butterflies to appear in early spring. Its wings have black stripes like a tiger's. It's a rare butterfly you can only spot briefly, while spring flowers are blooming.
facts: It is one of the first butterflies to appear in spring.
Its wings have tiger-like black stripes.
It appears only once a year, in spring.

0794.name: Robust Cicada
0794.desc: This cicada sings loudly in the midday heat of summer. Only males can sing, using a sound organ in their abdomen. After living underground for several years, it climbs up into the trees.
facts: It is one of Korea's most familiar singing cicadas.
Only males are able to make sound.
Its nymphs live underground for several years.

0795.name: Large Brown Cicada
0795.desc: This cicada makes a sizzling sound, like oil frying in a pan. Its brown, non-transparent wings make it easy to identify. It clings tightly to trees, singing away in the summer forest.
facts: Its Korean name comes from its sizzling, oil-frying sound.
Its brown wings make it easy to spot.
Unlike most cicadas, its wings are not transparent.

0796.name: Black Cicada
0796.desc: This is the largest cicada found in Korea. Its whole body is jet black, and it sings very loudly. When many sing together, the whole city can echo with sound.
facts: It is the largest cicada found in Korea.
Its whole body is jet black.
Its song is extremely loud.

0797.name: Meadow Katydid
0797.desc: This small katydid makes a wheezing sound as it sings. Its antennae are much longer than its body. Its grass-green colour makes it very hard to find once it's hidden in the undergrowth.
facts: Its wheezing song is how it got its Korean name.
Its antennae are far longer than its body.
Its grass-green colour lets it hide well in the undergrowth.

0798.name: White-spotted Longhorn Beetle
0798.desc: This beetle has a black body dotted with little white star-like spots. Its antennae are impressively longer than its body. Its larvae grow by boring through wood.
facts: Its black body is dotted with white star-like spots.
Its antennae are longer than its body.
Its larvae live and feed by boring through wood.

0799.name: Migratory Locust
0799.desc: This is a very large grasshopper that can fly powerfully over long distances. When its numbers grow huge, it sometimes forms swarms and migrates together. Its strong back legs let it hop across meadows.
facts: It is a large grasshopper found in many parts of the world.
When numbers grow large, it migrates in swarms.
Its powerful back legs let it jump and fly great distances.

0800.name: Tiger Beetle
0800.desc: This beetle's body shimmers with rainbow colours. When people approach, it hops just ahead of them down the path, which is how it got its Korean name meaning 'path guide'. It runs extremely fast to hunt small insects.
facts: Its body shimmers with rainbow colours.
It hops ahead of people along paths, earning its Korean name.
It is one of the fastest-running insects.

0802.name: Painted Lady

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Africa · Europe · North America

0802.nickname: The butterfly that circles the world
0802.desc: This butterfly has a mix of orange, black and white markings and can be found on almost every continent except Antarctica. It's one of the most widespread butterflies in the world, and it's also known for travelling extremely long distances.
facts: It lives on almost every continent except Antarctica.
It sometimes migrates thousands of kilometres.
Its caterpillar mainly feeds on thistle leaves.

0803.name: Red Admiral

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America

0803.desc: This striking butterfly has black wings with a bold red band and white spots. It's commonly seen in gardens and at forest edges, and it also loves tree sap and ripe fruit. It's curious enough to fly right up to people.
facts: Its black wings are marked with a bold red band.
It loves ripe fruit and tree sap.
Its caterpillar grows up eating nettle leaves.

0804.name: European Peacock
0804.desc: This butterfly has red wings marked with big eye-spots like a peacock's feathers. When a bird approaches, it flashes its wings open to scare it off with the eye-spots. It's found across Europe and survives winter as an adult.
facts: It startles predators with the eye-spots on its wings.
It hibernates through winter as an adult.
Its caterpillar feeds on nettle leaves.

0805.name: Asian Comma
0805.nickname: The butterfly disguised as a leaf
0805.desc: This butterfly has ragged, saw-toothed wing edges. When it folds its wings, it looks just like a dry, fallen leaf, helping it hide well. It has a white comma-shaped mark on its hindwing and survives winter as an adult.
facts: With its wings folded, it looks just like a dry leaf.
The white comma-shaped mark on its hindwing gives it its name.
It survives the winter as an adult.

0806.name: Old World Swallowtail
0806.desc: This large butterfly has a yellow base colour with black markings and blue and orange spots. Its hindwings have tail-like extensions like a swallow's, giving it a tiger-swallowtail name. When threatened, its caterpillar pokes out horn-like organs that give off a strong smell to drive predators away.
facts: Its hindwing tails give it a distinctive swallow-like shape.
When threatened, its caterpillar pokes out smelly horns.
It is found across a wide area of the Northern Hemisphere.

0807.name: Common Bluebottle
0807.desc: This butterfly has a bright turquoise band crossing the middle of its black wings. It flies extremely fast and is rarely still for even a moment. It's found in forests and gardens across warm parts of Asia.
facts: The turquoise band on its wings gives it its name.
It is an extremely fast flyer.
Its caterpillar feeds on camphor tree leaves.

0808.name: Great Mormon
0808.nickname: The big butterfly of many disguises
0808.desc: This is a very large black butterfly found in tropical Asia. Females can look completely different from one another, with some mimicking poisonous butterflies to fool predators. It flies gracefully through gardens and forests.
facts: Females can appear in several different patterns.
Some females mimic poisonous butterflies to fool predators.
Its wingspan is larger than the palm of a hand.

0809.name: Chestnut Tiger
0809.nickname: The butterfly that crosses the sea
0809.desc: This elegant butterfly has translucent, pale blue markings. Remarkably, it can migrate hundreds of kilometres across the sea. Toxins from the plants it ate as a caterpillar protect it, so predators leave it alone.
facts: It migrates long distances, even across the sea.
Toxins from its caterpillar diet protect its body.
It can also be spotted in Korea.

0810.name: Silver-washed Fritillary
0810.desc: This large butterfly has an orange base with black leopard-like spots. Its name comes from the shining silver streaks on the underside of its wings. It flies energetically through forest clearings, feeding on the nectar of flowers like thistles.
facts: The silver streaks on its wing undersides give it its name.
Its caterpillar grows up eating violet leaves.
It flies energetically through forest clearings.

0811.name: Indian Fritillary
0811.desc: This is an orange, leopard-patterned butterfly. Its name comes from the female's black forewing tips marked with a white band. It lives in warm parts of Asia, and its caterpillar grows up eating violet leaves.
facts: Its name comes from the female's black forewing tips.
Its caterpillar feeds on violet leaves.
Its range is gradually expanding further north.

0812.name: Dryad
0812.desc: This butterfly has dark brown and black wings, as dark as a chimney. Its wings are marked with bluish eye-spots. In summer, it flutters low over meadows, feeding on the nectar of flowers like thistles.
facts: Its chimney-dark wings give it its name.
Its wings feature distinctive bluish eye-spots.
It flies low over meadows during summer.

0813.name: Meadow Brown
0813.desc: This butterfly has eye-like markings on its wings, giving it a 'snake's eye' name in Korean. It's a common brown-winged butterfly that flutters through meadows in summer.
facts: The eye-spots on its wings trick birds into attacking its wings instead of its body.
It is one of the most common butterflies in Europe.
It stays active even on cloudy days.

0814.name: Diana Treebrown
0814.nickname: The dark butterfly of the forest shade
0814.desc: True to its name, this butterfly is a deep brown and black. It prefers shady forests over bright open areas and is most active at dusk.
facts: It prefers tree sap and fermented fruit over flower nectar.
It favours shady forests and is easiest to spot at dusk.
Its caterpillar grows up eating bamboo and dwarf bamboo leaves.

0815.name: Gotama Bushbrown
0815.desc: This brown butterfly has a row of round eye-spots along its wing edges. It flies low through the undergrowth and prefers shade.
facts: The number of eye-spots on its wings varies slightly by season.
Its summer and autumn forms have different patterns.
It flies low through shaded undergrowth.

0816.name: Common Brimstone
0816.desc: Males are a bright lemon yellow that really stands out. Since it survives winter as an adult, it's one of the very first butterflies seen flying in early spring.
facts: Its adult lifespan of nearly a year is among the longest of any butterfly.
It hibernates as an adult among fallen leaves.
Its folded wings look just like a leaf, helping it hide.

0817.name: Lesser Brimstone
0817.desc: This butterfly resembles the common brimstone but is a little smaller and lives mostly in mountains. The tips of its yellow wings come to a sharp point. It survives winter as an adult.
facts: It hibernates as an adult and flies again in early spring.
The tips of its wings jut out in a hook-like point.
It prefers mountain forests.

0818.name: Yellow Tip
0818.desc: This white butterfly has wingtips that jut out like hooks. Males have an orange patch at the tip of their forewings. It can only be seen briefly, in spring.
facts: It appears only once a year, in spring.
Its pupa looks just like a thorn on a twig, keeping it hidden.
The mottled green pattern on its wing undersides camouflages it in the undergrowth.

0819.name: Indian Cabbage White
0819.desc: This white butterfly looks similar to the cabbage white, but the black spots along its wing edges are bolder. It's commonly found in fields and near farmland.
facts: It has several generations a year and can be seen from spring through autumn.
Its caterpillar eats cabbage and radish leaves, so it's often found in fields.
The black spots on its wing edges are bigger and bolder than the cabbage white's.

0820.name: Green-veined White
0820.desc: The underside of its white wings has green, vein-like stripes. It's a common butterfly often seen at forest edges and along mountain trails.
facts: The leaf-vein-like green stripes on its wing undersides give it its name.
Its spring and summer forms look slightly different.
It sometimes gathers at wet ground or streamsides to drink.

0821.name: Common Grass Yellow
0821.desc: This butterfly has small, bright yellow wings. It's common in southern regions and prefers warm places. It sometimes gathers in groups to drink from damp ground.
facts: In warm regions, it can be seen all year round.
Groups often gather on wet ground to drink.
Some individuals survive the winter as adults.

0822.name: Common Five-ring
0822.desc: This small brown butterfly has several round eye-like spots on the underside of its wings. It flies low over the undergrowth in a wavy, fluttering pattern.
facts: Its English name, 'five-ring', comes from the several eye-spots on its wings.
It flutters low over the undergrowth in a wavy flight.
The eye-spots draw a bird's attack away from its body and onto its wings.

0823.name: Small Branded Swift
0823.desc: This small, plump brown butterfly flits about at very high speed. Its thick body and short wings make it look like a tiny rocket in flight.
facts: Its wingbeats are so fast it can change direction in an instant.
Its caterpillar cuts grass blades to build a shelter around itself.
Its plump body and short wings can make it look like a moth.

0824.name: Large Skipper
0824.desc: This plump skipper butterfly is a mix of orange and brown. It flies briskly through sunny meadows and has a habit of resting with its wings half-spread.
facts: It rests in a distinctive pose, holding its forewings and hindwings at different angles.
It loves sunshine and is most active on clear days.
Its fast wingbeats let it change direction in an instant.

0825.name: Great Purple Emperor
0825.desc: This is one of the largest butterflies in Korea. A male's wings shine a beautiful purple depending on the angle of light. It's a powerful butterfly that loves tree sap.
facts: It is famous as Japan's designated national butterfly.
A male's wings shimmer from blue to purple depending on the light.
It prefers oak tree sap and fermented fruit over flower nectar.

0826.name: Freyer's Purple Emperor
0826.desc: This butterfly lives in willow groves near water. A male's wings shimmer purple depending on the light. Some individuals have a mix of yellow and brown colouring.
facts: A male's wings shimmer purple depending on the viewing angle.
It favours riverside willows, so it's often seen near water.
It sometimes sips fluid from wet ground or animal droppings.

0827.name: Red Ring Skirt
0827.desc: This striking butterfly has a black base with white mottled patches and a row of red spots along its hindwings. It flies gracefully through hackberry tree forests.
facts: The red spots along its hindwing edges give it its name.
Like the great purple emperor, its caterpillar feeds on hackberry leaves.
It is known for its slow, gliding, graceful flight.

0828.name: False Heath Fritillary
0828.desc: This butterfly has an orange and black pattern that looks like a woven net. It prefers damp meadows and mountain grasslands.
facts: Its mottled, leopard-like pattern gives it the name 'fritillary'.
It prefers cool, damp grasslands.
Its caterpillars live together in groups before eventually scattering.

0829.name: Convolvulus Hawk-moth

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa · Oceania

0829.desc: This is a large, powerful moth with an extremely long, tube-like mouth. It hovers in place in front of flowers to sip nectar, looking just like a hummingbird.
facts: Its tube-like mouth is longer than its body, letting it reach deep into flowers.
It is a powerful flyer capable of travelling long distances.
It hovers in front of flowers to feed at dusk and at night.

0830.name: Lesser Death's Head Hawkmoth
0830.desc: This moth has a skull-like pattern on its back, which gives it its name. It sneaks into beehives to steal honey and makes a squeaking sound when it feels threatened.
facts: The skull-shaped marking on its back earns it the nickname 'death's head moth'.
It uses its short, thick tube-mouth to sneak into hives and steal honey.
It squeaks by forcing air through its body to startle enemies.

0831.name: Hummingbird Hawk-moth

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Europe · Asia · Africa

0831.desc: Unlike most hawk-moths, this one is active during the day. Hovering and buzzing in front of flowers while sipping nectar through its long tube-mouth, it looks just like a hummingbird.
facts: It is one of the rare hawk-moths active during the day.
It hovers by beating its wings dozens of times per second.
It travels long distances in search of nectar.

0832.name: Elephant Hawk Moth
0832.desc: This beautiful moth blends pink with olive green. It's called the 'elephant moth' because its caterpillar's front end looks like an elephant's trunk.
facts: When its caterpillar puffs up its front end, it looks like an elephant's trunk, giving it its name.
When startled, its caterpillar swells its eye-spots to look like a snake.
With its vivid pink and green colours, it visits flowers at night.

0833.name: Chinese Oak Silkmoth
0833.desc: This is a very large brown moth with a transparent, eye-like marking on each wing. Its caterpillar eats oak leaves and spins tough silk threads to build its cocoon.
facts: A tough silk called 'tussah silk' is harvested from its cocoon.
As an adult it has no mouth, so it doesn't eat and only mates.
The transparent eye-spots on its wings startle predators.

0834.name: Silkworm Moth
0834.nickname: The moth that gives us silk
0834.desc: This moth has been raised by people for a very long time to produce silk. Its caterpillar, the silkworm, grows up eating mulberry leaves and spins a cocoon of white thread. Adults cannot fly.
facts: It has been bred by people for thousands of years and can no longer survive in the wild.
A single cocoon can yield over a kilometre of silk thread.
Even though it has wings, the adult can barely fly.

0835.name: Japanese Giant Silkmoth
0835.desc: This is one of the largest moths found in Korea, with big eye-spots on its broad wings. Its caterpillar feeds on chestnut and oak leaves and spins a large, net-like cocoon.
facts: It is a very large moth that appears in autumn.
Its caterpillar spins a loosely woven, green, net-like cocoon.
Adults have no mouth, so they only mate before dying, without eating.

0836.name: Chinese Moon Moth
0836.desc: This beautiful moth has pale green wings trailing long tails. Its elegant look also earns it the nickname 'moon moth'. It flies towards lights at night.
facts: The long tails on its hindwings help confuse bat sonar and protect it.
Its pale green wings earn it the nickname 'moon moth'.
As an adult, its mouth has stopped developing, so it doesn't eat at all.

0837.name: Gypsy Moth

info.서식지: Rainforest · Near cities · Europe · Asia · North America

0837.desc: This moth's caterpillar eats enormous amounts of tree leaves, sometimes causing serious damage. Females are white and large but can barely fly, while males are brown and fly well.
facts: When caterpillars swarm in huge numbers, they can strip a whole forest of its leaves.
Newly hatched caterpillars spin a silk thread and ride the wind to travel far.
Females are too heavy to fly much, so they lay their eggs while clinging to trees.

0838.name: Garden Tiger Moth

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · Europe · Asia · North America

0838.desc: This colourful moth has brown and white patterned forewings and red hindwings dotted with blue. Its caterpillar is covered in brown fur all over, earning it the nickname 'woolly bear'. Its body contains toxins, so birds avoid eating it.
facts: The red and blue markings on its hindwings are a warning colour signalling that it's toxic.
Its caterpillar hibernates through winter and becomes active again in spring.
It is nocturnal and often flies towards lights at night.

0839.name: Crimson Speckled Moth
0839.desc: This pretty moth has white forewings speckled with colourful black and red spots. It favours warm regions and is often active during the day. Its caterpillar enjoys eating legume plants like alfalfa.
facts: It favours warm places and sometimes rides the wind to travel far.
Its red spots are a warning colour signalling that it's toxic.
It flies around flowers even during the day.

0840.name: Owl Moth
0840.desc: This is a very large moth covered in wavy, rippling patterns. Its markings look like an owl's eyes, which is how it got its English name 'owl moth'. Its caterpillar feeds on ash and privet leaves.
facts: It is among the largest moths found in Korea.
The wavy pattern on its wings confuses the eyes of predators.
It is nocturnal and flies towards lights at night.

0841.name: Oriental Moth
0841.nickname: The caterpillar with a nasty sting
0841.desc: This plump moth has a yellowish body. Its caterpillar, called a slug caterpillar, carries venomous spines on its back that sting sharply if touched. It grows by feeding on leaves up in the trees.
facts: The caterpillar's spines are venomous and give a sharp sting if touched.
It spends the winter inside a hard, egg-shaped cocoon.
It is nocturnal and flies toward lights at night.

0842.name: Fall Webworm Moth

info.서식지: Near cities · Grassland · North America · Asia · Europe

0842.desc: This moth has pure white wings. Its caterpillars spin web-like nests over tree branches and feed together on leaves inside them. It originally came from North America but has now spread to many countries.
facts: The caterpillars cover trees in white silk webbing, giving them the name fall webworm.
Huge numbers of caterpillars live together on a single tree.
It is an invasive species that has spread abroad and damages trees.

0843.name: Relict Longhorn Beetle
0843.desc: This is the largest and strongest beetle found in Korea. It has a black body, powerful jaws and very long antennae. It is so rare that it is protected as a natural monument.
facts: It is designated as Korean Natural Monument No. 218 and protected by law.
The larva lives inside dead trees for several years as it grows.
Males use their large jaws to fight rival males.

0844.name: Mulberry Longhorn Beetle
0844.desc: This is a large, slender longhorn beetle with a greyish body. Its antennae are longer than its body, and it favours mulberry and fig trees. The larva grows by burrowing through the wood.
facts: The larva tunnels inside the tree, weakening it as it feeds.
It uses its long antennae to sense what is around it as it moves.
It gets its name from its fondness for mulberry trees.

0845.name: Rosalia Longicorn
0845.desc: This is a stunningly beautiful longhorn beetle with a clear sky-blue body marked with black spots. Its lovely, rare colouring makes it a favourite among people. It lives in old forests, on trees such as maples.
facts: Its beautiful sky-blue colour makes it a precious species in need of protection.
It can only be found in old, healthy forests.
The larva takes several years to grow inside dead broadleaf trees.

0846.name: Hemp Longhorn Beetle
0846.nickname: Small beetle of the hemp field
0846.desc: This is a small longhorn beetle with grey and white stripes on its body. It gets its name from its liking for plants such as hemp and thistle. The larva grows inside plant stems.
facts: Unlike most longhorn beetles, it lives in plants rather than trees.
The stripes on its body help it blend in with plant stems.
It is often seen in hemp fields, which is how it got its name.

0847.name: Tuberculate Longhorn Beetle
0847.desc: This longhorn beetle has a bumpy back like toad skin, covered in black hairs. Its colour blends in with tree bark, hiding it well. It favours dead broadleaf trees such as oak.
facts: The bumpy pattern on its back resembles a toad, which is how it got its name.
Its bark-like colouring helps it hide from predators.
In winter it sometimes hibernates as an adult beetle under trees.

0848.name: European Stag Beetle
0848.desc: The male of this stag beetle has huge jaws shaped like deer antlers. It is one of the largest beetles in Europe. The larva takes several years to grow inside dead wood.
facts: The male's antler-like jaws look fearsome but do not bite very hard.
The larva lives inside rotting wood for several years.
Its numbers are declining as forests shrink.

0849.name: Giant Stag Beetle
0849.desc: This stag beetle has a broad, flat black body and strong jaws with an inner tooth. It is a popular species commonly seen in Korea. It comes out at night to feed on tree sap.
facts: Its flat body lets it hide easily in narrow gaps in tree bark.
It uses the tooth on the inside of its jaws to wrestle rival males.
Adults can hibernate through winter and live for several years.

0850.name: Astacoides Stag Beetle
0850.desc: This stag beetle has a yellow or reddish-brown body with two black spots. Its long jaws curve inward in an impressive shape. In Korea it can be found on Jeju Island and similar places.
facts: The two black spots on its back give it its name.
It is a rare stag beetle that needs protection in Korea.
Its bright yellow colouring is one of its distinctive features.

0851.name: Small Stag Beetle
0851.desc: This is a small, black stag beetle. It is very common throughout Korea. It comes out at night to feed on oak sap and is often drawn to lights.
facts: It is one of the most common stag beetles in Korea.
Though small, it can live a long time, hibernating as an adult.
It is nocturnal and flies toward lights at night.

0852.name: Caucasus Beetle
0852.desc: This impressive rhinoceros beetle has a shiny black body and three large horns. It lives in the forests of Southeast Asia and is among the largest rhinoceros beetles in the world. Males use their horns to wrestle each other.
facts: Despite its name, it lives in Southeast Asia, not the Caucasus.
Males lift and throw rival males using their large horns.
Its body has a shiny, metallic sheen.

0853.name: Hercules Beetle
0853.desc: Including its long horns, this is the longest beetle in the world. The male has two large horns, one above and one below, that it uses to pick up and throw rivals. It lives in the tropical rainforests of Central and South America.
facts: Including its horns, it can reach 17 centimetres, the longest of any beetle.
It is strong enough to lift hundreds of times its own body weight.
Its shell can turn yellowish depending on the humidity.

0854.name: Elephant Beetle
0854.desc: This large rhinoceros beetle is covered in short yellow hairs. It gets its name from the horn that stretches forward like an elephant's trunk. It lives in the forests of Central and South America.
facts: Its forward-reaching horn resembles an elephant's trunk, which gave it its name.
Its body is short and covered in yellow hair.
It is one of the heaviest beetles in the world.

0855.name: Goliath Beetle
0855.desc: By weight, this is one of the heaviest insects in the world. It has a large body patterned in white and brown. It lives in the tropical rainforests of Africa and enjoys tree sap and fruit.
facts: Its larva can weigh over 100 grams, making it one of the heaviest insects on Earth.
Despite its heavy body, it can still spread its wings and fly well.
Its white body with brown markings gives it a striking look.

0856.name: Rose Chafer
0856.desc: This pretty beetle has a metallic, shimmering green body. It flies among flowers by day, feeding on pollen and nectar. It can be found in gardens and fields across Europe and Asia.
facts: Its shimmering green colour comes from a special structure in its shell.
Unlike most beetles, it visits flowers during the daytime.
It can fly without opening its hard forewings wide.

0857.name: White-spotted Flower Chafer
0857.desc: This beetle has a dark copper-coloured body scattered with white spots. Its larva, known as a grub, is famous for crawling on its back. It enjoys tree sap and ripe fruit.
facts: The larva crawls on its back with its belly facing upward.
White spots are scattered across its hard forewings.
The larva helps break down rotting wood, enriching the soil.

0859.name: Cupreous Chafer
0859.desc: This beetle shimmers with a blend of copper and green. It feeds on the leaves of beans and fruit trees. It is often seen around lights on summer nights.
facts: Its body shines with a reddish, copper-like colour.
It feeds on the leaves of many crops, making it a nuisance to farmers.
It is nocturnal and often flies toward lights.

0860.name: Common Cockchafer
0860.desc: This is a large, brown beetle from Europe. The male has impressive antennae that fan open like a hand fan. It appears in swarms in spring and feeds on leaves.
facts: The male's antennae fan out into several branches.
The larva feeds on roots underground for several years as it grows.
It swarms and flies about on spring evenings.

0861.name: Korean Black Chafer
0861.desc: This beetle has a glossy, all-black body. It often flies around lights on summer nights. The larva grows in the soil, feeding on plant roots.
facts: Its whole body is jet black with a glossy shine.
It is nocturnal and flies toward lights.
The larva can damage crops by feeding on roots in the soil.

0862.name: Colorado Potato Beetle

info.서식지: Grassland · Near cities · North America · Europe · Asia

0862.desc: This leaf beetle has a yellow body with ten black stripes. It loves eating potato leaves, which makes it a headache for farmers. It originally lived in North America but has spread to many countries.
facts: It has ten black stripes on its forewings, which is reflected in its scientific name.
It is a major agricultural pest that feeds on potato leaves.
It evolves resistance to pesticides quickly, making it hard to control.

0863.name: Tansy Beetle
0863.desc: This small beetle has a metallic green shine. It mainly feeds on the leaves of plants such as tansy. It can be found in riverside meadows across Europe and Asia.
facts: Its metallic shine earns it the nickname jewel beetle.
It is a poor flier and mostly gets around on foot.
It lives only on certain plants, such as tansy.

0864.name: Blue Alder Leaf Beetle
0864.desc: This small leaf beetle shimmers with a deep indigo-blue colour. It gets its name from feeding on alder leaves. Both the adult and the larva eat the leaves.
facts: Both adults and larvae feed on the same tree's leaves together.
Its body shines with a deep indigo-blue colour.
It gathers in large numbers on alder trees.

0867.name: 22-spot Ladybird
0867.desc: This small ladybird has a bright yellow back with twenty-two black spots. Unusually, it eats powdery mildew growing on leaves instead of aphids. It can be found in meadows across Europe and Asia.
facts: Unlike most ladybirds, it eats mould instead of aphids.
Its body is so small it is smaller than a fingernail.
Its yellow and black pattern stands out clearly.

0868.name: Sap-feeding Beetle
0868.nickname: Regular at the tree's sap bar
0868.desc: This beetle's short forewings leave the tip of its abdomen exposed. It gathers around sweet sap flowing from trees such as oak, and around ripe fruit. Its body is black with a red pattern on its back.
facts: Its short forewings leave the tip of its abdomen showing.
It is drawn to the sweet smell of tree sap.
It is often found in the same spot as rhinoceros beetles and stag beetles.

0869.name: Mealworm Beetle

info.서식지: Near cities · Asia · Europe · North America · Africa

0869.desc: This is the black beetle that a yellow larva, known as a mealworm, grows into. It lives in grain stores and flour, eating almost anything. The larva is widely raised as food for birds and fish.
facts: Its larva is the very same mealworm used as fish food.
It moults several times as it grows from egg to adult.
It prefers dark, dry places.

0870.name: Bombardier Beetle
0870.desc: This amazing beetle fires a hot, harsh liquid from its rear with a loud pop when threatened. The spray, nearly boiling hot, drives enemies away. It moves about at night, hunting small insects.
facts: It mixes two chemicals inside its body to make a spray nearly 100 degrees Celsius.
It fires the spray with a popping sound, like a puff of smoke.
It is nocturnal, active at night.

0871.name: Ground Beetle
0871.desc: This is a large, striking ground beetle with a glowing red rim around its body. It cannot fly, but runs fast across the ground, hunting earthworms, snails and small insects. It hunts mostly at night.
facts: Its wings are fused shut, so it cannot fly and instead runs.
The glowing red rim on its body gives it its name.
It is a carnivorous insect that hunts across the ground at night.

0872.name: Giant Water Scavenger Beetle
0872.desc: This is a large, black beetle that lives in ponds and rice paddies. It looks similar to a giant water bug but is gentle-natured and mostly eats water plants. It breathes by carrying an air bubble under its belly.
facts: It carries an air bubble under its belly to breathe underwater.
As an adult it is mostly a gentle plant-eater.
At night it sometimes flies up, drawn to lights.

0873.name: Chestnut Weevil
0873.desc: This small beetle has a long snout like an elephant's trunk. It uses this snout to drill a hole into chestnuts or acorns and lay its eggs inside. The larva grows by eating its way through the chestnut.
facts: It drills into chestnuts with its long, trunk-like snout.
It lays eggs inside the hole, and the larva eats its way through the chestnut.
There are tiny teeth at the tip of its snout.

0874.name: Poplar-and-willow Borer

info.서식지: Rainforest · River & lake · Asia · Europe · North America

0874.desc: This is a small weevil that bores into the stems of willow and poplar trees. Its black and white blotchy pattern makes it hard to spot against tree bark. The larva gnaws through the wood, weakening the tree.
facts: The larva grows by boring through the tree's stem.
Its black and white pattern blends in with bark, camouflaging it.
It is widespread across Europe, Asia and North America.

0875.name: Giraffe Weevil
0875.desc: This funny-looking beetle has a neck stretched out long like a giraffe's. The female rolls up a leaf to make a cradle in which she lays her eggs. Its reddish body is quite pretty.
facts: The female rolls up a leaf to build a cradle for her eggs.
Its long neck, like a giraffe's, gives it the name giraffe weevil.
The male's neck is much longer than the female's.

0877.name: Yellow-legged Hornet
0877.desc: This hornet has a black back and yellow-tipped legs. It originally lived in Asia but has spread to Europe and other regions, becoming a problem there. It preys on honeybees, worrying beekeepers.
facts: Its yellow leg tips give it its name.
It hovers in the air and snatches honeybees to eat.
It is an invasive species that has spread from Asia to Europe.

0878.name: Paper Wasp
0878.desc: This wasp chews tree bark and mixes it with saliva to build a paper-like nest. It is easy to spot in flight by the way it dangles its hind legs. It hunts other insects to feed its larvae.
facts: It builds its nest from paper made by chewing wood.
It dangles its long hind legs when it flies.
It is gentler than true hornets but will sting if its nest is disturbed.

0887.name: Yellow Waxtail
0887.desc: This small damselfly has a thin, thread-like body with a yellowish colour. It flutters gently among the grasses at the edges of ponds and marshes. It feeds on small flying insects.
facts: Its thread-thin body gives it the name thread dragonfly.
Its larva, called a naiad, grows underwater.
It catches small flying insects in the air.

0888.name: Scarlet Skimmer
0888.desc: This dragonfly has a body as red as a chilli pepper. It flies swiftly over summer rice paddies and ponds, hunting small insects. Only the male is red, while the female has a yellowish colour.
facts: Only males are red; females are yellowish, so colour tells them apart.
Its larva, or naiad, lives underwater, hunting small water insects.
It is a skilled hunter that snatches prey out of the air in flight.

0889.name: Spotted Darter
0889.nickname: Little red one of the autumn paddy
0889.desc: This is a small red dragonfly commonly seen near rice paddies in autumn. Mature males turn a deep red colour. It feeds on small flying insects.
facts: It emerges in summer and mates in autumn, a classic autumn dragonfly.
It favours human-made wetlands such as rice paddies.
Its larva grows underwater and sometimes spends the winter there.

0890.name: Banded Darter
0890.desc: This dragonfly is easy to recognise by the brown band across its otherwise clear wings. It flies slowly around clear water. It feeds on small insects.
facts: The brown band near the tip of its wings is its most distinctive feature.
It flutters more slowly than other similar dragonflies.
It prefers areas with clean, flowing water.

0891.name: Asiatic Bluetail
0891.nickname: Slender fairy of the water's edge
0891.desc: This is a small, thin damselfly, slender as a matchstick. It flits lightly among water plants. It feeds on very tiny insects.
facts: Its very thin body gives it the name thread dragonfly.
Unlike most dragonflies, it folds its wings back over its body at rest.
It is common in everyday places such as rice paddies and ponds.

0892.name: Lesser Emperor
0892.nickname: The big emperor of the water
0892.desc: This is a large member of the emperor dragonfly group. It flies powerfully over ponds on its big, sturdy wings. It preys on other insects, and even small dragonflies.
facts: As an emperor dragonfly, it flies large and strong.
It is a powerful predator that hunts other flying insects in midair.
The female lays her eggs inside the stems of water plants.

0893.name: White-tailed Skimmer
0893.desc: Mature males look dusted with white powder all over their bodies. It is an extremely common sight around rice paddies and ponds. It feeds on small insects.
facts: Mature males turn a pale, powdery blue colour, as if dusted with white.
It is one of the most common dragonflies in Korea in summer.
It has a habit of returning to the same favourite perch.

0894.name: Sedakov's Bush-cricket
0894.desc: This is a large bush-cricket that lives in meadows. It rubs its wings together to make a loud chirping sound. It is an omnivore that eats both other insects and plants.
facts: It rubs its forewings together to produce its chirping sound.
It uses its long antennae to sense its surroundings day and night.
It is an omnivore that eats plants as well as hunting small insects.

0895.name: Japanese Conehead Katydid
0895.nickname: The green insect that sings all night
0895.desc: This green insect calls through the night with a chirping sound. Its leaf-like body helps it hide. It enjoys hunting small insects.
facts: It rubs its wings together at night, making a chirping call.
Its green, leaf-shaped body makes it hard to spot.
Its pointed, cone-shaped head gives it the name conehead.

0897.name: Japanese Rice Grasshopper
0897.nickname: Green grasshopper that hops in the paddy
0897.desc: This is a green grasshopper commonly found in rice paddies. It leaps powerfully using its strong hind legs. It feeds on the leaves of plants such as rice.
facts: It can jump many times its own body length using its strong hind legs.
It has long been a familiar insect, often caught in rice paddies.
Its green body helps it hide among rice and grass.

0898.name: Marbled Grasshopper
0898.nickname: The grasshopper that clicks as it flies
0898.desc: This is a large grasshopper that lives in meadows. When it flies, it sometimes clatters its hind wings to make a rattling sound. It feeds on a variety of grasses.
facts: It sometimes makes a rattling sound by clattering its hind wings in flight.
Its body colour resembles soil and grass, making it hard to spot at rest.
It is among the larger species of grasshopper.

0899.name: Common Backswimmer
0899.nickname: The upside-down hunter of the water
0899.desc: This curious water insect swims upside down, with its belly facing up and its back facing down. It rows with its long hind legs like oars. It preys on small water insects and tadpoles.
facts: It swims upside down on its back, which is how it got its name.
It rows itself along swiftly using its long hind legs like oars.
It can give a sharp bite if handled carelessly, so caution is needed.

0900.name: European Striped Shield Bug
0900.desc: This shield-shaped insect has a red body with black vertical stripes. Its bold colouring warns predators, I don't taste good. It feeds by sucking sap from plants.
facts: Its red and black stripes are a warning colour that says I taste bad.
Its broad, shield-shaped back gives it the name shield bug.
It favours plants in the carrot family, such as carrots and fennel.

0905.name: Japanese Mantis Shrimp
0905.desc: The mantis shrimp is a small crustacean found in the sea. It catches prey with its powerful front legs and lives inside burrows it digs. It is especially active at night, hunting small fish and crabs.
facts: It hunts prey using its powerful front legs.
It digs burrows in muddy seafloors at depths of 10 to 30 metres.
It is nocturnal, mostly active at night.

0906.name: Swimming Crab
0906.desc: The swimming crab is a crab that swims through the sea. Its rearmost legs are flat like paddles, letting it swim well underwater. It is a delicious crab that is very popular in Korea.
facts: Its rearmost legs are shaped like flat paddles, letting it swim.
When threatened, it buries itself in the sand to hide.
Swimming crabs caught in autumn are said to taste especially good.

0907.name: Snow Crab
0907.desc: The snow crab has very long, slender legs. Its Korean name comes from the word for bamboo, describing legs as straight and long as bamboo stalks. It lives in cold, deep water, which gives its meat a sweet, delicious taste.
facts: Its long, straight legs, like bamboo, give it its Korean name.
It lives in very cold, deep water.
Snow crabs from Yeongdeok in North Gyeongsang Province are especially famous.

0908.name: Red Snow Crab
0908.desc: The red snow crab is a type of snow crab with an all-red body. It lives in even deeper water than the common snow crab. Also known as hongge, it is affordable and has moist, tender meat.
facts: Its all-red body gives it its name.
It lives in deeper water than the common snow crab.
It is commonly known as hongge, or red crab.

0909.name: Hair Crab
0909.desc: The hair crab is covered all over in short, dense hair. Its shell and legs are covered in stiff bristles, so it feels rough to the touch. It lives in cold seas and is a very tasty crab.
facts: Its body is covered in stiff, bristly hair.
It prefers cold, northern seas.
Its hair makes it easy to tell apart from other crabs.

0910.name: King Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Asia · North America · Pacific Ocean · Arctic Ocean

0910.desc: The king crab is the biggest and strongest of all crabs. With its legs spread wide, it can be as tall as an adult person. It lives in extremely cold northern seas and its legs are packed with meat.
facts: It is the largest of all crabs, which is how it got its name.
With its legs spread out, it can be as wide as an adult is tall.
It lives in very cold seas near the Arctic.

0912.name: Chinese Mitten Crab
0912.desc: The mitten crab lives in rivers and rice paddies rather than the sea. Its pincers are covered in soft fur, making them look like they are wearing mittens. It is an interesting crab that travels down to the sea to lay its eggs.
facts: Its furry pincers look like they are wearing mittens.
It lives in rivers and rice paddies.
It travels down to the sea to lay its eggs.

0914.name: Ghost Crab
0914.nickname: The fast crab of the sand
0914.desc: The ghost crab dashes across sandy beaches at great speed. Its sand-coloured body makes it nearly invisible when it stays still. Its bulging eyes let it see in every direction.
facts: It runs extremely fast across sandy beaches.
Its sand-like colour helps it hide when still.
Its bulging eyes let it see all around.

0916.name: Mud Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

0916.desc: The mud crab is a large crab with a bluish shell. Its pincers are so powerful they can crack open shellfish. It lives in tidal mudflats and mangrove forests in warm seas.
facts: Its pincers are extremely powerful.
It lives in tidal mudflats in warm seas.
It is fond of mangrove forests, which is why it is also called the mangrove crab.

0917.name: Pea Crab
0917.desc: The pea crab is a small crab with a round shell like a chestnut. Its smooth, rounded body really does look like a chestnut. Unusually, it walks forward instead of sideways across the mudflat.
facts: Its shell is round like a chestnut.
It walks forward rather than sideways.
Its body is smooth and hard.

0919.name: Serrated Mud Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Oceania · Indian Ocean · Pacific Ocean

0919.nickname: The crab with a serrated edge
0919.desc: The serrated mud crab has sharp, saw-like teeth along the edge of its shell. Its pincers are large and extremely powerful. It lives in tidal mudflats in warm seas and grows to a large size.
facts: Its shell has saw-like teeth along the edge.
Its pincers are extremely powerful.
It lives in tidal mudflats in warm seas.

0922.name: Red King Crab

info.서식지: Ocean · Polar region · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Arctic Ocean

0922.desc: The red king crab is a large crab that lives in extremely cold seas. Its legs are covered in sharp spines. People consider it a very tasty crab.
facts: It lives in cold, northern seas.
With its legs spread wide, it is as broad as a person's outstretched arms.
It actually has only six walking legs.

0924.name: Sentinel Crab
0924.desc: The sentinel crab is a very small crab that lives on tidal mudflats. It feeds on nutrients in the mud and digs a small burrow to live in. Many of them gather together in clusters across the mudflat.
facts: It is a very small mudflat crab.
It feeds on nutrients found in the mud.
Many gather together in clusters on the mudflat.

0925.name: Fleshy Prawn
0925.desc: The fleshy prawn is a very large shrimp. It has long whiskers and a sharp horn on its forehead. It becomes especially tasty in autumn, when people love to eat it.
facts: It is one of the larger shrimp species found in Korea.
It has a long, sharp horn on its forehead.
It is caught in especially large numbers in autumn.

0926.name: Kuruma Prawn

info.서식지: Ocean · Asia · Pacific Ocean · Indian Ocean

0926.desc: The kuruma prawn is a shrimp with pretty stripes on its body. It hides in the sand during the day and comes out at night to find food. It tastes wonderfully rich when grilled.
facts: It has brown stripes across its body.
It hides in the sand during the day.
It is nocturnal, coming out at night to find food.

0929.name: Dokdo Shrimp
0929.desc: Dokdo shrimp is a name used together for several kinds of shrimp that live in the deep waters near Dokdo. It includes the coonstripe shrimp, the morotoge shrimp and the humpback shrimp. Prized for being rare and delicious, they are loved as a special treat.
facts: It is a name used together for three species of shrimp found near Dokdo.
They live in cold, deep water.
They are so rare that they are considered a special delicacy.
